name: 519 Church Street Community Centre
description:
There are good things about this organization, but unfortunately it is failing drastically in a number of important ways. The organization is very exclusionary. Nearly every person I've spoken to has been hostile. Supposedly there are trans support groups, but I don't know where and when they meet because the information is not listed on the website and nobody at the front desk seems to know. The Calendar of daily events almost never lists what events are taking place. It's been this way for at least the last six months. Volunteers there are generally rude and dismissive. Some have misgendered me deliberately. The one bright spot has been the nurse practitioner who has helped me get gender affirming care in Canada. Almost every single interaction I've had with a volunteer in the more than six months of trying to connect with this organization has been negative. I say this not because I want to damage its reputation, but because I want the organization to step up and serve the community it claims to serve. I really hope things can change. My belief is that a large part of this hostility comes from resenment toward the local homeless population. There's a sense among volunteers that they have to screen out the homeless people from the LGBTQ2S+ members they are trying to serve - but in practice, this results in gatekeeping. Every time I come in they seem like they're testing me to make sure that I'm a queer person and not some stinky mooching homeless person. It makes the atmosphere far from welcoming, and honestly makes me less sympathetic toward the organization as a whole.

My only experience with the 519 to date is the 2SLGBTQ+ yoga class run by Barb. Absolutely stellar! So welcoming and inclusive. She tailors to all skill levels and abilities. Bravo!!

No public wifi. They close facility an hour before 9; and staff are partially helpful. And limit / restrict where you can sit, not comfortable or accessible.

I'm in the 60+ age group and unfortunately I didn't find the meetings for this age group very helpful. So in my opinion more needs to be done to address the needs of this age demographic.

I just came back from a conference there.
A beautiful space and iconic in the lgbtq+ community

name: Access Alliance: 761 Jane Street, 2nd floor
description:
After being on the waitlist for this clinic for several years, I was excited to finally become a patient and expected professional, organized care. Unfortunately, my experience has been very disappointing. I’ve been waiting more than six months for specialist referrals, and despite several follow-up visits with my family provider, nothing has moved forward. Each time I see her, she reviews my old blood test results and tells me referrals have been submitted and to continue waiting. It’s been over six months, and my health hasn’t improved. Other times, she says my results aren’t ready, I don’t understand how Xray results can take months to arrive.

She prescribes medications that I take as directed, but they haven’t worked for me. When I try to book an appointment to discuss alternative medication, she isn’t available, and the next appointment is often two to three months away. The 2 family providers I have is very confusing, I feel my health is not understood well when I’m being seen with 2 different providers who claim they’re my family doctors, i don’t know who to see in order for her to understand my health well.

Reaching the clinic by phone is also a major challenge, calls are rarely answered, and voicemails often go unreturned. The front desk staff are frequently rude, dismissive, and unprofessional. Even when I called from the waiting room they wouldn’t answer the phone. I once had to walk in just to ask for a medication change, only to be told my provider wasn’t available for two months and advised to go to the emergency department for an issue that could easily be managed in a family practice setting.

It’s disappointing after waiting so long to join this clinic. I expected a higher level of care, communication, and professionalism. I hope the clinic leadership takes this feedback seriously and improves patient follow-up, accessibility, and staff communication.

We were today at Jane access alliance center and we had a good experience in there.  My daughter had vaccinations appointment and everything went great there with awesome nurses. Specially a kind and responsible person called Sylvia that was very helpful to us. Thanks a lot for everything.

Exceptional care. No health status in Canada, but Access Alliance give me health status, make me feel human again, listen to me. Thank you.

I want to share my negative experience at this place. From the very beginning, the receptionists’ attention was completely inadequate. They showed a clear lack of professionalism and empathy, evidenced by their disinterested attitude and inability to provide accurate information about the services I requested.

Instead of helping me, they sent me to other places where the services I needed were not offered, which was not only frustrating but also a complete waste of time. Moreover, I noticed that upon my arrival, due to my appearance, I was treated with disdain and even ignored on several occasions while they attended to other customers who arrived after me. This discriminatory attitude is unacceptable in any business.

This type of behavior leaves much to be desired from a place that should supposedly prioritize treating customers with respect and professionalism. I hope you take measures to properly train your staff in customer service and review the culture of equitable treatment in your establishment.

Very dissapointed with the way they are going down the hill. I have called since February, to reach Dr Lee and nurse for few matters, emailed and patiently waited till today May 8th 2025 to have no reply. Secretaries don't have calls the whole time, that is the biggest BS. I have been there for appointments and called from my phone to see the action, with my headphones in only one in my ear and they were talking and laughing. It just made me realised that how unprofessional behaviour is becoming more and more a thing. I need to retrieve information and they aren't reliable. Started to look already for other places because this one is just the most not reliable. Which they could bring Helen back, the portuguese lady who worked there 2020, 2021 the latest yrs I recall. What a lady, always on point very great person, nice polite and super helpful service provider there. Since her departure, jeeez Louise how bad it is.

name: St. Joseph Health Center
description:
🌟 My Heartfelt Thanks to St. Joseph’s Health Centre 🌟
On December 26, 2025, I suffered a serious fall that left me with a fractured hip and hand. I was in the hospital until January 23, 2026 — and in that time, the team at St. Joseph’s Health Centre changed my life.
From Emergency to the 4th‑floor Reactivation Unit, every nurse, doctor, therapist, and staff member treated me with dignity, kindness, and respect. Special thanks to Dr. Vincent, Dr. Gutman, Helen from Patient Relations, and the incredible PT/OT teams. You made my hardest days easier.
I even made new friends — including Andy, my neighbor — and shared laughs, stories, and even Star Trek moments with staff. “Live long and prosper” became our little motto.
My experience reminded me how lucky we are to live in Canada, where care is given to everyone, regardless of background, culture, or religion.
To all levels of government and to generous donors:
Please continue supporting our healthcare system.
One day, any of us may need the same care I received.
Thank you, St. Joseph’s. You are truly a place of healing, hope, and humanity.
— Mahbub Hussain

My family and I have needed to come to this hospital so many times throughout my life and each time was a bit different but the overall experience has been similar.

The wait times at this hospital are extremely long even when you have an appointment with a specific department unless it’s for imaging which usually is the fastest experience you can have at this hospital.

The area isn’t great so you get a lot of patients that are not mentally healthy and it creates a stressful experience for others visiting as some of those patients cause an unhealthy environment within the hospital.

The building itself looks like it’s going to need some repairs as there’s some areas where you see visible signs of deterioration in the infrastructure.

With that all being said, I’ve also had some positive experiences with this hospital.

When my mom had her heart attack her initial treatment was at a different hospital and then she was sent over to St. Joseph’s. Her team during the stay was fantastic and attentive and kept her in high spirits.

My mom also had most of my siblings at this hospital and each time the team was wonderful and my mom never wanted to go to another hospital to give birth. My sister in law and a few of my friends have also decided to have their babies here and all of them said it was such a positive experience.

I also have needed to come here for an abscess removal when I was a teenager and it was in a delicate area. They could have kept me awake but offered to put me to sleep for the procedure which I opted in for. The team took great care of me and gave my parents and I aftercare instructions which were easy to follow. I had to go back to have a small tube removed about a month later and it was a seamless experience.

Overall, the staff are mostly fantastic but they are overworked and short staffed. Some upgrades need to be performed on the building and some patients make the experience unpleasant for others but the care team have always done the best they could with the resources available.

I recently had a muscle biopsy surgery at St. Joseph’s Hospital, and the entire experience was excellent. The nurses, doctors, and staff were extremely professional, caring, and supportive from start to finish. They explained everything clearly, made sure I was comfortable, and treated me with real kindness. I truly appreciate the high level of care I received. Thank you to the whole team for making a stressful procedure as smooth as possible.

On a freezing -13°C morning, I walked into the clinic expecting a routine procedure. Instead, I experienced something exceptional.
When I sat in the large chair next to Nurse Jordan, I joked to Ashley that it felt like I had upgraded to business class and was ready to order champagne — and we all laughed. That moment of humor completely removed any tension.
Jordan was calm and professional, Ashley and April were warm and reassuring (and when I offered to buy everyone coffee, April kindly handed me apple juice instead — care first, celebration later 😊).
Dr. Springer was confident and precise, and Dr. McElroy, the anesthesiologist, was outstanding — clear explanations, thoughtful questions, and complete trust.
Outside it was -13. Inside, I felt warmth.
This team doesn’t just perform procedures — they quietly protect families and extend lives.
Highly recommended.
Vladimir Samokish

I would give 0 stars if I could. I had a horrendous experience. I arrived by ambulance at St Joseph’s emergency at 2 am on Saturday night (the morning of Sep 7).  Paramedics were outstanding.

I was put on a stretcher immediately in a hallway directly under a light and that is where I remained until Tuesday afternoon. Section B blue zone.

I had severe hip and back pain and I could not walk. I had a fever, constant headache exacerbated by the light, and my blood pressure got very low. I was parked directly under a bright light that was on 24/7 in a hallway with constant activity and noise.

The bathroom beside bed BS-05 had urine and blood on the floor, and feces on the toilet seat for almost 24 hours. I reported this multiple times to no avail. That bathroom was filthy.

Although there were a couple of gems (shoutout to the night shift and head nurse on Sunday who made sure I got food, vitals checked and meds when my nurse didn’t), the nursing care was the worst I’ve ever come across. I did not receive 4 meals in a row, all day Sunday and breakfast on Monday even though at each meal I was assured I was added to the list. That nurse who cared for me on Sunday put me at great risk, ignoring all of my needs including food. (She did bring me a dinner tray Sunday night after I didn’t get dinner when everyone else did, only after I made a big fuss, and then I had to ask for it multiple times. She kept saying she was dealing with more urgent situations which I did not see).

I had to beg for pain meds from most nurses and to get any care at all which was completely demeaning. I am not nor was I ever a drug addict.

Getting eye rolls and loud sighs when asking for help was the norm. Only when I was finally well enough on Tuesday  morning to tell the doctor that I either had to get a room or be moved to another hospital, and that I knew people in high places that could report this situation, did I receive any care or respect.

My IV fell out three times. I’m covered in bruises and they were so rough with the needles. No one offered me a walker to get to the bathroom until I had a room on Tuesday afternoon. It was horribly painful getting to the bathroom and back, so I drank as little as possible.

I hope that something is done about the management of this hospital as it goes beyond poor health care, which also clearly remains a huge problem. No one should be in a hallway for 72 hours. The management there is allowing people to work there who should not be allowed to care for people and clearly don’t care, and the filth was shocking.

Unfortunately I was far too sick to take pics of most of what I saw, but I have a few. Something must be done about the state of our health care.

name: Ossington Men’s Withdrawal Management Centre
description:
I was hopeless and depressed. I was already a man on the ledge. This place saved me. Saved me from insanity and, or death. So grateful for the chance they gave me to relook at my life. I came out of this place more focused and more equipped with the necessary tools to deal with my addiction and substance abuse. The staff was so cooperative and so was fellow clients who became more like a family to me. I can't thank Ossington Men's Withdrawal center enough...Happy to have had this lifetime opportunity

This detox changed hands from UHN to St. Michael's Hospital in May of 2022. This included a complete change of staff and programming. The reviews from before that change over were accurate for the time. I can't speak to the state of it now, but I hope people don't feel deceived by the good reviews; they reflect the care and amazing work of the staff from UHN for the many years leading up to the change over.

Hello, I'm Steven and I was suffering from a very intense addiction and mental instabilities during the addiction. I was making some direct inquiries to see how I could help this place or give back something for everything they have given me but unfortunately, the best I can do is write a review. Please heed my words as ACTUAL LIFE DEPENDS ON IT. It was brought to my attention that this facility may be shut down. Politics is beyond my reach of knowledge but whatever the reason may be, it is a devastating plan of action. By God's grace when I was researching places to go to be saved from my addiction I found Ossington Detox. I will always have shame for the involvement with drugs in my life and any ties to it, quite frankly it's almost impossible to get through it before you expire mortally or mentally. It's because of this place and the staff that work there I was able to have my life back. I can understand where bias may come in and all the other factors that may bring disagreement with my words. However, I have attended almost every detox the city has to offer, nothing compared to this place. The only downfall of this place is the aesthetics but that should only give the place more merit especially to the staff that endures this issue. Every single staff of this place EVERYONE, I had a connection with. The dynamic and chemistry of this place are nothing less than life-saving. My entire family is fully aware of this place for what it's done for me. They made such an impression on my wife and my mother whom some personally met. When they need to show love and support it's there. When they need to be stern to provoke your inner willpower it's there. When you need to talk to the doctor or nurse, they hear you and guide you the best way. These aren't general statements these are facts and I can elaborate on different situations to prove it. I'm a proud man and father today and it started with this detox. I know I mentioned this already but the staff that has dedicated years of their time in this place has paid off. The coincidence is almost godly. What I mean by this is that the way the staff interacts has created somewhat of chemistry and has such an effect on the clients positively. The thought of the higher-ups potentially closing this down just know... JUST KNOW you are making the biggest mistake in the detox world. If I had an opportunity to start a petition or anything of that sort to protest against this I would. I'm asking God first and the medical industry second to not close this place down. Do not make any changes with integrations or whatever you may have planned. You know the saying, don't you? If it's not broken, don't fix it! I want to thank the staff with all my heart for their dedication and hard work. I want to thank you again for putting your lives on the line during this pandemic and adapting to keep this place open during this bitter era. From the bottom and top and inside my heart, thank you for guiding me back to life. I will never forget and always remember you guys. I had such a pleasant time with you guys and so fulfilling with wisdom and knowledge I could go back sober just to be around your positivity! Any decisions that should be made on this place are the ones who work there for time and time. They are the ones who know and a client/patient like myself. It's beyond me how decisions can formulate from higher-ups and are blind to the potential negative impact it might have. I had a more rehabilitating experience here than some official rehab locations. Ossington Detox doesn't just manage withdrawal they save lives, to put it bluntly. I don't mean to be offensive in any way but the other 5 or 7 detoxes in the GTA do not and will never compare to the chemistry and dynamic Ossington Detox has. I stand beside you Ossington, thank-you for your hard work and dedication! You can see the difference between someone who comes into work to save lives and the ones who come in for a paycheque. God bless and Thank-you! All the love and respect my soul has! - Steven

This place is more of a homeless shelter than a detox center. They say it’s a non medical site which makes sense but there wasn’t a councilor or social worker on site.

I was admitted on a Thursday night after an emergency visit at Micheal Garron (abt 9pm). I was given an anti seizure medicine prescription, and was told to go to the shoppers across the street to fill my prescription. ( right beside the wine store)

There after I took my meds and spent almost the full day they discharged because I took my meds "without supervision".

The older Caucasian male with glasses ranted to me about how "what if I have a seizure we need to keep an eye on you" Meanwhile I was listening to them rant to each other about bitcoin at 3 am. No one told me i was supposed to give them my prescription.
By the way have fun with a boiled egg and slice of bread for breakfast....

This place should be shut down and turned into a shelter. NOT A DETOX CENTER

DISGUSTING Don't be fooled by fake reviews

WHAT CAN I SAY ABOUT 14 OSSINGTON. THE PEOPLE THERE ARE NOTHING SHORT OF ANGELS IN DISGUISE. MANY A NIGHT I WENT THERE. MANY A NIGHT THEY ACCEPTED ME WITHOUT DELAY. HOPING ONE DAY I MIGHT GET IT. WELL ITS 3 MONTHS SHORT OF 3 YEARS AND THOUGH LIFE IS NOT PERFECT, THANKS TO OSSINGTON I HAVE A CHANCE ONE DAY AT A TIME  GOD BLESS YOU ALL.  MOST SINCERLEY JAMES P. SUNSTRUM

name: Woman’s Own Detox
description:
Was a bit of a wild ride. At least 6-8 women all together in the observation room for the first three nights. Very difficult to rest or sleep with the staff sitting in the room clicking their binders and office supplies and chatting at the desk which is in the room with the beds and the people sleeping. No one could smoke for first three days. I think the idea is for it to be like a gauntlet where only the strongest will make it through the humiliation and discomfort of that situation. If you do in fact make it to the program side, the food is great.

Take good care of you there.  They go by the rules with alot of one on one help.

name: New Path Youth and Family Services — Orillia (Orillia Common Roof Location)
description:
What an awesome organization, started in spring suffering deep depression and alcohol addiction. 6 months later depression under control and haven't had a drink in more than 3 months. Thanks to all the wonderful staff that have helped me

Amazing caring people

Smells like pee.

name: Northern Ontario Counselling Clinic
description:
Valerie is a very knowledgable social worker and gives great advice when it comes to co-parenting.

I life changing experience for me. I felt like I was being heard and it helped me through a dark time. I definitely recommend this Clinic

name: Haliburton Highlands Mental Health Services
description:
Always amazing, always on time and by using this service along with other help i was able to kick a 20yr alcoholic death sentence. I cant say enough good things about everyone from Brenda the front desk attendant to Melissa the best nurse on the planet to my new psychiatrist…… this is a vital service and i recommend taking advantage of all they have to offer

They have saved my life countless times

I have used this office for over a decade ,  have had nothing but continued care and support .  The staff are friendly and courteous,  do their best to help you and treat you like a human not a number.

This place is absolutely useless. Better off seeking mental health help absolutely anywhere else.

name: The Farm Rehab
description:
The Farm saved my life. Full stop.

They go above and beyond what any other facility offers when it comes to helping people get clean, sober, and stay that way. The lifetime alumni support is unheard of — it truly makes all the difference. The community and family built through this incredible place have often been the life raft I’ve needed.

The staff, both on the floor and behind the scenes, are nothing short of amazing. Their compassion, dedication, and authenticity shine through every step of the way.

Glens turkey bacon is pretty great too.

I am very grateful to the Farm as it kick started a whole new outlook on life for me. I went in broken, and left inspired. The genuine caring of the staff was profound. I will never forget my talks with Sean, Bonnie, and most of all Eric. The Farm will not cure you but will give you the tools to start rebuilding your life.  After you leave they offer Alumni meetings three times a week which I am participating in and get so much out of. Thank you to all at the Farm.

The Farm was the second treatment centre I attended, which was in 2019. I struggled with addiction for over two decades. This place really worked for me. Today I am still clean and sober, I have rebuilt my life and my relationships, and believe it or not I have become an addictions counsellor today. No way I would have gotten clean without the farm, especially their aftercare program for alumni. Its the only facility ive ever heard of that offers lifetime aftercare for alumni and their families as well. Thanks to the farm, I have a life I could only have dreamt of. If you are struggling with addiction, this is the place. You won’t regret it.

Going to the farm was the best choice that I have EVER made.
The staff is unbelievably amazing.
They loved me when I couldn't love myself and gave the tools I needed to beat this disease.
I can't put in words what this place has done for me and it brings me to tears of joy to think about it.
The program is amazing.
The food was great.
The counselors are unbelievably caring and helpful and the biggest thing that has changed my life is that they never gave up on me and the after care never stopped.
As  long as I show up, they are there.
2 years after treatment and I do 3 zoom meetings a week and have met people I call farmily.
I couldn't have dreamt where I am at in recovery 2 years ago and it is because of these amazing people at the farm in Stouffville.
Thank you so much for your love and support

I went to the farm back in April of 2024 and can genuinely say it has changed my life completely. I was well fed, treated like a human and checked in on constantly. The staff are amazing people who really are passionate about helping us get better. I’m just over a year and a half sober and I give credit to the farm for directing me and supplying me with the tools to get where I am today. I really can’t say thanks to them enough.

name: Addictions and Mental Health Services Hastings Prince Edward
description:
If I could give a zero star I would. So I started CMHA years ago and honestly it was really awesome. The programs were awesome and so were the case workers. However, seems like the case workers have zero accountability for communication and never reschedule till you message them. They are always in a rush at closing your file instead of actually doing their job. (Helping the person). The manager for this office is a nice guy but I honestly feel like for a place that's suppose to support they really don't make you feel supported.

Also highly recommend the ADHD program that was a 5 star group and Natosha the coordinator for that is by far the only one that was actually helpful and she was in the belleville office.

I really think mental health is important and the people they hire need to be watched closer.

Honestly? This organization could do so much better. Case workers can't recognize when their clients are in crisis or volatile and acting out or cancelling services due to overwhelming emotions and clearly aren't in control of themselves. The de-escalation skills are lacking or nonexistent. I've been yelled at by a case worker before then had wait at the bottom of the barrel for another one, complained about her and never heard back, because why would I? I'm not important They care more about getting people off of the wait list than actually helping them. Don't cancel due to having a bad mental health day and not having the energy to go do it, you'll get a lecture when that's the last thing you need. Literally so pathetic that this is one of the few agencies in the area that can service adults with mental health issues, clearly no understanding of the population served.

This agency pretends to support people with mental illness, while prioritizing upper management and paperwork, there is far too many “managers” and not nearly enough front line support workers. I feel that this agency does not protect its employees or prioritize the clients they claim to serve.

As someone who has struggled for years with mental health and depression, I finally got the strength and mind set to reach out and connect with a therapist. After waiting 5 months on a waitlist, they called to get me booked in. A week later they called to cancel and reschedule that apt. No problem things happen. Rebooked that apt, for them to call me back and cancel it again. Another week goes by...After the third time now of calling me to cancel and reschedule with another therapist, Im getting the strong sense that no one there really cares to help. Thank you for confirming everything my depression has taught me all these years - that nothing is reliable and if its to good to be true, it probably is.

Disappointing. Had a great counsellor then no one for 3 months. Then 2 counsellors, no support in a meeting, they just sat there while I was being called a liar, I left, they stayed & couldn't look me in the eye after. If they don't believe & speak negatively about a client, what is the point. Asked my file to be closed. No trust, no faith.

name: Sexual Assault Kingston
description:
Incredibly rude receptionist. I attempted to meet my mother at this building and I understand that there must be confidentiality, etc. My issue was the way I was spoken to on the phone. I had called this office to express my concern with meeting my mother given her appointment time. As I was aware of the start time, but unsure of the end time.  When I called after attempting to see my mother at this location, Jamie, who I was later told by one of her associates, is not a receptionist, to which I replied, "OK then...Jamie, who picks up your phones and is the first point of contact.", continued to speak over me when I was trying to explain my concern. I wasn't being rude or anything!! After she had continued to interrupt and try to speak over me, I simply asked, "Jamie, PLEASE don't continue to speak over me like that. To which she replied, "I am not going to let you speak to me like that." and proceeded to hang up on me. Zero professionalism whatsoever. When a customer calls, you don't interrupt them and speak over them. And then when they politely ask you to refrain from interrupting, that's the response you get!? Nice!! I wonder if that's just the way men are treated there or if it's just people of colour. Not sure. Either way...perhaps hiring people who are more professional, educated, and have experience in customer service, along with some people skills, may be a great solution.

**UPDATE* Given this company's response, they clearly didn't bother to listen to the call. Especially to suggest aggressive behavior. Didn't realize that using pleasantries like please, etc. were forms of aggression. Calmly speaking to a business where they rudely interrupt you and when you politely ask them to not be rude and unprofessional, that's the type of response you get. Since when is asking someone to not interrupt a form of aggression??

Horrible place, horrible program. No one here cares for the well being of their clients. Myself and friends have all had horrible experiences with SACK, leaving us with no one to talk to, telling people they’re not in crisis when they clearly are, transferring clients from psychologist to psychologist and having to rehash their trauma again and again, cutting people off from services without warning or good reason, THEY SIMPLY DO NOT CARE. Go get help somewhere else, trust me. This program is a joke and the fact that they think they’re helping anyone is truly delusional.

A wonderful, kind organization. The work that they do is a real gift to the Kingston community. They deserve all our support and more.

I had a wonderful experience at the sexual assault centre Kingston, the staff were polite and professional and made me feel welcome and heard. I would highly recommend this place to any survivor.

I went here for counseling and the woman who I saw wasn't nice at all. She told me I just wanted attention and didn't listen to me with respect. I wouldn't recommend coming here for help. If you're looking for help, go somewhere else. :( :(

name: Mental Health and Addictions Crisis Centre - Walk-in
description:
If you're in a crisis and desperately need don't come here. Ive had many many very odd and weird experiences here. The employees and volunteers seem to have their own issues. It's almost as if they need their own crisis worker. They are uneducated, inexperienced and most importantly lack compassion. I get it they are probably over worked and underpaid and some don't even get paid. But If you are in crisis situation and you come here or you call them and the person who attempts to help you actually makes you worse, then what's the point. The in person sessions and the ones on the phone are very short, too short in my opinion. You say that they really helped you but there is good chance you haven traveled outside of London or southwestern Ontario. As you share your anguish and major issues in your life many of the workers will just sit there and laugh at you. This has happened to me so many times. The psychiatrist that works here is even worse considering his level of education and years of experience. There just isn't a passion to help people here. Everyone just drag their feet. Can't wait to go home. Can't wait to get paid. Everyone is just treated like a number. The resources they give us can easily be be found online with no need for a crisis worker. They're not professional or organized.

Shame on this place — and honestly, on London, Ontario as a whole. It feels like everywhere that’s supposed to help with mental health just hires anyone, even people who clearly don’t care. If you only care about the paycheck and not the people, you shouldn’t be working with those who are struggling. All you’re doing is pushing people away from getting the help they need,  what a shame.

I am so incredibly grateful for the nursing/support staff here. I use services regularly without issue and I think this is a wonderful place to seek help for many issues, however the one star review is due to their psychiatry service.  It is very disheartening to feel that proper treatment, credence, or respect is  given only to the experiences of those falling under the cookie cutter straight, cis, white male category of patients.  It is unfortunate to see such an archaic approach to psychiatry, especially from a place that strives to eradicate such barriers in our community.  My main suggestion? Listen to your patients. Even if you end up disagreeing, people deserve to have a voice in their care and to have their experiences heard and listened to. Not hearing what your patients have to say is a disservice to everyone involved.

I was treated with respect. They helped me navigate the problems i was dealing with and gave me great advice on how to deal with it in the future.

"A" for effort. If I was on my way to jump off a ledge and went for an intervention through CMHA, I would have gone straight back to the ledge after my appointment.

Some points to consider:

1) Give the patient an opportunity to respond without interruptions.

My counselor talked over me repeatedly. I started to think he didn't actually want me to answer his questions.

2) Don't suggest that the patient doesn't seem like they belong at a crisis centre.

3) Take time to investigate what the patient actually wanted to talk to you about. Chances are they are there because they want to work through it even if it has to be with a random stranger.

4) Consider that this was not their first choice. Honour their courage beyond a simple verbal acknowledgement. Let your practice prove your words.

5) The service is advertised as unlimited with regard to timeframe, but yet I felt pushed out of the room. The counselor seemed uncomfortable and inexperienced. I could tell because he kept leaning on relating my unique experience to his own life. Professional malpractice 101.

Just like many professions, the mental health sector appears to be striving to ensure they have enough bodies available without the proper training.

The crisis centre is in crisis per this experience if you ask me. Fortunately, I am not in as life threatening of a position as I imagine others are.

I am grateful for this service, but I'd be hesistant to try them again in the future to be completely honest.

name: Women's Health Care Centre - Sexual Assault Domestic Violence Program
address: Regional Health Centre, Emergency Department - 1 Hospital Drive
description:
KSAC is a great counselling resource and offers workshops/hosts events that are relevant to their work. It is always a friendly environment. They offer support in different ways that are inclusive and accessible to people who are different stages of healing and who may benefit from different entry points.
They prioritize consent and confidentiality at every step of service. They are supportive and their staff are very informative about the work they do and how they can help.

A wonderful and safe space to go to. Staff are so kind and patient. Jordanna was very knowledgeable and supportive, Cheryl was so incredibly sweet and kind, and Katie was truly the BEST counsellor that i have ever had. I cant thank Katie enough for how much she supported me throughout the past year. KSAC, thank you for making me feel so safe and supported when i had no one else to turn to. ❤️

This is an excellent resource for the city of Peterborough and surrounding areas that supports victims of sexual abuse and assault.  They offer counselling and therapies, as well as drop in services for clients of KSAC such as Purls of Wisdom, Wings & SEED.  If you are in need of help, do NOT hesitate to contact KSAC.  Their staff is friendly, knowledgeable and trained to handle issues surrounding victims with PTSD, depression and anxiety.  They are a ray of light in my life as I'm sure they will be for those who might be wary of getting help.

They said they would reach out to me about joining their programs and I received no phone call or any contact at all from them so I decided to call them myself.

The entire time I was on the phone with them I felt very uncomfortable as the lady on the phone sounded like she was holding back from laughing at me the entire phone call.

I do not think I will be seeing these people any further and will get help elsewhere.

This group provides a necessary service for anyone who has affected by sexual assault.  Please consider donating to KSAC or volunteering for them!

name: Crisis Intervention Services (Downtown)
description:
I called in march to get myself in to counselling for childhood SA. They said someone would call in June, July came and nothing, I called them back and they tell me I’m not even in the system. What a joke you are!

Placed on a Community treatment order by Dr. Kumar, Dr. Joseph and  worker Robert. I need a lawyer to stop it. I tried fighting this order but the lawyer was inexperienced,  unprepared and inept. The appeal was literally a clown show. Now I'm forced to go places and see people against my will, ordered only by a doctor with no recourse. Everytime I go to pick up meds, Robert the employee gives me a hassle literally holding my pills back after answering his questions. I had to call the Police just to get pills!!! What is this shadowy operation allowing doctors and staff to act like cops without a badge and force you to do things against your will. Don't I have rights as a Canadian citizen?? Why can they have police arrest you and force you to comply??On only their words?? I feel like a prisoner in my own province and have suffered as a result of their behaviour. Now I don't trust doctors,  police, the province's law makers. Thanks Trudeau.

Changing my review, I have had some not so good experiences but I find myself back here for the mood and anxiety program again. When I'm in group, it feels easier and more manageable to work on the coping skills I learnt, rather than trying to manage it on my own. On my own, I slowly regress.
Thankful to be working with Charles again, I can't keep restarting with new therapists and this took a lot of stress off my shoulders.

I was scheduled for an in person assessment appointment on Tuesday.  I left in plenty of time because I was coming from Mindemoya and wanted to allow for any surprises.   I got 2 phone calls from my “case worker” but couldn’t hear him thru the car’s system.  Told him I would find a place to pull over.  Had to park on the shoulder of the highway.  Was almost to Sudbury by this time.   Even though I was almost there, he decided he was saving me time by canceling the appointment because I had access to help here.  I explained that I wanted to see who I could establish a rapport with.  When I said I would have appreciated the call on the previous Friday he said the office had been closed.   I told him I was very upset but he was very off handed about it.  Sorry and all that.  No wonder people want to do sad things if this is what we have in the north.  I am very happily going to seek help here and I’m grateful we have services.

The reception staff were always so lovely to deal with, and services were easy to access. Grateful for the stellar service I received at 127 Cedar Street. So much love for the 6th and 8th floor! Thank you for everything you do!

name: Health Sciences North Manitoulin site
address: 11 Meredith Street, Little Current, Ontario P0P 1K0
description:
In mid November I received excellent care at the Manitoulin Health Centre. I was rushed to hospital in severe pain and  Dr. Nelson and the staff at the MHC quickly diagnosed and resolved my issue. Vary grateful for the excellent care I received. Thank You MHC

Great small town hospital with good services and friendly staff. This hospital has a walk-in clinic, doctors offices, emergency department, physiotherapy, imaging services, obstetrics, optometry, minor operations (scopes mostly), dialysis, acute care, and prenatal care. Wait times are generally short, doctors are well trained with a good bedside manor, and nurses are caring. I don't spend much time here luckily, but it is a solid hospital when I do. There is also emergency helicopter transportation for really serious incidents.

Dissapointed that the technician was gossiping, then said they were behind and Iwould have to wait, but was coughing and hacking. As a senior with breathing issues , I had to leave.

Needed to get stitches from an accident.  Very professional staff.  No waiting in emergency.  For a small town hospital I felt they did a fantastic job!

I was in the hospital for 1 week, many thanks to Dr. Jeffry and the nurses and staff, for taking such passionate and professional care of me. I'm well on my way to recovery. I feel great! Thank you all.!
Ken Horsfall.

name: Thunder bay regional health sciences centre
address: 980 Oliver Road, Thunder Bay, Ontario P7B 6V4
description:
We waited in Terrace Bay for 3 nights and 4 days to get a room, and then stayed 2 nights in Thunder Bay. Nurse MEERA in 3C, shout out to you girl! Did an excellent job. Her kindness, compassion, and outstanding service truly meant so much to us. She was attentive, caring, and very supportive throughout our stay.

When the doctor explained that my husband did not need surgery, he explained everything clearly and in a very reassuring way, which gave us peace of mind.

Because of the bad weather, we were not able to go home right away. Nurse Meera informed us that we could stay for another night and be discharged the next day. Her coworkers helped arrange transportation, although we didn’t get the taxi they arranged for us but still appreciate the help.

We are very grateful for the care we received.

-Angelica/Harold

Arrived at 8:30am, was triaged in a timely manner.  Front desk staff were all very pleasant.  Waited about a half hour to be taken to a room where a nurse checked on us, asked all the appropriate questions pertaining to the issue and said the doctor would be with us shortly.  (I didn’t catch his name but he was very polite and kind)
The doctor (Dr. Agostino) was with us in a timely manner,  again asked all the appropriate questions pertaining to the situation, plus a few more.   He explained the process going forward and all of the things he said would happen,  happened in a timely manner.
We had to wait quite a long time (2 hours) for some test results to come back and I am unsure of how long these tests take to complete so I’m not counting this as a negative.  In the meantime the nurse did check on us several times and offered us some water.
After results were completed, the Doctor quickly told us and gave some follow-up to what would happen after we left.   The care rendered was excellent and I have zero complaints.
There is the issue however of a weird smell in the area we were in.  (Similar to that of opening a can of wet dog food,  yes very weird indeed).
It was also quite a lot dirtier than I expected it to be.  (Discarded gloves under a desk, syringe left on the sink in the patient washroom (no needle attached.. and other things that just needed some attention.).
I fully expected to be in emerge for 10 or more hours but was only 4 so that was a win :) overall with the state of health care in Ontario I thought TBRHSC did great  for us today.   Thank you very much folks.

The most disgusting and atrocious hospitals around.
Going to the emerg is an absolute gong show.
You can't even be sick to go to this place and be taken care of.
Once you have tests, they put you on display in the hall for every person to look at you and you to be subjected to more germs until a doctor has a moment to come tell you your results, next steps or whatever so everyone can hear it.

Being admitted,  having insurance and wanting just a bed is horrendous.
They throw you in a room that isn't meant to actually be a room such as a closet or " sunroom " that doesn't have a bathroom which again, you need to be subjected to many others germs, blood and other biohazard materials...... Which doesn't have a shower and you're left to sit and plead your case to all the nurses just so you can clean yourself.

Going for a smoke, they expect you to walk all the way around the hospital to the " main " entrance after 11pm rather then using security doors, however they want you to again go back the opposite way to the emergency area ( these idiots told you both to go through ) to be let back in.
It's pitch black outside,  construction in the front and ton and TONS of intoxicated people,  individuals with substance abuse issues, etc that linger all around the hospital...... So, by the time you get back inside and you made it safe, you should probably buy yourself a lottery ticket.
This hospital has know idea how to operate.
They don't have any beds, let alone the options of using your work benefits to pay for a private room.
Half the nurses are rude and awful.
They treat you like you're an inconvenience and I busted the one nurse and her " manager " talking about me.
They try to use dirty cuffs/sleeves to check your blood pressure ( even though it touches areas where blood is taken from ) and often open wounds from having blood work done, and then just passing it around to others that have no idea or think they are " clean ".

You really have to advocate for yourself because if you don't..... Nobody will and some of these people just don't care.
I found needles outside on the sidewalk, tons of blood drops, spit, among other things.
You really don't want to come here unless its your very last option.

When I looked under my " bed " that was actually a stretcher being used as a bed, it was filled with another person's belongings and a prostatic leg of some sort.
I told the nurse 4 times to have someone clean it as I have no idea what type of contamination may be on it.....

Where is all the money going for this hospital?
They receive government founding, as well as a great deal of money through the lottery they have..... Millions in fact..... This hospital is awful and trust me when I say if I could have been able to go elsewhere I most certainly would have.

The worst hospital i have ever experienced. I gave birth in the emergency room I faced racism and prejudice from nurses and doctors. I was denied pain meds because I’m a recovering addict and staff repeatedly told me I wouldn’t be given anything because I’m a drug addict. I gave birth in the ER in a room locked from the outside  because I was screaming in pain I delivered my own baby and was screaming and begging for help while giving birth nobody helped me.
The nicu nurses behave like high school girls gossiping and when I filed complaints the nurses in the Nicu retaliated by not notifying me of changes in my daughter’s health. They found a heart murmur and I wasn’t notified until a week after it was found

It’s been a great ride so thankful for everything you’ve done for me honestly especially for my freak accident back in May means so so much. To the surgeons, anesthesiologist and the nurses who helped me feel comfortably as possible. Honestly I don’t know where I’d be without the constant checking in on me making sure I’ve been okay helping with just everything that I’ve been through. Every ounce of support goes really a long way for me. ❤️❤️

name: Timmins District Hospital Mental Health Services – Crisis Intervention
description:
This is by far one of the worst emergency departments in Canada. Waiting four to five hours just to see a doctor is unacceptable. It raises serious concerns about how healthcare resources and tax dollars are being used. Such delays can put lives at risk. Extremely frustrating.

My first child was born here and I had a very good birthing experience. The nurses were super helpful and took great care of mine and th baby. The nurse in the operation room was a wonderful lady who helped me calm down before the C section.

This is an old hospital, but it's fully equipped with everything a patient needs.

Hi! I was flown in by plane then by ambulance  from detour lake to er department in Timmins with extremely bad pain on my lower side to the point i couldnt move so they sent me for ct scan 3 people were there instead of helping me from stretcher to stretcher they stood over me and watched me struggle in pain no help given.Then  i was returned back to er.The dr said all my tests were okay to follow up with family dr  and says we are not here to test you on everything we are an er department. All my luggage was in detour lake had no clothes except a gown so i waited for  my husband coming from north bay the nurse closed the curtain left me in corner,no food or  nothing to drink ,no regular checks but the 3 other people in same room got regular checks and got food.For me they pretended i was not  even there. i do have a heart condition and no call bell i did yell for help and no one came i needed to go to the bathroom,  instead i peed in a cup beside  me at my reach  cause they left me in the dark pretended i was not even there finally 6 hours later before husband walked in they asked if i needed anything i told nurse i could of had a heart attack no one checked on me she lifted her shoulders and says oh well it happens and they wouldnt even notice like at all this is not appropriate specially as they are getting funding. People are dying because of neglect like this everyday. I support ontario hospitals every month. Timmins will never get my donations not after this bad experience. This should not be allowed to happen.NO STARS GIVEN

The emergency room definitely needs improvement. I came from the states and the emergency are taking people that are not emergency patients. They need acute cares that are for after 4 and only til 10 if you can't make it to see your doctor. I had the rubber piece of my hearing aid stuck in my ear. It's not an emergency but it's the only place to get help. Dr was good the wait time not so good.

Much better experience here than at North bay regional hospital. Intake and triage were instantaneous. I got to see the doctor after about a two hour wait. I knew I was going to wait cause of how serious my injury was compared to others but only waiting two hours to see the doctor was fast compared to past experiences at other hospitals

name: Toronto Distress Centre
address: Box 243, Adelaide P.O., Toronto, Ontario M5C 2J4
description:
I've never actually spoken to someone whenever I've called because the hold music is quite unpleasant, especially in a heightened sensory state (aka, distress).  I hang up after a few minutes. It's frustrating, because the Mental.Healrh system and resources are not particularly helpful or hope inducing.

I sincerely wish that helplines would start providing an automated meditation or some kind of de-escalation walk through in leiu of poor musical tastes. At least give folks the option! Because even when you do talk to counsellors at other lines, they simply don't provide callers with these (or any) skills. I don't know if the folks here do provide those skills, because I haven't gotten through yet because I can't listen to the hold music.

I hope help lines start to utilize technology (like AI) to better help provide timely and/or preventative care measures. Something that can provide helpful techniques while callers are waiting, in distress. Please stop this repetitive music....flute music, at that! This is definitely a barrier for me personally in reaching out for help when I need it.

I called here to get information for someone and I had to repeat 10 times the person is in Toronto. It really seemed like the girl who answered had her mind elsewhere. She kept asking "where is this person? Oh Toronto? I thought you said Alberta". And 2 minutes later "where is this person again?". This happened repeatedly. Very odd way to help someone in distress.

I called to find out about suicide loss survivor programs. I have been looking for resources since my husband died in May. Someone called me back within an hour and provided me with all the info I needed to enroll in a grief program.
I have reached out to other groups (names provided by my doctor) and either they didn’t focus on suicide loss or they didn’t call me back at all.
There aren’t a lot of options if you are trying to get through the trauma of suicide loss in Toronto. I am glad this service exists, and so thankful that they called me back!

In the past, I had some decent experiences (but also some terrible experiences). I'm really in a low place so I tried to call today. The countdown seemed relatively quick but then once I got to 1, I had to wait so so so long. Finally a man answered. I don't think he received any sort of training. He would cut me off in the middle of sentences, make assumptions, and ask questions that I already answered (like do you have a husband or kids? I already went into detail about how I felt so alone and like I was failing for not having those). I asked if I could just wait for a woman counsellor at that point. He said okay and instead of transferring me back into the queue with my place in tact, he hung up on me. Maybe they don't have adequate funding and that's why they're so bad. Don't call here unless you want to feel worse than when you started.

I have had a few good experiences with this helpline a few years ago so I’m giving an extra star for that..However I called about a half hour ago for support and maybe a positive perspective and a male answered who seemed abrupt and did not seem interested in what I had to say or in hearing my issues..Then the line went dead so I think I got hung up on 😢 I wasn’t rude or anything so no excuse for that..I tried to email through the website to explain what happened but was unable to because of captcha or something so I’m explaining my situation here and hopefully this doesn’t happen often to people on this helpline because they are struggling..
Thankyou for listening..

name: Community crisis centre of Hôtel-Dieu Grace Healthcare
description:
My Brother recently had a lengthy stay in the Emara Centre, From the bottom of our hearts we Thank the exceptional staff Dr Cox, RN Tamara, NP David, Social Worker Adele to name a few. The staff are incredibly caring , attentive and professional. Thank you to the nurse who thanked me for sharing my brother with them. I will never forget those kind words. Windsor/Essex County has the best!

Paid parking. Several parking lots. Lots of available parking after 5pm (majority of staff has gone home). Helpful information service desk and helpful staff.

Kudos to the young lady in the er she's been  here since I came  in at noon. I watched  this young lady deal  with  everything  thrown at her
And she continued  smiling she definitely  chose the right career.
Such a sweetheart
Lake Tahoe  California.

Best care best nurses
care was amazing everyone was good
The only thing is the kitchen was bad tbh
I was on the second floor most of the time jay was the in charge nurse and this man is the best
Therapy is the best . Thank u every one for the grate care 💪🏽

I went there for my first time with my parents
the building is nice inside the building is beautiful and organized
The staff are nice and full of manners

name: Family services windsor
description:
They’re all weird and suspicious. The money department opened all my cheques when I wasn’t on service and the trustees are terrible. Tatyana and heather are horrible workers and do not do their jobs. Tatyana ruined my life so significantly that I have been homeless since march because she refused to help me with things I asked her to and now I’m screwed because of her and it’s November. Heather is mean, cruel, and disrespectful. She does not acknowledge when she’s wrong and she also was very disrespectful to me as an autistic a mentally ill person. She refused to help me and hung up on me when she triggered me by lying and hurting my feelings. She is terrible and doesn’t care about your mental health and she doesn’t care about you succeeding. This organization is horrible for trusteeship and I wouldn’t trust anyone in the whole building other than Carol. Carol is incredible and has never done anything even slightly wrong or suspicious. Everyone else is a whole issue and been consistently wrong and suspicious.

Very attentive and caring. Thank you so much for helping me navigate my complicated life. Highly recommended

Horrible service, likes to insist upon things in other people's relationships that destroys them and builds walls. Already had enough issues with my relationship and then they got involved and starting trying to put walls up.

Should be extremely ashamed of yourselves honestly.

I want to give special recognition to Riva, my assigned worker for several months. Her attentive attention and respectful demeanor bears mention to her employer. She is gifted in this capacity and I am truly grateful for all that she has done to help me in my journey of healing.

Such mice staff and the social workers are fantastic I would 100% recommend

name: CMHA Windsor-Essex county
description:
Do not call this office receptionist  is rude abrupt  talks over you does not listen . Also do not call manager  Steve in housing  program never returns calls.  When receptionist  does transfer no one is in I spent half day trying to  call and complain  about  this awful serivice no one picks up all go straight to voice mail. This chma has to be the worst for service especially  that receptionist.  I just want to ask basic question turned into  the royal around. I truly  feel sorry  for the people of winsdor if they have to deal with this organization. Horrid horrid service go else even for general information. Nothing can shame this office.  Hopefully some one from this offic views my review. Also go else for services stay clear of chma windsor. This one frustrated  angry citizen.

Myself and my son have been patients for 3 years now, and despite the CMHA name this center is not about mental health or health care in the best interest of the patient. They do not have consistent health care providers, no full time doctors, mainly RPN acting as doctors, they are not supervised to ensure that proper care is being given. In fact they treat patients very poorly, when you speak up they automatically dismiss you, saying they have never had any problems in 30 years being a "doctor"! Additudes like this are throughout the whole clinic, secretaries are angry when the phones ring. One saying out loud "oh give it a rest". Managers who dismiss you as a patient without your knowledge, when they are supposed to provide patients with alternative care. Most do not even listen, examine, and will send you for tests over and over, to specialist all over the city just to put your illness on someone else. Now without even meeting or seeing a new health care provider they send you for more tests just to swipe your health card, only finding out after Clear medical contacts you to book your appointment. Since covid health care has been completely monetized because they didn't have to swipe patients healthcards, RPN's abuse their power in a doctor's role, every contact has to be a booked appointment in order to speak to someone, double appointments in order to administer a shot when you are already there so your health card can be swiped again.
With this it is no wonder mental health, and undiagnosed illness is on the rise.

Adrian Deschamps is a very caring staff member.

I personally like to advocate and support those who are vulnerable and marginalized within the mental health field and those requiring empowerment/assistance. I love the work CMHA does within the community. However, I would like to see more diversity, equity, and inclusion being exercised towards visible minorities and not having biases towards certain groups over others. We are all humans at the end of the day with unique stories and situations. This is how I feel at least from this CMHA location. It is essential we look at both sides of the stories and not just one side or perspective.

If I may say that I'm very greatful to have provided the outstanding services and assistance from two of the many professionals, caring , motivating and devoted councillors Christie and in particular Miss Judi Lai .
I often think about the conversationsand how much they helped me, prepared me towards my current situation and the transition of everything I experienced i within the last few years  .
I am honored to have had the pleasure of metting you ,for always being there for my needs by listening and understanding without any prejudice nor judment
I am healthier and happier and I sincerely owe it all to you.
Gracias 🙏

name: Canadian Centre for Addictions
description:
Almost 4 months sober. Denise, Hope, Tim, and Lee are amazing. Mandy and Brenda are also complete angels who go above and beyond with your medical care. Steak night was my favourite. I have gone from weighing 145lbs and looking like hell to now 180lbs and still gaining a few lbs a month. Like anything in life it wasn’t perfect here. The few disappointments did not outweigh the efforts and the treatment given by the staff. Shout out to all the amazing staff especially Rebecca and Brenda. My first week was a shock and they made me feel comfortable and were always willing to help. I made some great friends here and they did give me the tools i needed to live sober. My favourite part was the morning walks and getting to see my favourite golden retriever Cheddar. She is a special animal and gave me something to look forward to.

I completed a stay here in August and overall had a really positive experience. The facility itself is beautiful, clean, and very calming, and the food was genuinely great—way better than I expected. The counsellors and all of the staff were incredibly kind, supportive, and easy to talk to. I left with a lot of helpful tools that I still use in my recovery.

The only parts that didn’t work as well for me were the art therapy and the aftercare. I didn’t connect with the art therapist personally, and I also feel the aftercare sessions should be longer than 15 minutes to be truly effective.

That said, my time here was still meaningful and I’m grateful for the support I received. I would recommend this rehab to anyone looking for a supportive and structured place to begin their recovery.

When I discovered that my sister was in trouble with drugs I immediately searched for a rehab centre for her to go to to help her get clean. After doing some research CCFA sounded like the best option for her. I’m so glad I made that choice because they truly saved her life. Her time there was overwhelmingly a positive experience and she learned so much on her path to recovery. It was also so heartwarming to see how much everyone there cared about the clients they have there. They were genuinely so proud of my sister and tears were shed as we picked her up to go home. I would highly recommend this place to anyone looking for help for themselves or a loved one with addiction. Absolutely amazing.

I came to this treatment centre at one of the lowest points of my life. I had recently attempted suicide, my bulimia was completely out of control, and I had just come off a binge of drinking to numb the pain — something that was new and honestly scary for me. I entered treatment sober-curious, not knowing exactly what my relationship with alcohol would look like, but knowing something had to change.
I owe most of my gratitude to my primary counsellor, Tim. His consistency, honesty, and ability to meet me where I was made a massive difference in my recovery. The other counsellors were also refreshingly real and direct, which helped keep me accountable while still feeling supported. That balance mattered more than I can put into words.
I left treatment eating-disorder free, no longer suicidal, on a new PRN that has significantly helped my severe panic attacks, and living what is essentially a sober lifestyle — not because I was forced into it, but because I chose it. That choice feels empowering, and it’s something I didn’t expect to walk away with.
The program wasn’t perfect, and I think it’s important to say that. There were some flaws in the programming, including SMART Recovery groups being run by facilitators who weren’t certified at the time. However, when I raised this as something important to me (since 12-Step isn’t my path), the response mattered — those counsellors are now in the process of getting properly certified so SMART can be a legitimate option going forward. That willingness to listen and improve says a lot.
Some of the most meaningful work I did came from open sharing journal groups and journaling itself. I was able to dig into trauma I genuinely believed I had already worked through, and that process was uncomfortable but incredibly healing. I also made sober friends for life, which is something I’ll always be grateful for.
Food-wise, I’d give it a 3/5 — and that rating is heavily influenced by where I was at with my eating disorder. Breakfasts and dinners were fantastic with lots of options. Lunches leaned more toward comfort and fried foods, which did trigger me at times, but there were always healthier options available, and learning how to cope with those triggers became part of my recovery.
My relationships with my parents and friends grew stronger and more honest during my time here. I also understand that rehab can have boring moments — but rehab is what you make of it. I’ve been to treatment before, I know how these places work, and this centre worked for me because I worked the program. I went to yoga, used the gym daily, went on walks, attended every group, tried meditation, and even through the ups and downs, I grew.
Thank you again to Tim, the other primary counsellors who ran groups, the residential counsellors who helped keep me grounded and humble, and to Maria and Lee for working so closely with my family. Thank you to the chef and cleaning staff as well — your work doesn’t go unnoticed.
Realistically, thank you to CCFA for changing my perspective on how I choose to live my life.

The staff at CCFA  are wonderful.  The program has helped me tremendously.  But this is not a 5 star as advertised.  There was a lot of wasted time.  The art therapist was not available for 2 weeks and nothing  else was planned.  They had an employee appreciation lunch in our program room and sent us outside to carve pumpkins.  I was nit impressed with this for the money I spent.  Also did not know that people who were court ordered were there and getting the program for free when I paid a lot of money to be there and was serious about my recovery.  This is not right.  It became unhealthy for me at the end due to certain people that should not have been there.  Staff are great but something wrong with the management.  Seems they are more concerned about money.
