name: Centres for Health and Healing
description:
I cannot say enough good things about Centres for Health & Healing. From the moment I arrived, the staff were incredibly understanding, compassionate, and supportive. The accommodations were excellent and truly made the experience feel safe and comfortable. Having three meals a day prepared by amazing chefs was such a gift — the food was nourishing, thoughtful, and clearly made with care.

Most importantly, the knowledge, tools, and wisdom I learned during my time here have been absolutely life-changing. When I was admitted, I was in a very dark place in my life, and I can honestly say this place was a lifesaver. Centres for Health & Healing gave me hope, clarity, and the foundation I needed to move forward in a healthier, stronger way. I will always be deeply grateful for this experience.

Had an enriching experience at CFFH thanks to the amazing staff, as well as the centre's strong sense of community. I received all of the necessary tools to approach the next chapter of my life, and was empowered to continue on this life-long journey of healing. Thank you for showing me the compassion and caring I needed in such a time of need, I will forever be grateful for this experience. (Also the food was sooo good 🤌)

My experience at this rehab centre has been truly profound, and I’m incredibly grateful for the time I spent here. From the moment I arrived, the staff made me feel safe, supported, and cared for in a way I didn’t expect but deeply needed. Every member of the team, from the medical staff to the counsellors, treated me with genuine kindness, patience, and respect.

What stood out the most was how much they truly care about the people who come through their doors. They took the time to listen, to understand my situation, and to guide me at a pace that felt right for me. Their compassion and professionalism created an environment where healing actually felt possible.

The experience itself has been life-changing. The programs, the group sessions, and the one-on-one support all helped me gain clarity, build confidence, and reconnect with parts of myself I had lost touch with. I left feeling stronger, more grounded, and more hopeful than I have in a long time. They truly saved my life.

I can’t thank the team enough for everything they’ve done for me. This place truly makes a difference, and I would recommend it to anyone who is ready to take the next step in their journey.

Okay, let's begin.  I have a duty to share my experience as CFHH completely deserves it.
I was present for a 30-day program (July 29 to Aug 28), staying onsite.  Right off the bat, my time there was nothing but exceptional.  The entire CFHH Team and Facilities were top notch.  Very comfortable, my home away from home.
A big shout out to all the councilors, medical team and management.  I would love to mention some names however, future attendees, you will know.
The education and therapy I received was stellar.  If you are there with the intent of true healing, this is the place.  Full participation is required.  Staff is always available for one-on-one sessions.  They prioritize all individuals and genuinely care for you.
After completing 30 days, I've came out with great relationships.  My departure was also heartfelt.  It was a bittersweet moment for me as I was excited to complete the program, a sense of accomplishment however, on the flip side, now a month after being out, I miss CFHH.  I miss the CFHH Team and their property.  Beautiful property BTW.
Lastly, I cannot leave without giving credit to the great Chef's onsite.  Ken and Roberto.  Food was amazing, all three meals a day.  Being there for one month, I happily consumed all meals.  I also enjoyed interacting with them both daily.  Always made time for them.  Actually, they may have made time for me as I spent a good amount of time in the kitchen just observing and chatting away.
Highly recommend this establishment. A big Thank YOU once again to Team CFHH.  Keep up the excellent efforts. God Bless!

I was closely involved in a family member’s recovery at CFHH, and the support we got was incredible. They took the time to listen and worked with us to give us personal recommendations for success. That made a huge difference in his progress and we are thankful for their professional during a very difficult time.

name: EHN Bellwood Toronto
description:
I owe so much to all staff at Bellwood from Dr Weiss to my Psychotherapist Edward to all nurses, SCs, all educators , kitchen crew and cleaning crew all made me feel welcomed , cared for and that I truly mattered .
Without Bellwood can honestly say I would not be here today . With a CPTSD  , major post concussion syndrome , major depressive anxiety disorder , TRD diagnosis  before all this was determined I  was ready to throw in the towel ,as I felt my situation  had let my children down and I would no longer be able to return to my career as a FFer . With kindness ,professionalism of Bellwood staff  as well as the genuine kindness and support from my fellow Bellwood cohorts (friends)  I began to see there was a light and I slowly began to believe in myself again . I could write a book on my experience at Bellwood both the highs and the lows but like anything if you keep on fighting you can and will succeed . I'm happily  living a positive life surrounded by my children good friends and work colleagues and was promoted to Lieutenant in SJFD.
At first when arriving at Bellwood I was lost and in a daze but each day ( was one of the longest to stay 5.5mths) it would get more clear . But into the program do all the programs from Wendy's fitness classes to Christine's art classes and you will begin to see and feel more clear .

I am beyond grateful to Bellwood Treatment Centre for the incredible care they gave my friend, Peter Macreanu. He was in a dark place and we were all so worried about him. The transformation after his time at Bellwood has been nothing short of amazing. He came back healthier, clearer, and more hopeful than we have seen him in years. The staff cleanly care deeply about what they do. Bellwood did not just help Peter, they help all of us who love him.

My close friend Peter Maclean’s went to treatment to Bellwood and have a life changing experience. The program was structured, affective and delivered by professionals who clearly know what they are doing. Since finishing the program, Peter has doing better than ever. Bellwood gave him the tools to rebuild his life and I can’t thank them enough.

Peter M’s transformation at Bellwood Treatment Centre has been truly remarkable. Their holistic and compassionate approach played a vital role in his recovery. I would highly recommend this centre to anyone seeking meaningful, lasting change.

I was a client at Bellwoods in 2013, for a period of 80 days. It was a tremendously positive experience. I have maintained complete sobriety since then. This has led to vast changes and improvements in my life. I also continued on to volunteer thier in a few capacities. I have also participated in alumni activities over the years. I have also spoken a few times for the organization, to the clients, about my experience. I have found Bellwoods to be a positive environment, and I would recommend it to anyone.

name: Well Beings Counselling Clinic - Yonge & Davisville
address: Davisville Centre, 1920 Yonge St Suite 200, Toronto, ON M4S 3E2
description:
Well Beings Counselling has been a wonderful experience for our family. Romina Monastero was incredibly welcoming and made my daughter feel comfortable right away. She turned her first visit into a positive and reassuring experience, and we’ll definitely be booking with her again soon.

I've been working with Maria as my psychotherapist, and she has been exceptional. Her insight and guidance have been instrumental in my personal growth. Maria creates a supportive and understanding environment that really facilitates healing and self-discovery. Her expertise in therapy is evident, and she equips you with the tools needed for significant improvement. I wholeheartedly recommend Maria to anyone seeking effective and compassionate psychological support.

My experience with Well Beings Counseling was deeply disappointing. As a grief counselor, the individual
I spoke with struggled to retain key details of my experience during our 45-minute session. Her responses felt disengaged, as though she was distracted—possibly reading or typing while we spoke. At one point, she even placed me on hold due to what seemed like an interruption in her space.

The flow of the conversation was disjointed and unnatural, with frequent pauses and “umms,” giving the pplimpression that she was reading from a script rather than engaging meaningfully. Her language and articulation lacked the depth and empathy one would expect from a grief counseling session.

Overall, I would strongly discourage others from seeking support through this platform based on my experience.

My experience has been exceptional to date! Making me feel emotionally safe was paramount for me and my therapist did that so effortlessly! I would highly recommend Well Being as they will match you up with the best counsel based on what you need help with!! SWP

Growth takes time and Romina has the patience to help you heal from your long term and short term pain.

name: Adult Mental Health Services (Thunder Bay Regional Health Sciences Centre)
address: 980 Oliver Road, Thunder Bay, Ontario, P7B 6V4
description: There is a reason it's ranked as 3rd worst Hospital in Ontario. It's bandaid approach to more concern with emptying beds instead of treating the patient, has gone from being a joke to something the staff freely admits.

name: Assertive Community Treatment Teams (ICR Discoveries, Suite 1000)
address: ICR Discoveries, Suite 1000, 290 Munro Street, Thunder Bay, Ontario, P7A 7T1

name: Addictions Centre Inc
Addictions Mental Health Prince Edward
description:
If I could give a zero star I would. So I started CMHA years ago and honestly it was really awesome. The programs were awesome and so were the case workers. However, seems like the case workers have zero accountability for communication and never reschedule till you message them. They are always in a rush at closing your file instead of actually doing their job. (Helping the person). The manager for this office is a nice guy but I honestly feel like for a place that's suppose to support they really don't make you feel supported.

Also highly recommend the ADHD program that was a 5 star group and Natosha the coordinator for that is by far the only one that was actually helpful and she was in the belleville office.

I really think mental health is important and the people they hire need to be watched closer.

Honestly? This organization could do so much better. Case workers can't recognize when their clients are in crisis or volatile and acting out or cancelling services due to overwhelming emotions and clearly aren't in control of themselves. The de-escalation skills are lacking or nonexistent. I've been yelled at by a case worker before then had wait at the bottom of the barrel for another one, complained about her and never heard back, because why would I? I'm not important They care more about getting people off of the wait list than actually helping them. Don't cancel due to having a bad mental health day and not having the energy to go do it, you'll get a lecture when that's the last thing you need. Literally so pathetic that this is one of the few agencies in the area that can service adults with mental health issues, clearly no understanding of the population served.

This agency pretends to support people with mental illness, while prioritizing upper management and paperwork, there is far too many “managers” and not nearly enough front line support workers. I feel that this agency does not protect its employees or prioritize the clients they claim to serve.

As someone who has struggled for years with mental health and depression, I finally got the strength and mind set to reach out and connect with a therapist. After waiting 5 months on a waitlist, they called to get me booked in. A week later they called to cancel and reschedule that apt. No problem things happen. Rebooked that apt, for them to call me back and cancel it again. Another week goes by...After the third time now of calling me to cancel and reschedule with another therapist, Im getting the strong sense that no one there really cares to help. Thank you for confirming everything my depression has taught me all these years - that nothing is reliable and if its to good to be true, it probably is.

Disappointing. Had a great counsellor then no one for 3 months. Then 2 counsellors, no support in a meeting, they just sat there while I was being called a liar, I left, they stayed & couldn't look me in the eye after. If they don't believe & speak negatively about a client, what is the point. Asked my file to be closed. No trust, no faith.

name: Brock Cottage The
description:
Was actually a decent program to start but they lost a couple staff and then it was just people trying to fill holes they had no ability to. I hope that they replace the new regulars with actual competent staff instead of just what’s readily available otherwise they will go in circles.

Great place to start your recovery. With friendly staff who really want to help you and make a plan with you

gerald thornton  brock cottage saved my life when i went there i was a broken man and with the help of the staff i was able to understand were the alcohol did to me and were it took me i was there 11 months  and i am still sober today life could not be any better if you want to surrender call brock cottage you will get sober trust me you will learn to live again and be happy with yourself one day at a time thanks brock cottage and all the staff

name: CHEO - Montreal Road
description:
When I first arrived in Canada as a newcomer, I spent nearly two months with my daughter at CHEO. It was a challenging time for us, but the experience at CHEO made a huge difference.
Dr. Gardin closely monitored my daughter’s condition, providing clear explanations and answering all our questions with patience. The surgery was performed successfully by Dr. Maharajh, whose professionalism and care gave us great confidence throughout the process.
The nurses were incredibly kind and gentle with my daughter, helping to ease her stress and mine as well. Throughout our stay, I felt supported, respected, and cared for—not only medically, but emotionally as a parent in a new country.
I am very grateful to Dr. Gardin, Dr. Maharajh, and the entire CHEO team for their compassion and expertise.

We were sent to CHEO ER with a referral from our doctor today for an infant ultrasound (our baby is 5.5 months, we suspect a milk allergy, our GP wanted to rule out intussusception). The security guard that spoke with us upon arrival seemed a little too happy to inform us of the potential 20-hour waiting time ahead of us. Due to it being flu season, only one parent was allowed to stay in the waiting area with our baby. I left my wife with our son and headed for the place I was allowed to wait at the opposite end of the hospital. Beyond the initial waiting area, every hallway I walked through was lined with more parents and their children, waiting. It was a real shock to see. I returned shortly thereafter to my wife and son and we decided to head to the comforts of home rather than risk gathering a different illness from the cramped ER. The whole experience left me feeling helpless as a new parent. What a sad state for a children’s urgent care facility.

Edit: I have to revise my review following having to return to CHEO four days later and finally getting beyond the emergency room after an arduous 8-hour wait post-initial-triage. The nurses and doctors are all wonderful, hard-working individuals that have been so kind and loving. No one working the floor is at fault for the lack of support hospitals are receiving from both provincial and federal governments. If your child is sick, these people will take care of them, you just have to get through that first challenging hump. If you’re reading this, I wish you and your family well.

Extremely disappointed with the emergency wait times. Waiting over 6–8 hours with a sick 9 month infant is unacceptable and incredibly stressful for parents. While I understand hospitals are busy, better staffing and communication are urgently needed. No family should feel this helpless while seeking medical care.

I am very disappointed with my experience at CHEO ER, especially with the registration staff.

I brought my young son to the ER, where he was assessed by a doctor and suspected to have strep throat. The doctor clearly told me a throat sample would be sent to the lab, results would take 1–2 days, and I would receive a phone call with a prescription if the result was positive.

The result came back positive on MyChart, yet no one ever called me.

During this time, my son became extremely unwell. He did not eat or drink for two full days and did not urinate the entire day before I returned to the ER. Concerned and desperate, I went back to the ER only to get the prescription so my child could start treatment.

At registration, instead of receiving help or concern, the staff member was very rude, dismissive, and even laughed. When I explained that my son hadn’t been eating, drinking, or peeing, she showed no care or urgency at all. She repeatedly said, “They will call you when they call you. Maybe tomorrow, maybe after tomorrow.”

I was told that my only options were:
•	Wait at home for a call that never came, or
•	Sit in the ER for another minimum of 8 hours just to see a doctor again only for a prescription.

This was incredibly frustrating and unsafe.

To make matters worse, I am pregnant and due next month, yet I was not allowed to have my husband accompany me inside to help manage my sick child. Handling a very ill child alone, while pregnant, with no compassion or flexibility shown by staff, was overwhelming.

The lack of communication, empathy, and basic professionalism at registration was shocking. No parent should be treated this way—especially when their child is clearly unwell and at risk of dehydration.

CHEO is supposed to be a children’s hospital that supports families. Unfortunately, this experience showed the complete opposite.

One of  nurse who was working in triage, told us that she “bet” our daughter was not sick.

Parents come to the emergency department because they know their children better than anyone else. We monitor them for hours before deciding to seek medical care. When we come to the emergency room, we are looking for professionals not people who are willing to “bet” on our child’s health.

They were hiding their badge names, likely because they are ashamed of their behavior and actions.

name: Arab Community Centre of Toronto (Scarborough Office)
description:
I had a great experience with the Arab community while renewing my work permit. Tasbeeh was extremely helpful, patient, and professional throughout the whole process. I truly appreciate her support and dedication. I highly recommend their services to anyone who needs help with immigration or work permit matters.

The community center is absolutely wonderful! The facilities are clean, welcoming, and always full of positive energy. The staff truly go above and beyond to make everyone feel included and supported.

A special thanks to Sally Ishag for her kindness, professionalism, and dedication. She always greets everyone with a smile and makes each visit such a great experience. Her efforts make a huge difference and really reflect the heart of this center.

Highly recommend this place to anyone looking for a warm and supportive community!

I would like to express my sincere appreciation for the staff member ASHLEY اشلي at the Arab Community Centre for Service who supported me. They were exceptionally kind, patient, and professional throughout our interaction. Their thoughtful guidance through the application process ensured that I understood each step clearly, and their respectful approach made a positive impact on my experience. I am truly grateful for their support and dedication.

I had an excellent experience working with Ismael Joya today. He was incredibly helpful, professional, and attentive throughout my appointment. He guided me through every step, answered all my questions patiently, and made the entire process smooth and stress-free. I truly appreciate his dedication and support and he went above and beyond to assist me. Highly recommend working with him!

Excellent experience with Mr. Bahaa. Fast service, professional handling, and the credential evaluation was completed efficiently.

name: EHN Guardians Gateway
address: 2225 Lansdowne St West, Peterborough, ON K9J 0G5, Canada
description:
Doing the CTAP program @Gateway was a pivotal point in my recovery, the staff and programming helped me move forward and keep fighting for my recovery. For those that are staff, its apparent its not just a job, they truly care and are invested in supporting every client. It's a place I learned to smile again, to fight for myself and be more comfortable taking up space and find peace in my career as a First Responder ending. I am not sure where I would be had I not been able to attend this facility. Forever grateful

I just completed the 9 week in-patient program and it has changed my life for the better. The staff was very supportive and positive. They understand the unique trauma and addiction issues among first responders, veterans, and active serving members. The staff were quick to identify interpersonal situations and deal with them quickly and effectively. I felt confident and safe while working through the groups and the grounding and wellness programs. I will be eternally grateful to everyone at Gateway.

I cannot say enough positive things about Gateway! The staff, the facility, the program. This program saved me… and I mean that quite literally.  I was apprehensive about the group sessions initially but they were amazing and I’m so grateful to have had that experience with my peers. If you are a first responder/military struggling, please, get yourself into Gateway. It will change you. It will change your life. Drink the kool-Aid and jump in with both feet and I promise you, you won’t be disappointed. These people truly care about first responders and ITS OKAY to need the help and it’s okay to take the help!

If you have negative presuppositions about rehabilitation centres due to previous experiences, rest assured that Gateway is unlike any treatment centre you've ever been to! The staff are GENUINELY extraordinarily kind, caring, supportive, intelligent, and will help you through any difficulties that may arise! The meals are gourmet and accommodating for ANY special needs! It feels like love, respect, peace, and hospitality the world needs. You will have an experience that will leave an unforgettable, meaningful, positive, and peaceful imprint on your life. This is the place to be when you're ready for help and a catharsis for your soul, spirit, physical, and mental health.

When I first arrived, I was very close to dying due to my abuse of alcohol. The five fine folks from my cohort are a truly outstanding group of friends. The staff is amazing and very compassionate. I do not regret my time spent at Gateway. I left a far healthier and better person than when I first arrived. If you or someone you know require help, this is the place to go.

To Sean and the rest of the staff, you saved my life. Thank you!!

Jean (John) Blain

East Nipissing Mental Health Services (Mattawa) - Crisis Intervention Program
description: 24 hour service. Need help then call and talk

name: Uxbridge Hospital
description:
Despite reading many negative reviews, we decided to bring our child to Uxbridge Hospital to avoid long wait times elsewhere. We were pleasantly surprised by the excellent care we received. From the front desk staff to the X-ray technician, nurse, and doctor, everyone was kind, professional, and respectful. Test results were completed promptly. We would not hesitate to return, even if it means a 30-minute drive.

My father spent close to a month in this hospital due to fluid overload related to liver issues. The doctors here prescribed him diuretics to help shed the extra fluid but they also prescribed him propranolol after they saw varices on a CT scan. As a result his blood pressure kept dropping and he couldn't safely take his diuretics. His condition worsened yet they still discharged him.

A month later he had an endoscopy to see if there were any varices and there none. The doctor was shocked to hear he was prescribed propranolol based off of a CT scan. Because so much time had passed without him being able to take diuretics, his condition worsened which lead to his death another month later.

Run from this place if your loved one is here.

If I could give 0 stars, I would.Everyone at this hospital seems like they absolutely hate their job. Instead of greeting or assisting patients, the nurses just sit in their office glued to their phones. The lack of care, compassion, and urgency is shocking. If you have an emergency, avoid this place at all costs- they do not care about their patients or their time.

What kind of hospital only has one doctor on a Saturday night?

Longest wait ever with a serious inquiry. Checked in at 6:15 pm seen the dr at 11pm. Did scans and x-rays got results and the dr told me she doesn't see anything to the pain I'm experiencing. Ready to discharge me without doing an examination or checking my issues physically.  Sabir, Sonia MD, the worse ER doctor I've come across. Very rude with an attitude, no compassion, very distraughtful experience.  I would never return back to this hospital. Because, this hospital did completely nothing for me concerning my health, I then went to Southlake at 11pm and got the full attention  and compassion there. I was in and out of ER at Southlake by 12:45 am with my concern and issue resolved. I was able to sleep in peace this night, thanks to Southlake.

My daughter visited this hospital Monday night (mar3) for her shoulder. She arrived around 4 pm and needed someone to look at her possibly dislocated shoulder. They took ex rays within 45 min then proceeded to wait until after 10pm. She politely asked once at 7:30 if there was any updates. Nothing told to sit and wait. Again at 10 she tried asking if any updates and she was the only person left in waiting room. The nurse on duty proceeded to tell her that she was being ignorant and when my daughter said she was being polite was just wondering what was going on the nurse motioned for a security guard. What the heck is going on here?!?!?  She walked out never getting any results and still in pain. Absolutely ridiculous treatment and will never be going back. Wish I could give a negative star

Canadian Mental Health Association - Muskoka Parry Sound - Huntsville Office
Canadian Mental Health Association - Simcoe County - Orillia Satellite Office - Assertive Community Treatment Team

description:
This is an amazing place. The staff member that helped us was amazing. Thank you.

In regards to the passport one program, Catulpa Can not and should be trusted in regards to finding a broker for passport one funding, i have personally gone through 2 brokers, user nearly 1000$ worth of passport funding only for the brokers to constantly make mistakes, miscommunicate, and just blatantly not read emails correctly, which results in your passport funding getting botched.

Do not get brokers through Catulpa they can not be trusted.

All of the people there are so great and so helpful and so kind

name: Enaahtig Healing Lodge and Learning Centre - Community Mental Health and Addictions Outreach Services
description:
The place used to be number 1 in healing for the community.  Now it's just a empty shell of its former self.  If you're in need of help. Please keep searching.

Great team of people working together for the greater good.

Nothing like a friend that never disappoints, I recommend the service.

Amazing people

Ha

name: Kinark Child and Family Services - Simcoe County Region - Barrie Office
description:
Kinark has now failed to communicate for 3 weeks regarding their actions, have stated in emails they should follow up and haven't continued questions from 3 weeks ago regarding a child protection matter. Since then I've spoken to a supervisor and manager and neither will give me a clear answer. From their emails it is clear they believe their time too valuable to give me a detailed answer and only want to give partial answers to specific aspects of the situation.

The manager I recently emailed refused to meet with me to discuss the situation with my autism support worker as their emails show they put as little effort into addressing this issue as possible.

The response on my last review caused me to delete it and lower my rating as well as lower my review score, as it is clear the "owner" has to not talked to the people I have dealt with and they are refusing to answer a simple question.

Previous review by Taylor Marisa is me, but cannot edit / change it, so reposting an updated review:

Access Centre only supervises for your (parent) screw ups. Staff have missed / not recorded many things my children have said that are extremely important and relevant for our custody case. I will have to pay hundreds of dollars for the printed records, as well as having to pay for them on behalf of sending the children's father whom assaulted me. When I am expected to pay HUNDREDS of dollars for notes (and to provide them to the man whom assaulted me) where staff has been watching every second between my children and I- I expect accurate notes, otherwise what am I paying for? Absolutley rediculous and unacceptable. Not fair to not record that my children have not been bathed in over two weeks while my daughter is verbally expressing that, and chose to not record my son telling me that he asked his father if he had my phone number as to call me. My children are expressing concerning statements about their basic needs not being met, as well as expressing that they want more communication with their mother, which is absolutley crucial information when it comes to going back to court, which I absolutley plan to.

When I called CAS about my children not being bathed, I was told the access centre staff should have called. Upon speaking with Shelly, management, she feels a child not being bathed for 2+ weeks is not a concern of theirs. So now I have been informed that 1) I cannot call CAS as it should be the centre staff making the call and 2) The center staff don't find it concerning that my children are coming in complaining of not having ate enough, or not having been bathed in over 2 weeks.
They do not have the children's best interest in mind. They do not have parents best interest in mind. No one is addressing my children's best interest and due to working with this centre, CAS is claiming I cannot make inquiries about my children's best interest.

Agree with the other review that states they care about one thing and one thing only, keeping their jobs. When I expressed that this is not working for me the way it is going, I was told "well it's been like this for 30 years". Currently waiting for someone whom I can make a formal complaint with over how short and basic these notes are- as was shared in another review posted here. Clearly multiple people have an issue with their -lack of- notes, and when charging people hundreds of dollars (they charge one party and both parties recieve the papers) for these notes... they need to be doing better. In fact, pretty sure the review I left just now in 5 mins or less has more information in it than any of the write ups that have been shared with me thus far.

I was told I can feel free to make my own notes each visit.. does that not defeat the entire purpose of this center? Given that us, as parents, were sent here because we are not trusted to be alone with our kids for one reason or another.. and staff expects the court to take our handwritten notes seriously...? No.
That's why we're at an access center... so the CENTER may send reports to the judge- like the judge requested. I'm not paying to do someone else's job for them, when i'll be paying HUNDREDS of dollars for useless notes.

Read another bad review about Donna.
I refuse to work with or speak to this worker whatsoever due to feeling she is biased, as well as her lack of professionalism in the field. Larry and Renee are quality workers and can't thank them enough for making it a positive atmosphere for my children and I. Have issues and concerns with new staff member Glen as well.

Update: was told staff would be making changes to the way my visits go as to address my issues and concerns, and not a thing has changed. In fact, worse. Told me what I wanted to hear, yet nothing has changed and now I am not even given time to ask questions at the end of the visits. This place is a headache.

Overall, I’m grateful that the Access Centre provided a generally safe place for my children. The process and ongoing scheduling was needlessly complicated and time consuming. I often felt that if I wasn’t incredibly accommodating to last minute scheduling requests, I’d be portrayed in a negative light in the eyes of the court and was reminded of this by the scheduler repeatedly. I do not feel that the way things were explained to me initially were how things transpired. My understanding was that detailed unbiased notes would be taken of each visit, however, the notes were exceedingly brief and in my opinion did not portray a clear picture of what transpired start to finish during a visit.

Had issues during sign up with this company but luckily my issues were addressed by other staff members.
Was feeling discouraged and concerned with this company in the beginning but my concerns have been addressed each time- now feeling hopeful and excited to see my children.

Larry is phenomenal and a pleasure to work with. Renee is wonderful and also pleasant to work with.
Shelly is considerate and a big help.
Thank you all.

For my situation with family child services has been great! I am a Dad fighting in court to spend time with my son and this side of the facility was great to me and my situation.. the facility was clean and organized! The staff did a great job bonding with my son and helpful with any questions I have had

name: La Clé - Penetanguishene - Main St - Children and Youth Mental Health Services
description:
My son has been working with this organization for 3 months and he still isn't any closer to funding for a course he wants to take. They should admit they don't know anything about some courses so people can go elsewhere before they completely run out of money to live. Not acceptable.

An organization that offers alot of french services in Simcoe County. Lots of activites for families.

The server here was very rude. Didn’t seem to like her job at all and made it pretty obvious. Seemed annoyed when people asked for drinks.

Great events.

Location of administrative offices, the early childhood center, the radio station, and a large meeting/activity room. A place to celebrate in French!

name: Children's Hospital London Health Sciences Centre - Child and Adolescent Mental Health Care Program - Eating Disorders Services
address: 800 Commissioners Rd E, Zone B, Level 8, London, ON N6A 5W9
description:
I want to sincerely thank the doctors, nurses, and Gynecology team at London Health Sciences Centre for the excellent care I received during my stay from referral from Sault Ste Marie.
The doctors were professional and reassuring, and the nurses were amazing — attentive, kind, and compassionate every step of the way. They made a difficult experience much easier, and my partner felt completely at ease knowing I was in such good hands.
The services overall were excellent, right down to the meals — even the food was great. Everything was well organized and supportive, and I truly felt cared for throughout my stay.
I am very grateful for the outstanding care and professionalism. Miigwech

Ive been holding on to process what happened to me at LHSC. I fell pregnant with twins in March 2025, and I was assigned to have Dr Schmidt as my OBGYN. by month 7 I met him twice, and they did barely any exams or observations except scans to tell me everything looks “good”. thats all i ever got told. I had a thinning cervix and not a single person spotted it or checked for it. I had no cervix examination, and on scans they only checked my babies that they’re growing healthy. not the surroundings for my babies or myself. I continued as normal during my pregnancy, I took it easy of course but I would do minimal house work and was unaware i should have had MUCH more bedrest for my cervix. I went into early labour, and this resulted in my twins being born 2 months premature. they spent 2 months in the NICU. During my babies time in the NICU, they barely updated me. i visited EVERY single day, and found out information half the time when i asked for it. I cant even begin to put into words how disgusted I am that I was treated this way. thank god my babies made it home safe, and healthy. the NICU had 3 nurses that actually went above and beyond for my daughters. the other nurses let my baby get a bad diaper rash, bruises, when i advocated for my baby or tried to ask for specific things it barely got any action afterwards. It was rare i felt heard. there was mostly good days yes, but those bad days still haunt me to this day. my OBGYN did not once check on me afterwards. they didnt ask how i was. when i asked for an investigation into how my cervix got missed, what it means to have a thinning cervix or just any help on understanding what to avoid for my next pregnancy and stay healthy, crickets. id feel ashamed if i was them. my postpartum nurses are angels on earth. my handful of nicu nurses that helped me are angels on earth. everyone else is just… yeah. I dont think its appropriate for me to write how i feel about them. I hope no one else goes through what I have.

LHSC has been very hit or miss in my experience. There are many amazing staff members across other departments, and even some within mental health, who are kind, professional, and genuinely supportive.

Unfortunately, my experience with psychiatry was disappointing. I found my psychiatrist, Dr. Pollai, to be dismissive and at times rude. I was given a diagnosis after a single 45-minute appointment, which did not feel thorough. Because of this, I sought a private second opinion, which was significantly more comprehensive and accurate.

I later asked if she would be willing to review the private assessment with me, discuss the findings, and consider writing an addendum. Instead of having that conversation, I have had to involve Patient Relations in hopes of facilitating a fair and respectful discussion.

Additionally, despite having a documented history of an ADHD diagnosis, she stated that she does not “see” my ADHD. When I shared concerns about suspected autism, she refused to refer me for an assessment and instead provided a questionnaire, told me to ignore the answers, and said that if I scored high, I “had it.” This felt dismissive and not aligned with appropriate assessment practices.

In mental health care especially, it is vital to work collaboratively with patients, provide clear communication, and generate accurate, well-reasoned reports. Unfortunately, this was not my experience within psychiatry, which left me feeling invalidated and unsupported. I expected a more collaborative and professional approach within a hospital setting.

LHSC has repeatedly made me feel unsafe, dismissed, and unworthy of treatment. From 2020 to present, I have had several terrible experiences within different departments of this hospital.

I had a referral appointment to psychiatry yesterday. I was treated poorly. I felt very uncomfortable, and excused myself before my appointment ended. The psychiatrist questioned the use of my mobility aid. My gender identity; my transition; my gender expression, my sexuality (and relationship with my partner).

During the covid-19 outbreak, I tried to reach out for mental health help. It was a referral, but it was for video conference due to social distancing rules. I found the session unhelpful, and the person I'd been referred to speak to ignored my concerns, and spoke over me instead. I did not want a follow up.

I have been talked out of pain management for an intra-uterine device insertion. The procedure made me feel violated; it was so painful and terrifying. I was left naked and alone in the room afterwards. Zero explanation about how to check strings, address concerns, or when to book an appointment to check-up.

I'm tired of being treated like I don't matter.

Worst hospital ever in my opinion, their doctors are ignorant, rude horrible misdiagnosis and huge miscommunications to dying family members. When you receive a phone call early in the morning that your dad had a bad fall in their care.Meanwhile, he was dead.How do you misinterpret that in a phone call?Leading family members to think their loved ones are still alive.And they're just coming in to see them banged up.  people are better off dying on the street than entering Hell At LHSC and not having good care 😒 my father would have been better off.Dying at home than in the care of this ridiculous hospital.I wish the ambulance drivers had a taken him to u.H like they were to do to begin with, they changed their minds.What a mistake

name: Mood Disorders Association Of Ontario - Changing Minds With Youth
address: 1600, Unit 17-2300 Yonge St, Toronto, ON M4P 1E4
description:
I have been going to MDAO for a long time. I've done the WRAP program. Attend dropin groups. Have used the warm lines, and social worker.
I can't say enough positive things about this organization.  This organization is run by volunteers and peer support services, along with paid staff. They are empathetic, kind, supportive. They have walked in our shoes.
In WRAP, I  learned I have agency, can ask for help, but can do something to help myself.
In dropin group I realized I'm not alone. I listen. Offer validation and support.
I don't know where I'd be today. I can't thank them enough.

*Edit from 5 stars. I NEVER gave this place 5 stars. My review had been edited somehow because I tried this for the "WRAP" program and never got a call back even a few years later

The person I spoke with was so unhelpful and withholding. I still wish to keep them anonymous, but the service was awful, like they weren’t trained to do their job and completely uninterested. I was highly disappointed.

Empathetic and caring staff, helpful peer to peer and drop-in programs, and a supportive environment; all of which have helped me in healing the mind one day at a time. Thank YOU Mood Disorders Association of Ontario. God Bless!

I was looking forward to participating in the painting program and peer support they offer only to learn that over a year after Ontario ended the segregation between people who choose to get a vaccine and people who chose to excercize their right not to, the Hope and Me organization are still discriminating, even tho Ontario ended requirment of proof of vaccination, in all settings on March 1st, 2022. Its very unfortunate that an organization and program geared toward mental health is refusing people for a non mandated vaccine, especially since some people did not have a choice in being able to get the vaccine due to a severe phobia that is life altering and debilitating.

name: Wellness and Emotional Support (WES) For Youth Online
Anishnawbe Health Toronto – Gerrard
description:
When the Program Director flips a handicapped community member the middle finger and there is no accountability. This kind of behavior is acceptable at this organization. This was done in plain sight of other staff and community. The leadership is absolutely vacant, out to lunch. The community member is still waiting for an official apology from the organization. This place is an absolute joke. More to come...

This is the only location for Psychiatric help and as a Two-Spirit Ojibwe woman, the demand for female psychiatrists are high as I’m aware there’s only ONE available.
Hopefully with the new location at Cherry St, it will be more diverse as I’ve recently switched from my misogynistic psychiatrist to a female psychiatrist as I take my health and well-being seriously.

This place offers Indigenous Peoples a place to get all health services here. From seeing a doctor for referills or a dentist when needing work done on your teeth. They will help you with addictions counseling as well as therapy. Social workers on site. Doctors on site as well as helping with homeless outreach. If you need help with putting g your life back together they can help. Dont hesitateto deop in and ask for help.

Great place of healing! Thanks to all the team members that work tirelessly to assist the community. Pete, you are so special! Thanks for always making the bookings so seamless Shelly! Great job everyone.

A very warm welcome with respect and kindness from lining up to leaving, from each and everyone you come in contact with , wow if all places could be like this it would be a wonderful place to live, so simple with no effort just love and peace and joy.

name: Anishnawbe Health Toronto – Carlton Street
description:
Been coming here since I moved here and was in their oshki program
For me the sense of community has been there since day one. If I need anything I will always be supported.
The hub is more accessible than the old locations was for me and I have all my providers in one spot which makes it easier.
Miigwetch AHT team

Ps I included some pictures of the new building

They never answer the phone no matter what time of day it is...When I finally got in contact with someone to help me move, Chelsea ruined everything for me.

Story:
I called weeks prior to schedule the movers and she said the date and time and confirmed in email as well. Then the day of my move, they didn't show up and she called me to tell me she never booked it hence why I paid $1000 last minute to get my stuff over the next day. I filled out the entire application as well only to be told that I don't qualify for anything because I don't have a disability and I'm not an old time client. (I've been a client for over 5 years and had help moving before so I don't get the issue?). I asked for an exception and for a refund but she said she couldn't help me.

TLDR: Chelsea screwed me over with my move and I paid $1000. Don't even bother with these people to help you with housing. I'm going to tell my Reserve this establishment does not help native people and they are a FRAUD.

5 stars for the architecture and for the incredible public art/ sculpture !!!!

very helpful caring staff except when making appointments to see your dr its 3 weeks away but other then that its a very warm caring place:) kristie is the best very helpful.

As a native. Ive been waiting WEEKS on these folks to help me get back on my feet but I had to go elsewhere just because im starting school in September and I really need help. I found counseling at a two spirits group for natives. I have a new case manager. I had BAD FEET both of them and I have an ingrown toenail. I found a foot doctor on Bay Street when I asked anishnawbe I really needed to see someone. BUT NOOO, they keep me waiting, waiting and waiting still nothing. They are taking too long to get back to some people but I noticed there are other people that they WILL get back to in an instant like as if they are the golden people of nishnawbe health.

I wanted to get help for depression and anxiety and I told them that on the phone. Email even in person. STILL NOTHING!!!!!

I had to go elsewhere for mu services and im starting school soon and I want to be prepared.

Im sad of the new location, its soo far now from downtown and sometimes hard to get to because I hardly see buses going on front street and im always WALKING!!!

I MISS THE OLD LOCATIONS AND WORKERS!!!
They cared about me in 2016-2019 but aftet covid started, it ruined EVERYTHING and now these new people are RUINING MY PLAN to get BETTER!

THEY DONT CARE UNLESS YOU HURT THEIR SCRAWNY LITTLE FEELINGS! THEY WILL CLOSE YOU DOWN!

name: Durham Community Health Centre - Oshawa South
description:
As a former youth in care, I want to go on record about my experience with this agency. I live with agoraphobia, complex PTSD, depression, and anxiety. Despite these challenges, I have always been respectful and cooperative.

Unfortunately, the worker assigned to me at DCHC consistently treated me with hostility and disrespect, often taking her anger out on me and worsening my depression. Her conduct was unprofessional and emotionally abusive, to the point that it triggered my PTSD.

When I requested a different worker for my own mental health, management claimed there were no other options. After I withdrew consent to continue, they retaliated by blacklisting me from receiving help through the agency altogether.

I work with multiple professionals — including lawyers and other agencies — who can confirm how unreasonable and discriminatory this treatment was. I have extensive written evidence documenting this ongoing mistreatment and am currently exploring a civil lawsuit.

This behavior is unacceptable from an organization that claims to serve vulnerable individuals. People seeking support deserve compassion, not cruelty.

I’m writing this review as a disappointed and frustrated neighbour that lives in this area.

Since the new development and add-ons to this establishment have started, I’ve noticed a difference in traffic. Specifically, the designated smoking area has been taken away with no new one in sight. Instead, employees and clients are standing on our curbs, side walks and our lower lawns, hacking cigarettes. The amount of cigarette butts my neighbours and I are witnessing is rather disgusting and frankly, disrespectful. Firstly, as a parent, I don’t want my kids looking out the window seeing adults smoking and flicking ashes and butts on our sidewalk and lawn. Secondly, the lack of awareness and respect for our properties is astounding. Who has the balls to willingly stand on private property littering their cigarette butts all over?

The biggest kicker of all is months ago I contacted the centre asking to speak to management about this. I was directed to a manager named Jeff Dart. After speaking with him for about ten minutes, expressing my frustrations and concerns, he assured me he’d bring it to his superior and that it would be rectified. He also mentioned he would give me a phone call back in a few weeks to follow up. Surprise, no phone call, no follow up. I shared my name, my cell phone number and email and Mr. Dart did NOT do what he said he would. And what is the aftermath of it all? More cigarettes littered all over the sidewalks and curbs. Mr. Dart deserves to be called out for his lack of care and broken words.

Every single day I walk by this disgusting litter. As a neighbour and parent, I am disappointed and frustrated which has lead me to leave a public review in hopes managers will actually take this matter seriously. I’ve also posted about this in public forums in hopes other community members know about the tasteless, disrespectful behaviour that occurs from this establishment.

Suggestion for management to consider: Creating a DESIGNATED smoking area that employees and clients can access that doesn’t affect nature and neighbours. This is an easy solution that is respectful and affordable for your team to develop. Please consider it so the littering and loitering stops.

Update: Cig butt photos on my neighbours property and all over the sidewalk. I’ll continue to document and push this further until management acknowledges my concerns.

10/10. Dr. Oketch at the Durham Community Health Centre has been my husband's doctor for several years.

Dr. Oketch is a great listener, shows compassion towards the elderly, and cares about the whole body (mental and physical).

Dr. Oketch is one of the best family doctors under one roof at the Durham Community Health Centre. Highly recommend.

I have been trying to get in contact with a doctor for over 3 weeks now to start hormone therapy. The lady who helped me at first, Julie, was very lovely and accommodating until she started to ignore my emails. I was told that I would receive a call more than a week ago from the Oshawa manager Sabrina to book an appointment. I have not received any calls to book my appointment with this doctor, nor have I received any emails from anybody at the clinic about why I am being ignored. I definitely will never consider or recommend this clinic.

Five star to Carea, the airport boulevard clinic opp Mandarin. From the administration staff to the nurses, absolute 100/100. If it’s appropriate to review the events/programs organized for children and parents in the community, then five star to the teachers too. They make living in Durham region, a welcoming and safe one.

name: Central Toronto Community Health Centres – Queen West Site
description:
This is the first time in about 10 years that I’ve had a genuinely great experience with a family doctor and clinic.

Unfortunately, I forgot his name, but I haven’t seen him in a while and I hope he’s still working there—the receptionist (a Black Jamaican man, if I remember correctly) was absolutely amazing. From the moment I walked in, completely lost and unsure of who to ask for help, he welcomed me and offered assistance right away. I was looking to apply for a new primary care physician and had a few general questions about the clinic based on things I’d read online. He was professional, friendly, and incredibly helpful. Thank you!

As for the doctors, I briefly saw Sonika Kainth, who was great, and now I’m with Dr. Jennifer Gordon, wow. From day one, Dr. Gordon was thorough, precise, and extremely attentive to detail. I immediately felt welcomed and genuinely cared for. As someone with PCOS, I’ve dealt with many dismissive doctors in the past who made rude comments or completely ignored my concerns. But Dr. Gordon took everything I said seriously, offered recommendations, made the necessary referrals, and truly made me feel heard.

It’s clear that the people who work here care about what they do, and for the first time in years, I don’t dread going to a doctor’s appointment.

…. Also my indigenous boyfriend comes here as well for the last 6+ years and loves how great they are!

Excellent community health hub, with a wide range of services, from family doctoring, to counseling, to walk-in medical treatment, to a safe injection site, to services for the indigenous community, to street-involved help and general advice. Very friendly and open. An elevator for those with mobility issues. Great care is taken with public health measures, with masking and sanitizing. Picked up my nasal naloxone kit to have in case of emergencies.

Front desk staff have been friendly in person, but sometimes a bit short on the phone. Appointments are often mixed up. For example, booked a phone appointment  but it got switched to in person with no update so we lost the appointment. Our dr seems incompetent: confidently gives inaccurate information, denied a referral, doesn’t prepare for important appointment about starting a medication so we went to a different Dr for that. I am very dissatisfied with this place and hope to change clinics.

Update: it took them many months and multiple requests from my new doctor to transfer my files. I also had to call them multiple times and they then claimed my doctor only contacted them once and they immediately complied. This is not true as I had already contacted them myself and my doctor has recorded each request. So now I believe the admin staff is also incompetent.

Dental Clinic

We scheduled an appointment and informed the dental office that the tooth had cavities. When we arrived, they merely examined the tooth and did not take any further action. They explained that they did not have enough time to address the issue. This is perplexing, as they were fully aware of the problem when the appointment was made.
We still need to paying $80 for nothing.

Their lack of consideration for others' time is evident. We now have to wait another two months to have the cavities treated. This situation is entirely unreasonable.

I'm so disappointed. I saw the incredible resources. I saw that they support Indigenous people with traditional medicine on top of western medicine. I saw the free naturopathy and dental. I wasn't able to use any of it. This place is extremely difficult to get a hold of on a good day. There's no voicemail or email as an alternative to their phone line. I also just found out, after two years, that my new patient forms weren't submitted, so my old GP was being fined all five times I visited. I'm going back since apparently I'm still his patient, but I also found out there's a massive fee for having transferred my medical records, which is fine and part of the process, but nobody at this health centre warned me about any payment. I've also had concerns about hygiene (like putting my speculum on the table right up against someone else's pee sample before using it and acting like I'm irrational for asking not to do that) so I'm not too sad to leave. I just feel like this was a huge waste of time. None of my health issues have been addressed in these two years.

name: Centre de santé communautaire de l'Estrie – Embrun
description:
Dr. Sabrina Seguin is truly a blessing. She takes the time to really listen and makes you feel heard and supported. She’s always there when you need her, and that kind of care is rare. I’m so grateful to have her as my doctor.

Got my blood test done today. And these people went above and beyond. With someone who has struggled her whole life with blood tests and passing out, this was one of the best experiences. Thank you Tracy and the gentleman at the front (not sure what his name was). But he was truly a gentleman. Although I nearly passed out these two took such good care of me.

Terrible service to see s doctor. Lack of communication.

Excellent service

Excellent service and care.

name: Centre de santé communautaire du Grand Sudbury – Clinique du Coin
description:
Compared to another centre in another city, the front person here is rude at best, unwelcoming and certainly neither communitaire nor fostering sante. A great waste of taxpayers money.

A very warm welcome, always up-to-date information, and the best advice given without any taboos. May all the kind souls who work there and care for patients at all times be blessed.

The welcome is warm and professional, and the medical staff truly takes the time to listen to patients. Consultations are well organized.
This center offers a wide range of services, including primary care, housing, training, immigration services, counseling, and more.
This contributes to providing comprehensive care for a French speaker.
I highly recommend this center to anyone seeking quality healthcare, especially newcomers due to the immigration service.

Enjoy line dancing every Monday with Thérèse in Chelmsford! Thank you for making this free for seniors!

Excellent staff and healthcare assistance, affordable additional fees. A good place to go for visits or appointments. Highly certified!

name: Centre de santé communautaire de Sudbury
description:
Compared to another centre in another city, the front person here is rude at best, unwelcoming and certainly neither communitaire nor fostering sante. A great waste of taxpayers money.

A very warm welcome, always up-to-date information, and the best advice given without any taboos. May all the kind souls who work there and care for patients at all times be blessed.

Enjoy line dancing every Monday with Thérèse in Chelmsford! Thank you for making this free for seniors!

Excellent staff and healthcare assistance, affordable additional fees. A good place to go for visits or appointments. Highly certified!

name: Centre Francophone de Toronto
description:
I had a good experience with CFGT by providing excellent support and services to the Francophone community, and the staff are professional, welcoming, and genuinely committed to helping people feel supported and understood. The environment is respectful, inclusive, and well-organized, making it easy to access services and receive guidance.

I would also like to express my sincere appreciation to Zineddine, who was extremely attentive, friendly, and helpful.

Thank you to Jeanne & Barbara too.

We have been working with Zain for a long time, and it has always been an excellent experience. He consistently referred us high-quality, well-qualified tenants and handled everything with professionalism, care, and a very human approach.

Zain is reliable, attentive, and genuinely focused on creating positive outcomes for everyone involved. His dedication and integrity have made a real difference to our business, and we are very grateful for the collaboration we’ve had with him.

We highly recommend Zain and wish him all the best in this new chapter of his life. ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐

I recently had a wonderful experience thanks to the excellent customer service provided by Zinedine. He was very helpful, patient, and genuinely willing to assist. He listened carefully to my concerns and went above and beyond to make sure everything was taken care of. It’s rare to come across someone so dedicated and professional. I truly appreciate his effort and kindness. Highly recommended!

I'll give anytime 5 stars to the Francophone Centre of Fairview.
I am a patient there for more than 10 years. The good thing it starts with parking. You have a free parking at Fairview mall. Very easy access even by subway.
Every time I call for an appointment the Reception/Secretary Brikena always will comfort me with an appointment the first available in the office . I am always happy with the services there by all staff. I am very happy with the doctors, nurses, and the secretary that work at Fairview site.

Centre Francophone Clinique de Fairview—
I’ve been a patient under Dr.Gmora for 10+ years and have always received the best treatment. Unfortunately he is retiring and the new doctor who will replace him along with the receptionist Brikana  are the reason why I left this clinic and found a new doctor.

Brikana the receptionist is extremely rude to patients and lacks basic patient care skills. She yells at patients which is unprofessional and is not always honest about doctor’s availability.

The new doctor, Dr. Yasmine,  needs to learn to listen to patients and know when to outsource when unsure. I came to her complaining of bone pain for a couple weeks, but she kept suggesting that my ACUTE pain was CHRONIC in nature and was insinuating that I had fibromyalgia.

When my lab results would return abnormal, she was never able to provide me a concrete explanation and kept navigating the conversation towards chronic pain even though I had not even met the criteria of chronic pain. My hematology report was abnormal but she didn’t feel the need to refer me to a specialist until I told her my sister’s hematologist took a look and suggested i be referred.

She prescribed me chronic pain medication for pain that was between 7-10/10. After my 2nd emergency visit the doctor prescribed me morphine as he was suspecting a possible sickle cell crisis. She proceeded to say a crisis was impossible for people with sickle cell trait. Clearly she is uneducated as research says it is possible under certain circumstances for people with SCT to experience bone pain/pain crisis. She made me feel crazy for believing the emergency doctor and refused to prescribe me morphine.

When my family stepped in due to her lack of care, and I appointed my sister as the one who would speak for me due to being in so much pain, which I have a right to do, she rudely said to me while in the hospital in pain that she would no longer communicate with my family and accused us of harassing her and the clinic.

Long story short, my pain was CONFIRMED to be related to sickle cell and it was affecting my kidneys which led to 2 back to back hospital admissions. One doctor said I could’ve died if I hadn’t returned to the hospital due to how low my potassium got. If I had stayed under her care and did not have my family “harass” the clinic God knows what would’ve happened to me.

Extremely disappointed in the care I received at Centre Francophone Fairview Clinic from this new doctor.

name: Flemingdon Health Centre - Fairview Community Health
description:
Ridiculous wait time to book in an appointment for a family doctor. Whoever the receptionist for Dr. Nigel fernandopulle thats working on Tuesday October 17th at 11am. You better learn to have some respect my friend. An emergency came up where I have to go the ER because I just cut my hand and I have a business meeting shortly after. I called the doctors and this lady picks up and says “well maybe you can tell me before. There’s other people that I could’ve brought in” I’ve been a business owner for quite some time,
Dr. Fernandopulle, you are only as good as your front line. I can’t wait to meet this receptionist soon.

I have a feeling that many of their problems are due to high volume, but nevertheless, if you're able to travel to get to a better clinic, you're better off doing so unless maybe you have a condition you're able to reliably self-diagnose and just need someone to sign off on a prescription.

The receptionist was god-tier and remained friendly and professional despite always appearing to be doing five things at once. It seems to take a couple of minutes to get her attention unless you're a bit pushy at the counter. When I arrived, she told me today was busy and there would be about a 1.5 hour wait, so she took my health card and I went home to chill until it was time to return. The waiting room has eleven seats which were all filled, and a few people were standing around/outside the door as well - it's quite crowded. I came back an hour later, but based on when I was called in, if I had waited the entire 1.5h, I would have been in good time.

I came in for a suspected first-time yeast infection and was seen by a male doctor. He asked me a few questions, and then excused himself. A female doctor came in a few minutes later and took his place, asking me the same questions plus a few more about my sex life (which I presume the male doctor was too squeamish to ask about), and then wrote me a scrip without asking me whether I had any allergies or, as my pharmacist commented on later, specifying how long to take one of the medications for. At no point were any swabs taken or any physical examination done.

They say you're supposed to go see a doctor the first time you get a yeast infection in case it's something else, but I honestly left the office no more or less certain that I had a yeast infection than when I was just looking stuff up online, and would have preferred to just save three hours of my life by buying the over-the-counter treatments.

The doctors at the walk in clinic are so unprofessional!! The doctor did a quick check up on my son he barely focused looking inside his mouth and decided to prescribe him        antibiotics. They never even bothered to check his temperature!

Most of the reviews are not for Fairview Community Health. This is health clinic with multiple services such as medical care, classes like diabetes education and community engagement.  This is not a walk in clinic.  I have been a patient for years and am very happy with the staff and approach.

We have a very good experience at the centre. They are so helpful especially Dr. Marina Aptekman, Nurse practitioner Katty, receptionist Nicky & Counsellor Sophie. Thanks & wish all them Good Luck.

name: Grand Bend Area Community Health Centre Inc.
description:
This clinic was overall unhelpful. They were unwilling to communicate with my daughter’s medical team in London when there were issues with sample delivery due to weather. The receptionist said “not my fault I’m unwilling to help”

I’m really disappointed in the lack of care, empathy and compassion provided and from now on will make the trek to Goderich just to avoid the entitlement and lack of class at this clinic.

They're pretty useless. I've had to call them for weeks to answer one simple question they keep asking if they can call me back but they never actually do so I have to keep calling them. Now they've wasted a month of my time.

I lost my ADHD medication out of my pocket doing manual labor at work, after much time on this medication and never having lost any medication before in my life I called for some replacement medication to find out my physician was on holidays and the doctor there declined my request for more medication.  I have been a registered patient here for around 20 years, have never given any indication of drug seeking behavior, and always take my medications as prescribed.  I was told I would have to wait seven days until my next scheduled appointment (which is dumb luck that I actually have an appointment).  After some research I have found out that abruptly stopping this medication can result in such side effects as but not limited to extreme fatigue, depression, anxiety, and irritability (two of these symptoms, I am already being treated for).  I feel seriously let down and disheartened and I only hope others have experienced a better level of care.  I write this review feeling very let down as I have worked really hard to make my recovery on my mental health journey.  I have always felt this place had my back 100% until today!

why can you not give blood tests on a walk in basis.
we live here and have to go to London or Sarnia.
why have this medical centre?
if everyone cannot go in as needed?

some do not have the luxury of cars

PLEASE CHANGE YOUR PROTOCOLS.

totally unacceptable

name: Grand Bend Area Community Health Centre – Thedford
description:
This clinic was overall unhelpful. They were unwilling to communicate with my daughter’s medical team in London when there were issues with sample delivery due to weather. The receptionist said “not my fault I’m unwilling to help”

I’m really disappointed in the lack of care, empathy and compassion provided and from now on will make the trek to Goderich just to avoid the entitlement and lack of class at this clinic.

They're pretty useless. I've had to call them for weeks to answer one simple question they keep asking if they can call me back but they never actually do so I have to keep calling them. Now they've wasted a month of my time.
