I was told I would have to wait seven days until my next scheduled appointment (which is dumb luck that I actually have an appointment).  After some research I have found out that abruptly stopping this medication can result in such side effects as but not limited to extreme fatigue, depression, anxiety, and irritability (two of these symptoms, I am already being treated for).  I feel seriously let down and disheartened and I only hope others have experienced a better level of care.  I write this review feeling very let down as I have worked really hard to make my recovery on my mental health journey.  I have always felt this place had my back 100% until today!

why can you not give blood tests on a walk in basis.
we live here and have to go to London or Sarnia.
why have this medical centre?
if everyone cannot go in as needed?

some do not have the luxury of cars

PLEASE CHANGE YOUR PROTOCOLS.

totally unacceptable

name: Hamilton-Niagara Community Health Centre – Hamilton Site
description:
There aren’t enough good words to describe Dr. Danielle Harris. She is an incredible doctor — so thorough, attentive, compassionate, and truly knowledgeable. It’s clear that she genuinely cares about her patients and goes above and beyond to help them. My mom and sister both struggle with ongoing health issues, and having Dr. Harris’s support means more to us than I can express. Knowing we have someone like her in our corner brings us so much comfort and peace of mind. Thank you, Dr. Harris, for the exceptional care you provide — we are so grateful for you!

Great experience every time I take my mother. She enjoys her visits, and the staff, dentist, hygienist, and assistant are all warm and welcoming.

This establishment has the nicest and kindness dentist and hygienist. I’ve never had such treatment from both services that use impeccable equipment and the examination was so thorough like no other. Best dentist and hygienist I’ve ever had. Thank you so much.

Unfortunately, this place is not what it used to be. You need a same day appointment or need to see your family doctor asap? not likely going to happen. Family doctor is always booked up and it's usually days before you can get in to see them. Furthermore, they have no respect or consideration of your time whatsoever as they will cancel last minute, sometimes up to 20 minutes before your appointment.

I LOVE LOVE LOVE LOVE LOVE my doctors and nurses and front desk staff at centre de santé communautaire. all of you  are super awesome and super  amazing people. You go above and beyond for your community. Thank you thank you so much 😊 🥹 I love you all. 😘
10000000000/10

name: Hamilton-Niagara Community Health Centre – Welland Site
description:
Great family place with tons of different family activities. Doctor's office as well as tons of specialist right on site. Tons of activities for kids and young families all free. Great resource for any French family in the community. The even offer free after school child care as well as free hip hop classes and other free events for children during the week nights.

Very helpful and you don't have to speak French.

Community health centre, but only if you speak French lmao

Wonderful service

The healthcare services are excellent, from reception to family doctors and nurses—they provide outstanding service. But when it comes to services for newcomers, for the past four or five years, it's been a complete mess: there are staff members who have absolutely no concern for the service they provide, who give the impression of being there just to produce reports, with no desire to support clients. I truly believe there's a need to restructure these services.

name: Kingston Community Health Centres – Street Health Centre
description:
Streethealth has been there for me in my 5 years through my addiction journey and they continue to support me with my physical and mental health needs. I could not of done it without the amazing nps, reception and staff.
However my momma told me if you don’t have anything nice to say don’t say anything at all. The reason for 4 stars instead of 5 is the nurse practitioner Carol is distasteful, rude, abrasive and doesn’t know how to deal with patients experiencing a mental health crises. If you are having one of the worst days of your life, trust your worst day to Hazel, Alex, Natasha, Cindy… etc. basically anyone but her.
Otherwise, thank you so much for the continued support far and wide. Could not have done it without you guys.

Absolutely amazing people that work and volunteer at street health. We're definately lucky to have them. So thanks to all the staff and volunteers that work really hard to provide their amazing services.

I just want to say that I am very disappointed with my call I made today it took everything in me to pick up the phone and reach out. Im Trying to get help for some of the things that I need. And not only did they tell me as I'm crying that they are too full, but they did not give me any resources to where I could get help or support

I visited here to apply for a vaccination review. The staff member was very kind and clearly explained the process and how I could get my vaccination reviewed. In addition, she wrote down extra information on sticky notes and attached them to my receipt, which was helpful for me as a foreigner to understand the next steps.

I have yet to have a bad experience here. They are all very knowledgeable and getting an appointment is quick and usually convenient. The medical receptionists are great. A few times they've been a little bit late but that's the way healthcare goes.

name: London Intercommunity Health Centre – Huron Site
address: Huron Heights Plaza, 1355 Huron Street, Unit 7
description:
I love this place where you can see your Doctor who listens, I was falling and made a Doctors appointment with my Doctor thanking it might be my diabetes.  Well that wasn't it and after sending me for tests it turned out I has a crushed spinal cord and needed to bump my knee replacement surgery for fear of paralysis due to the crushed spinal cord,  im home and healing, waiting for knee replacement surgery.

Very bad team and unprofessional service by the entire team, they were so racist and disrespectful, i was a client of Dr.Sharma and all i can say is she is of the hardest doctors to deal with she doesn’t care about what the client wants and she’s very unprofessional. depends on my experience there i don’t recommend it at all

I go every Monday for arts and crafts....you can also play cards, board games or knit...just about anything for fun...they have a free snack for everyone with juice, coffee, tea or even hot chocolate sometimes...the first Monday of each month they celebrate that particular month's birthday so if it's January and you have a birthday in that month they/we all sing Happy Birthday to you and a volunteer of the group bakes a little something for everyone to help celebrate with you. we also celebrate the Holidays...we try to make everyone happy and feel welcome...personally I like doing the crafts hehe...how do I rate this place for the groups they provide excellent...there's also a doctors office but I don't go here for personal reasons just to the groups...if you live in the area check us out I'm sure you won't be disappointed :)

I paid $14 for good food box...ripped off not worth $5
Disappointed you have changed..
Won't order it again
Total rip off

I’ve been with them over 30 years. Through out the years, I’ve  met many staff that helped me in many ways. Friendly and supportive people who helped me through my struggles.

name: Parkdale Community Health Centre
description:
I have been a client here for well over a decade and after experiencing more of their services, I thought I should write a review. Firstly, the Parkdale community is very diverse and I really appreciate the level of professionalism and calmness exhibited by the front desk staff at the medical desk. I must give credit to Melissa Abrams especially because not only is she super educated, she truly cares for her clients. I feel lucky to have her support through the health centre and as a member of the 5Ps at the satellite office. It might be a weird thing to say but I get happy before every appointment I attend with her. Seeing a health care professional isn't always a pleasant experience but with her it is. Whether it's good news or not, I feel confident that I will be supported in the right way. The PQWCHC is extremely lucky to have her.

I can’t say enough good things about Parkdale Dental Clinic! They have been incredibly kind, patient, and accommodating while caring for my mother, who has Alzheimer’s. Dr. Ahmad is outstanding—gentle, compassionate, and highly skilled. The reception staff, including Anu and the rest of the team, are always warm and welcoming, making scheduling easy and stress-free. The dental assistants are just as wonderful, ensuring my mom feels comfortable and cared for at every visit. Their kindness and professionalism make all the difference. I highly recommend this clinic to anyone looking for a caring and considerate dental team!

The harm reduction program caters to the Parkdale area and includes a bus service,  satellite workers and safe consumption site. Update, the Safe Consumption Site has closed.

There are so many programs here that the range of expertise plus the location means that the service is a necessary asset to the community. An art show featuring photos is on to reflect needs, challenges and obstacles street involved folks.

Free running shoes event was yesterday.
Just amazing to have a foot check, get shoes and lunch.

Jen the physiotherapist is amazing! She listened carefully to my problems and helped craft a custom regime. She expanded on my concerns and explained how this came to be, and I could not be more happy with her professional support. Keep it up! 💪

This medical clinic has the worst trained, rudest most unhelpful medical reception ever assembled. A phone call for a prescription renewal was  terminated by me because the response to me detailing the two simple medications I required was " you don't have to tell me that because I'm not gonna do anything about it ". In the 4 years I attended this clinic both doctors on my file have vanished overnight with little or no explanation. The only constant being practiced at this clinic is reception staff that present as unhelpful, rude and commicatate a particular distain for anyone that dares ask to book an appointment, get a prescription refill or simply need medical services this month. If your thinking of becoming a patient here - don't its  revolving door for qualified staff and what amounts to a  Carbuncle of festering angry reception staff.

name: Planned Parenthood of Toronto
description:
Suzannah was very lovely and kind. She treated me with compassion. This is the nicest I’ve been treated in the medical community of Toronto thus far. I had difficulty getting any medical people to take me seriously and treat me with compassion. Which I really appreciate and following my assault from 3 weeks ago. Suzannah also gave me my first PAP Smear since I was there at the clinic so that was great. Also, Em who took my blood was kind and gentle. The staff at the front desk were very nice and helpful to me also. They called me back and gave me an appointment as soon as they could because I needed medical care and no one would help me. I really appreciate everything. ❤️

I had the best IUD insertion experience here. Kristin, one of the nurse practitioners performed the procedure. She made me feel very comfortable by explaining the process every step of the way. She offered me a hot water bottle to ease my cramping and she let me keep the window open in order to allow fresh air to come through which helped me a lot. Although the procedure was uncomfortable to say the least, Kristin’s kindness helped make it much more bare able. If you are thinking of getting an IUD I definitely recommend doing it here. It is less than 5 minutes of discomfort for a few years of peace of mind.

Great IUD experience after hearing so many horrible stories. Everyone i spoke to was super kind and helpful. Erin was patent and understanding, and made sure I was comfortable throughout the process.

Came here for an IUD replacement. I had no issues getting in contact or bookings my appointments. Day of I purchased my IUD and Kyle at the front was so friendly and also checked in on me before I left the clinic!

Erin, who do my IUD switch, was incredibly kind and supportive during the whole procedure. She offered numbing but I decided to go without as it is a needle.

She talked me through every step, I was able to ask for breaks, was offered a hot bottle, juice, little towels to cool down, and many other little helpful bits. I was never rushed or made to feel like I was impeding on them.

Really had a great experience myself regardless of my nerves. Thank you a ton!!

My third time visiting here. The receptionists are fairly nice and accommodating. However, NP (I believe) Selam was phenomenal. I went in to get my implant inserted and she did an amazing job comforting me. I was so anxious, I almost considered backing out but she made me feel at ease with the whole process. She talked me through each and every step and assured me there was nothing to worry about,  thank you!

name: Rexdale Community Health Centre – Burnhamthorpe 427 Community Centre
description:
Our family has been seeing Dr. Sediqa Amery for about six months now, and even in this short time, her care has been outstanding. She is patient, kind, and truly attentive to every concern we bring to her.

Dr. Amery takes the time to listen carefully and explain everything clearly, never making us feel rushed. Her calm, reassuring approach gives us a lot of confidence and comfort, especially when we’re anxious about a health issue.

In just half a year, she has already earned our trust through her professionalism, compassion, and dedication. We genuinely feel lucky to have her as our family doctor.

Highly recommended to anyone looking for a warm, knowledgeable, and reliable family physician. Dr. Sediqa Amery is truly wonderful.
Muneeb

Clean atmosphere, but the reception is impolite and quite rude when speaking over the phone. A lot of scheduling gets confused because they’re unclear about where their staff will be working from (on/off site) and though it’s not their responsibility, it would help if they called to remind you about important dates, but instead they excuse it by saying their machine is broken... The staff has proven to be unable to meet my needs and expectations. When complaining about my anemia and asking for help on how to improve my low energy levels or iron deficiency, the doctor responds defensively, “Who doesn’t have anemia?” I don’t want to name her, but I was shocked that my concern was dismissed in this manner and no help was rendered. Given how modernized the facility is, I just wish the service from staff members, including the front desk, was also up to speed, courteous, kind and more accommodating to diverse communities.

I cannot begin to express how happy and grateful I am for having found this health centre. Starting with the friendly and helpful receptionists to the professional, caring and empathetic practitioners, everyone who works here makes you feel so welcome. A special mention to Eden who is amazing and has been like a Godsend for me with her guidance and support. Also Dr. Gibson who is by far the best doctor I have had in years. She goes above and beyond what you would expect from a doctor and has been a tremendous help for me.
I highly recommend this place and their services.

Three years ago, this health center was absolutely a 5 star , with docs like Gibson and Almont ( nurse practitioner) however in recent times , it's not even worth 1 star. Don't get me wrong, there are good doctors here ( I'm sure) but the one I have is extremely dismissive. After complaining of a pain in my face, I was asked ' what can I do about that now mellisa'. I was flabbergasted to think a doctor was dismissing me. It's as if I was the doctor and she was the student. Please, remember as patients advocacy is everything. Advocate for your self. We pay for our health benefits via our taxes so demand the service you need.
NB: I purposely didn't name the doctor on this forum. My intentions are not to insult her but to voice my concern of the below par service.

great and amazing centre for the youth. i am a regular around both youth hubs (Rexdale Community Health Centre, and Rexdale Women’s Centre) and both of them are the best in engaging me into our community with the rest of the youth.

name: Unison Health and Community Services – Bathurst-Finch Community Office
address: 540 Finch Avenue West, Toronto, ON M2R 1N7
description:
I am very disappointed with the recent decline in care at Unison. I previously had an attentive doctor, but since they left, the quality of service has dropped significantly. I have raised several worsening health concerns, but they were dismissed with claims that 'nothing is wrong.'
Despite my requests for diagnostic blood work which should be a basic standard of care the clinic seemed reluctant to order it, citing costs, even though these services are generally covered. Most frustratingly, I have been waiting for a referral and a call back from the manager for months. It is irresponsible to ignore a patient's worsening health and skip necessary documentation for family care. I am still waiting for the manager to fulfill their promise and contact me. I was told I’d need to speak with a manager. Months later, I’m still waiting for that call. If they’re not too busy, then what is it disorganization, neglect?

This experience has left me feeling dismissed and unsupported. I hope this feedback helps improve communication, accountability, and access to care for others.

They gave my appointment to a better  patient. I was recommended by my caseworker that, their service is quick and fast - all I had to do was a TB test for employment. But as a new immigrant not being able to have access to healthcare to its fullest, or a family doctor - having to face issues with settlement and now also having to face discrimination is something else. I didn’t understand why my assessment was passed to the month of April! when I had booked for March, in January. Bad Service! Get your system in order. Understand and acknowledge how people actually ask for help and give them what they want. Don’t  hide your mistakes on people ! it’s your FAULT!

Now, the Hub will be the home of BFFC Community Fridge program. Great services like Social Action Committee, Senior Dental Clinic.

It's one month that I have emailed them and asked them some questions regarding newcomers and immigrants, nd it was kind of much needed information. They haven't got back to me yet, neither acknowledged the receipt of my email. Their web page requires a complex system of questions to reply to be able to email them. So disappointed. Even one star is too much for them.

They have wonderful customer service. The girl on the phone was amazingly understanding about my mom's situation. She gave us an appointment for her dentist. They had a cancelation and offered help for my mom. I think we need to consider them as role model for being responsive, caring, and polite.
I wish them all the best.

name: WellFort Community Health Services (Bramalea Community Health Centre)
address: 40 Finchgate Boulevard Suite 325, Brampton, ON L6T 3J1
description:
I can’t say enough about this place I have been going there since day one. All my needs have always been met in a professional and caring manner. Annie is my regular caregiver she is a very compassionate and lovely women. This is an an amazing place front desk is marvellous as well and I can’t forget Debbie .🥰🥰🥰

My sister had one of the best experience coming to the clinic with the social worker komal. Komal was very polite and patient and explained the whole process of what my day would look like. My sister felt comfortable sharing her problems with Komal. This clinic is the best because not only did my sister get her healthcare, she was also informed of ontario works program, which helped me with me and my sister financially. Komal explained the whole process to helped each step of the way. She even helped my sister with employment and komal even recommended my sister to join language classes. All thanks to Komal my sister has now secured employment and I couldn’t be thankful. This is really one of the best clinic. If you plan to go, make sure you asked to be booked with komal. She has really amazing knowledge on how to help and guide people.

I had one of the most exceptional experiences with my midwife, Suneet Saraw from wellfort community health centre, Brampton.She demonstrated calm professionalism, extensive knowledge, and consistent support, always ensuring that I felt heard and understood. Throughout my pregnancy, she provided clear explanations, addressed all of my concerns with patience, and continually went above and beyond to ensure I felt supported in every aspect—both emotionally and physically.
I am sincerely grateful for the high level of care she provided and wholeheartedly recommend her services to any expecting mother.

This is one of the best health care centres in Canada. The office staff, nurses, nurse practitioners, and doctors are respectful and knowledgeable. They use every resource available to save and help you. Best of all, they welcome everyone. There is no discrimination. No matter your race, you will be accepted and respected.

I have called multiple times in the past week to book a simple phone appointment and have not received any call back yet. Every time I call it always goes to voicemail.

name: Windsor Essex Community Health Centre – Chronic Disease
address: 2885 Lauzon Parkway Unit 107, Windsor, ON N8T 3H5
description:
We found the Diabetes clinic to be well organized and very efficient- hardly any wait times. Kate, the dietitian, was really helpful and provided great guidance. Her recommendations back to our doctor should make a real difference in the diabetes plan.

Went in for a pre-diabetic class. It started on time, and was very informative for me. Karen, the dietitian, explained it in an easy to understand language using examples and props, and answered my questions. I've also received some literature to help me further understand the issue. Free 1 on 1 sessions are available but must book in advance. Something I plan to do in the near future, to stay on top of my health.

Dr. Maple is an angel simple as that

Ive been going to this place for about 11 years off and on... this visit today was terrible... the  nurse  or educator was very rude,  condescending   not supportive at all      dont even feel like going back there!!!!!

The nurses are life savers! Always willing to help you. Can't ask for more to deal with this disease.

name: Windsor Essex Community Health Centre – Sandwich Community Health Centre
description:
I would give this clinic minus 5 stars if that were possible. After LaSalle Police advised me to install video cameras to cut down the mischief of rock and salt throwing at my windows and trespassers armed with knives, two clients of the Windsor-Essex County Health Centre (Sandwich site)—one of whom is a tenant attendant for a Windsor-Essex Community Housing Corporation property—have walked past my cameras over 210 times - 3 times right outside my window during this years 8 day Hannukah celebration. These repeated pass-bys appear deliberate, involving contrived reasons to walk by despite having their own outdoor access and no apparent need to use the route in front of my door. Neither individual is employed in a way that justifies it.

To illustrate the intensity of hatred of this behavior, the wife has repeatedly walked over ice and snow rather than using cleared, safe sidewalks, seemingly to continue passing my residence, demonstrating a disregard for her own personal safety and for the safety and privacy of others. There are also rumours within the building that she has entered other tenants’ units when doors were open. I personally observed her enter a neighbour’s unit once to remove a vacuum cleaner. I can only hope proper consent was given and that master keys were not misused.

Despite the fact that the individuals involved are clients connected to this agency, the Windsor-Essex County Health Centre (Sandwich site) refused to provide me with supportive trauma-related mental health services regarding this situation. Even with a referral from their own social worker to Family Services Windsor-Essex specifically related to this matter, I was outright denied trauma-informed care when I requested it.

This raises serious concerns about community responsibility, safety, accountability, and equal access to trauma-informed services for disabled women. The response demonstrated clear politicking and disregard for my safety and security despite being government funded.

I felt judged and dismissed with my new NP as she extremely downplayed my mental health issues solely because I was "well dressed, on time, and advocating for myself". I am impacted by my health but a stranger is able to determine otherwise within a 15 minute appointment.

However, I will say, social work services here are amazing and have saved my life multiple times. You know, the one I lead impacted by mental health issues. That needed saving multiple times, because of major depression and other mental health issues. But hey, so long as I'm well dressed and on time to my appointments,my severe depression and PTSD don't affect me and everything is fine, according to my NP. Do you just expect me to be late or not show up to an appointment I have to wait literal weeks, sometimes months for?

"Drink water and lose weight, you'll feel better, here's some tylenol" as they say.

Dr State is the is very excellent doctor. If he is unsure of any medical issue he will NOT hesitate to send you where you need to be not many doctors will provide that type of care. I have had many surgeries and he always takes good care of me. He has to travel to get to his office every day. I truly hope we never lose him. It would be a great loss for Windsor.

Absolutely love my nurse practitioner but I am regularly left with a bad taste in my mouth after phone calls from the receptionist. She has an incredibly rude tone of voice and sounds offended when I told her my son no longer required 2 month shots to be done by them as he obtained a pediatrician. She immediately starting insisting he needed to come in for a well baby visit as she had pressured me the first time to bring him there at all, before he even needed his shots and while he was still actively being seen by someone else. She then stated she was going to close his file to which I had to insist that he's not keeping the pediatrician forever and I am still a client. Then she proceeded to doubt that I had his shots done and wanted the pediatricians name and continuously kept sighing. The first time, she had called and said she was aware that I had my son, will he be a patient, and when will you be bringing him in- this was the first week he had been born. I responded that I would contact them to set up an appointment when he is not being followed by the midwife any longer. She called back and told me she was booking me in for that week because he has to come in. I've never felt so much attitude from a receptionist and it constantly prevents me from seeking help from this Centre. I love my nurse practitioner, but hate the process and people I have to deal with in order to see her. The receptionist regularly makes me feel as though she doesn't trust me to provide adequate care for my son, as if I am a child. If you have experiences that are like this, please leave a review so we can make this centre a peaceful and welcoming environment for everybody. Healthcare and life is already stressful, we don't need to feel bad on top of it. Thank you for reading!!

We have been loyal members of this clinic for over 18 years, but our recent experience has been deeply disappointing. My daughter needed blood work and has been anxiously awaiting the results to get some clarity. Unfortunately, her care provider, Lena, decided to withhold the results until the end of September, deeming it "not urgent," despite it being a simple phone call. The only available times were during her school hours, or mid-October at the earliest. "Not urgent" does not equate to "non-actionable." My daughter has been on edge, filled with anxiety, while waiting for answers.

When I inquired about the possibility of another healthcare professional, such as an NP or RN, providing the results sooner, I was met with resistance at every turn by the receptionist. Each suggestion I made was dismissed, to the point where the situation became almost laughable. The receptionist was immediately disagreeable, uncooperative, and unwilling to work with us in any capacity.

This clinic seems to have lost its focus on patient-centered care. I have never had such a poor experience in all these years. Speaking with the healthcare staff felt more like a confrontation than a conversation. Your role is to provide care and support to patients, not to leave them feeling lost and uncertain about their next steps.

name: North of Superior Counselling Programs - Nipigon Administrative Office
address: 16 Front St, PO Box 670, Nipigon, ON P0T 2J0

name: Northern Ontario Counselling Clinic
description:
Valerie is a very knowledgable social worker and gives great advice when it comes to co-parenting.

I life changing experience for me. I felt like I was being heard and it helped me through a dark time. I definitely recommend this Clinic

name: Community Mental Health Centre (Scarborough Health Network)
description:
Avoid Dr. Behzadi all costs,  extremely dismissive, lacks empathy, compassion for his patients, extremely dismissive and caused more harm than good. It's a shame people like him who lack empathy and compassion are so called psychiatrists. He needs help himself!

Dr. Nguyen is the best - he should win an award for his outstanding abilities as a Doctor. The front desk staff and the nurses are all excellent too. What a great team of people!

5/5! I've had a very great experience with this place. Doctor is top notch and very accomodating (Dr. Ngyuen;) - very understanding; nurse is always polite and professional. I get a needle here every 3 months now because i've been doing so well (used to be every month). I've never had an issue. But I have noticed that you need to schedule your appointments ahead of time with the doctors and nurses and so long as you make your appointments you shouldn't have an issue at all. In all the years I've been going there, I don't remember ever having to reschedule or having an issue. That having been said I couldn't get somebody on the phone during covid-19 recently; nor could I leave a voicemail (voicemail no longer exists for some reason); The phone just keeps ringing; And also I couldn't email them a question. I had to physically go to the location and talk to the receptionist and book a phone call with the doctor, which might take weeks to hear from because they're very busy. So if nobody is picking up the phone dont even bother just pop in one day and talk to the front desk. That's the only issue i've ever had with this place. But considering that everything is covered (including my 3,500$~ needle every 3 months) i can't complain at all. Thank you to Ontario and Canada for this place. Words cant express my gratitude. 5/5! WE'RE BLESSED!

I appreciate finally getting meds . Bit other people kept walking in during appointment . I found that once again unprofessional . Middle.of.appointment Asian lady walks in On appointment and doctor leaves with her . It took a year to get help and meds . It only tool 8 cancelled appointments that were cancelled . Not impressed . The lettrr to my doctor was different from what she told me . She was suppose to up zoloft she up trazadone and recommended heart test . Just trazadone.was.mentioned  in letter . Getting mental help in this city well begged for it cant get help .

Visits with family member always handled well. Reasonable size waiting room based on services. Dr Sze and staff are always helpful.

name: ACT - AIDS Committee of Toronto - Gay Men's Health Programs
address: 543 Yonge St, 4th Fl, Toronto, ON M4Y 1Y5
description:
I love this place ,very interested when I'm involved with the community.

Hit and miss whether they get back to you or not so don’t hesitate to follow up because you can fall through the cracks. They tend to be bureaucratic (they are government funded so they have numerical targets to preserve that funding) and where I can I get services or advice from other organizations first rather than negotiate the ACT maze. Some decent people work here though; as with any organization, it helps to cultivate relationships with them and seek their guidance.

I was there this morning.
The girl at the front desk was very kind. She suggested to me a couple places to go to continue a treatment.

I'm so happy to meet people who cares about others.

I'm thankful.

Very welcoming team , they offer drinks say water or coffee and offer you a seat then they will attend to you with smiles 😻

Attended their Peach workshop. They are such an awesome and friendly staff. Very lovely and supportive organization.

name: Afghan Women's Organization Refugee and Immigrant Services - Toronto - Don Mills Rd
address: 747 Don Mills Rd Unit 200, Toronto, ON M3C 1T2
description:
Review For Maryam Latif Ali At Afghan Women Organization I’d like to share a heartfelt review about Maryam Latif Ali, who works at the Afghan Women Organization. Maryam is an incredibly dedicated and hardworking woman who has earned the respect and gratitude of so many clients. Not only I, but really every Afghan in Toronto who knows her appreciates how kind and supportive she is. She’s always there to help newcomers, especially Afghan women who have come from challenging backgrounds, and she does so with such warmth and grace.

Maryam’s work is truly outstanding, and she stands out as someone who is deeply committed to the well-being of her community. We all value her support and feel lucky to have her. In short, she’s an exceptional individual whose dedication shines through in everything she does.Thanks

Maryam Ali latif is the best employee, great supporter and a excelent worker. Since we came to Canada we received help from her. She is always helpful and very kind. She helped us with PR applicatin, OESP, housing and school registration. She is very profitional and i always recomend her.
Wishing her all the best and good wishes for AWO and Maryam jan.

I would like to thank Maryam Ali Latif for their support with my application. They were very knowledgeable, patient, and supportive throughout the process. Highly recommend their help.

Absolutely incredible organization!
I can’t say enough good things about this Afghan women’s organization. From the moment I walked in, I was welcomed with warmth, professionalism, and a deep understanding of the challenges I was facing.They offer such a wide range of services.
Each staff member I’ve interacted with has been kind, patient, and extremely knowledgeable. They really take the time to listen and make sure you're getting the help you need.
If you or someone you know is new to Canada or just needs support, this is the place to go. I’m so grateful this organization exists.
Thank you for all the amazing work you do Mrs. Malalai Jamshid Nejabi.

I had the absolute pleasure of working with Baran on my citizenship application. She was exceptionally kind, supportive, and detail-oriented, going above and beyond to follow up until I completed my CELPIP for the exam. Her patience in answering all my questions and her unwavering support made the entire process smooth and stress-free. I am deeply grateful for her help and highly recommend her.

name: Barbra Schlifer Commemorative Clinic, The - Group Counselling Program
description:
This clinic, particularly Roga Robaee and Cheryl Scotland, offered invaluable assistance throughout my divorce. They provided me with resources to help me manage the situation and to locate a lawyer and housing after I lost my private apartment as a result of the divorce. It's been a challenging year, but I am grateful for the progress I've made, largely due to the support from this clinic. I no longer require their services since they have successfully fulfilled their role in my journey.

Thank you!🙏🏻

One of the worst examples of the non profit industrial complex in Toronto. They aren't here to help you. You won't receive counseling. You won't receive any kind of protection from a stalker. You won't be able to file historical abuse charges. They don't help adult survivors of incest or survivors of domestic assault. It is unclear what they do other than intakes that lead nowhere. Perhaps if you've got a winning case on your hands that is easilly probable they might take the case to help increase their own funding.

My sister had a counselor that left and no one reached out to continue services.

My sister has severe complex PTSD and while her counselor Nickollete was wonderful in counselling, the clinic didn’t  follow up to help my sis.

My sister also mentioned a counselor Stefanie leaving and honestly it’s sad the clinic lets wonderful counselors go and don’t not provide follow up for clients.

Disappointing.

I was very disappointed with my experience at the Barbra Schlifer Commemorative Clinic( 489 college St. Suite 503. Toronto. On) . Their flyer and mission statement sounded heart-warming and supportive, but the reality was different. I was asked for an overwhelming amount of very personal information — full name, date of birth, home address, my partner’s information, and more. After nearly 40 minutes of answering questions, I was told they could not help me.

This left me feeling frustrated and misled. It felt invasive and like a waste of time. I had expected real support, not to give up so much private information only to be turned away.

I truly hope they review their intake process and consider the emotional impact it has on people who come to them seeking help.

I am desperate and begging this clinic for help again. I need a roof over my head and protection from the people controlling my finances and the amount of food I’m allowed to eat. I am begging you for help. Please help me.

name: Baycrest - Baycrest Hospital - Clinical Services - Ambulatory Mental Health Clinic
description:
Getting approved for external care (physio, OT) is cumbersome and takes months... Yet the care provided in-house isn't consistent and not well organized and seems to inhibit progress in health condition. Our family feels that our family member would have made some more progress if it weren't for the delay in approval for the external providers and it's something to really consider when considering Baycrest for your family member. Though a lot of the nurses are wonderful and truly care.

Has improved a lot.  My mom has been here for 2 rehab stays.  One 4 years ago.  One recently.  She says it has improved a lot!  Her recent stay was a great, very pleasant experience.  No complaints on food.  Room is shared but spacious and clean.  Staff, specialist, doctors are all very friendly and helpful.  Thank you everyone at Baycrest for this.  Much appreciated

My mother is on 4th floor of the  apotex
Maybe because this floor cares for those with dementia
they don’t feel the need to have a warm presentation.
It’s dirty, neglected and short of staff.
Dirty floor a dusty old aquarium and what appears feces
on the cabinet.

My grandfather has been here after a long visit in the hospital and his condition has improved and his spirits. It’s a nicer facility than a hospital and the staff are friend.

Easy to reach with public transport. The hospital is quite big with multiple buildings. Each building encapsulating its own properties. I have been working here as a research student at Rotman Research Institute. I found all the people to be very helpful. Walking in the hostel and seeing happy faces of old people is what makes the aura awesome.

name: And Mental Health Program (JAMH) - Toronto Region And Provincial Office
address: 300-95 King St E, Toronto, ON M5C 1G4
description:
I participated in the RIA 2022 Fall session and found the sessions to be fun and intelligent, the facilitators are caring and attentive. The program still makes sense to me today and I'm glad to have met everyone involved.

I participated in a set of sessions for family caregivers and found the group to be helpful and insightful.

name: LAMP Community Health Centre - Among Friends
address: 1-2788 Lake Shore Blvd W, Etobicoke, ON M8V 1H5
description:
This is one of the best community and health centers I have ever been to, it's  always been a good experience here as the people working here have a lot of compaction and empathy to there clients and the local people from the Lakeshore area. The receptionist and all staff here try to help and direct you to the right department and other services offered. I have been a client here for over 16 years and have always had a good experience as my Dr is located here, they have several doctors and nurses to help there clients and there health care options.  I love this community center because they have so much to offer,  such as tax services free of charge depending on your income and legal services as well. There is a housing department to help people find a good place to live with a limited income. This building also has a diabetes clinic, a foot care clinic cutting toe nails and foot care taking  of and there is also a dental care center for low income under 16 years old or over 65 years old not to forget the daycare center and many other services offered by them. This is just part of the reason I give this place 5 star's plus. God bless all the great personal there  please stay safe and thank you for all your help and kindness.  If you like my review please leave a positive thumbs up and positive feedback and please also check out my other reviews and photos so stay safe and God bless 🙌. And by the way New Toronto fish and chips is just around the corner 😊❤.

Visiting LAMP Community Health Centre at St. Margaret's can be a bit of an adventure, especially if you're not used to the area. The surroundings might seem a bit intimidating, but once you step inside, the warm and welcoming atmosphere quickly puts you at ease. However, there's this one fire hydrant outside that seems to have seen better days—it's like it's been a magnet for too many visitors, if you know what I mean. On some days, the area can get pretty lively, with some enthusiastic folks screaming out their frustrations or just generally being loud. Despite these quirks, LAMP itself offers a fantastic range of services, from healthcare to community programs, making it a vital resource for the community.

I might return to revise my review. I didn't get a chance to enjoy the supports since I was ushered outta there quick fast.

Came here for newborn hearing test. Was treated well by staff, and I’m grateful the technician conducting the test allowed me to breastfeed my son during the test to help keep him relaxed. Plenty of parking available on site, which is a bonus!

Becareful going to lamp community centre, there is a nurse that breaking confidentiality to give out medical record ,This nurse been going around giving out my daughter medical record to her people. Yet she still employed .

Very friendly  and helpful staff.   Always accommodating and welcoming.  Love the programs which are very beneficial mentally and physically.

name: LOFT Community Services - 416 Drop-In Centre
address: 416 Dundas St E, Toronto, ON M5A 2A5
description:
They offer help to one of the most disadvantaged sectors of society. Offering so much to those with so little. A great drop in food program, companionship for the homeless with severe mental health issues. The ones no one else will help. Kudos to you all.

Amazing lunches cooked there!! They have a food bank plus other resources available the staff are friendly, work hard when preparing and serving meals!! Thank you all there! I appreciate you so much just to let you know!!

This place is very welcoming to women who fit their criteria of the type of women they help the most. And it seems the things this place offers to women who fit their criteria is as it is advertised.  So from the impression of that I would give this place 3 stars out of 5 stars although it may actually be 4 stars out of 5.  However, if you do not fit the criteria of their place  for you to be able to use it, you would think they would be kind enough to direct you somewhere else you can go that would be more fitting but they are not like that.  I don't know why.  You just get unexpectedly snubbed.  Maybe their staff gets too much involved in their own surroundings at their workplace they forget other people's existence and the needs of others who are in poverty.  So for not communicating appropriately to people that are not of their criteria, and for not willing to direct people that do not fit their criteria somewhere else they can go that would for sure be of assistance since they can't,  I give this place 0 stars out of 5 stars for that.  I think they really need to improve on this bit that may not seem important because it does not look good for them to help their people but be rude to people that are not of their criteria.

WENT TO VISIT WITH DONNA ELLIS SHE IS KNOW A LOFT/Support Worker 9 years tell retirement
Donna has not changed she still remembers me 30 years later
She always cooked the meat sauce that I could eat
She was only sick once when council fire bought us canned mushrooms soup that was riotous We all had food illness after that M.Joy.Reid decided to get food supplies in cans from other manufacturers
I missed the people that are gone especially  Scottie and Kelly and Carol Shannon...

People here r very helpful n kind with good useful programs, my personal favorite lunch which is always well presented healthy n flavorful. I come here as often as I can as it's usually quite chill

name: LOFT Community Services - Wilkinson Housing And Support Services - Case Management
description:
LOFT is a beautiful and amazing service ❤️! I want to send my special thanks to Brittney and Yassi who helped my mom and I with taking care of us and my dad who was in the hospital. Their guidance and consoling during this time is greatly appreciated!

Thank you so much for great care on my husband  I send my sincere appreciation special to YASSI and BRITTANY can't thank enough for excellent care and support.

Promised so much...still waiting for anything to happen, Big disappointment, so very sad for all those in Need.... Sad and let down. Yet another "non for profit" ..

Great people helping a great cause

Offers great service for those in need

name: Michael Garron Hospital - Consultation, Assessment and Time Limited Therapy (CATT)
description:
D.Gazala and his team were fantastic with explaining, discussing anr performing my Hiatal Hernia surgery. Every part of my recovery has gone precisely as I was told it would. The PSW’s helped in so many was and the nursing staff was second to none, especially Mark he always maintained a high level of care while being professional and informative. Thank’s to everyone that made this go smoothly regards
Don Thurston

I took my daughter to Michael Garron Hospital today for a pediatric emergency visit and the experience set a new standard.

From intake to registration, triage, the doctor consult, imaging, nursing care, and transport to and from X-ray, every step felt calm, respectful, and kind. Each staff member showed warmth and professionalism. Communication stayed clear throughout the visit.

This visit was pre-booked online for pediatric emergency imaging. The appointment time was 3:30. Arrival was 30 minutes early as requested. By 4:00, we were back in the car heading home.

The resident took time to explain the imaging results and the treatment plan in plain language. Transitions between teams felt seamless. No confusion. No unnecessary waiting.

The pediatric emergency team delivered an outstanding experience for both child and parent. Gratitude for care delivered with efficiency, humanity, and genuine warmth.

I cannot praise the team at Micheal Garron enough. From the procedure room to the recovery ward, the nurses and doctors were attentive, compassionate, and professional. The facility was pristine, and they communicated every step of my treatment clearly. The follow-up care was equally excellent, ensuring I was comfortable and recovering well. Truly a 5-star experience during a difficult time."

Men's Washroom (accessible) opposite Pharmasave, main floor.
In the photo is the handicapped stall note the metal plate on the floor which catches small wheels on a wheelchair.  The Vertical grab bar is just visible (far right side of toilet), is not reachable with the right hand, when attempting to stand from a wheelchair.  There is no other horizontal bar on the left hand side to assist.  Difficult to either sit or Stand
The picture showing the sinks has the soap at one end, the sinks in the middle and the towel dispenser at the other end.   Proper hand washing requires water, then soap, then rinse off, then dry.  The layout does not support read ready flow for this activity.   Towels not readily reachable from a wheelchair, due to bin size.

Nurse Peggy and Nurse Paige were absolutely amazing. My wife had a very smooth delivery of our child. They were very caring and passionate about their jobs. They deserve a raise! ♥️ We will be forever grateful to their child birth team.

name: Parkdale Activity - Recreation Centre (PARC), The - Parc Outreach Program: Case Management Support And Services
address: 1499 Queen St W, Toronto, ON M6R 1A3
description:
The staff there manages my mom's money and are suppose to be helping her.. Instead, I have made numerous emails about my mom and not one concerned person can email me back..Made numerous phone calls, nobody can contact me back.. My mom left my father for a Korean Popstar she met online, he took all her money hence why she has them managing it.. but whatever money she has left over from them, she sends to the boyfriend in Korea.. I have recently moved in with her and realised how far she was gone.. She is telling people I abused her and other things that can seriously put my life in harm and no one cares.. the boyfriend has her believing he can't come to Canada because I called the Canadian Embassy and put a stop to it, she told people she will put me in jail for a long time if she finds out it was me.. hence again why she is making up these bizarre lies.. anyone that knows me knows I would never put my hands on my mom nor emotionally abuse her.. I just want someone to call me back so I can actually talk to someone

The Parc saved me lots of times, I wouldnt have eaten if it wasnt for the parc.
Im a pensioner who lives in the area they also helped me with advice and I wouldnt have been able to keep my housing if it wasnt for the the Parc referrals.  5 Stars absolutely.

The community centre is welcoming and inviting, and the staff teams are courteous and kind. Always show concern and provide a safe haven for those in need. Bailey and Eric, my housing support staff team, are incredibly kind and helpful, and they communicate constantly. Nice area, great location.

I live in their housing and go to the drop in. The housing, case management, and drop in workers are always there to help and understand. I have also made great friendships and established a supportive group of people in my life here. I ❤️ PARC.

Thanks Patrick To You And Your CO-Worker For Food And A Warm And Subway Token! Shower!!!

name: Sinai Health System - Mount Sinai Hospital - Mental Health
description:
I had my baby here. Extremely good obgyns, doctors and nurses. I felt very supported and listened to my entire pregnancy. Instead of going to ER they have a separate pregnancy ward and they always support and take care of you no matter what scare you may be having. I stayed for 5 days for my entire induction and delivery and everything. I am grateful to everyone.

We just had our baby at this hospital, and the experience was truly outstanding. We don’t have a single complaint—on the contrary, we want to congratulate the entire staff.

From the moment my wife was admitted, the care was more than kind. The nurses were extremely attentive and thoughtful, all the way through delivery. We had multiple doctors and nurses supporting us during labor, as well as a team of specialists ready for when our baby was born. Everything went perfectly.

Our delivery room was very clean and fully equipped to keep us comfortable. After our baby was born, we were moved to a semi-private room, and everything was organized and in perfect condition. The instructions were very clear, and they provide excellent information to take home—especially helpful for first-time parents.

For new parents, the parking in front of the hospital on Murray Street is very convenient, and the food area on the ground floor makes it easy to grab something to eat during those waiting hours.

100/100 in every aspect. Congratulations, and keep up the great work! In the end, it truly feels like the team working there genuinely enjoys what they do—and that makes all the difference.

I absolutely loved my entire birthing experience at Mount Sinai Toronto. From my OB prenatal appointments with Dr. John Snelgrove to the delivery and postpartum care, everything was beyond amazing. They didn't send me home as my water-broke. The nurses and staff were so warm, kind, and genuinely caring — it truly felt like everyone was invested in making sure both me and my baby were doing well. Nothing ever felt rushed or delayed; everything happened so naturally, at the right time, with the right experts guiding us.

They not only took care of all the postpartum essentials me and my baby but also made sure we learned so much during the postpartum phase. The number of specialists who checked in and the level of attention we received were simply incredible. I’m so grateful to every doctor, nurse, and staff member who made this experience so positive, comforting, and memorable. It’s something I’ll cherish forever. ❤️

I had fever for several days and I had to go to the emergency room. Everyone I met was really nice. The lady with short curly hair at No.3 registration window was carrying and friendly. The doctor I met Dr. Irene Ying, she was also professional and caring and patient！

On October the 2, 2025, I  visited the Oral and Maxillofacial Surgery Department at Mount Sinai Hospital for an examination of a large radiolucent lesion on my left ramus. I was referred by Dr. Justine Chan and Dr. Kevin Gavet.

From registration to consultation, every step of my visit was handled with professionalism and care. The registration and customer service staff were welcoming and efficient, and the nurse who escorted me was exceptionally kind.

I was seen by Dr. Karl Cuddy and two other specialists who took the time to explain my condition, discuss treatment options, and ensured I was comfortable throughout the biopsy procedure. Their attention to detail, clear communication, and genuine concern for my well-being were outstanding. The specialists carefully took a biopsy sample, which was sent to pathology for further analysis. The local anesthesia was effective, and I experienced no discomfort during or after the procedure.

Before I left, the team arranged a CT scan and scheduled a follow-up appointment for me to return once the pathology results are available.

Overall, my experience at Mount Sinai was excellent. The specialists, nurses, registration, and customer service teams were all professional, compassionate, and thorough. I highly recommend Mount Sinai’s Oral and Maxillofacial Surgery Department to anyone requiring specialized dental or oral care.

name: Springboard Clinic
description:
I had an assessment here but the final report has been delayed far beyond what is reasonable. I contacted the clinic multiple times by phone and email, but staff did not provide updates or take responsibility. They acted as if nothing was wrong and gave no solution. This lack of communication and accountability is very unprofessional, especially when clients rely on timely reports for important needs.

My son and I had an exceptional experience with Springboard, and I can't recommend them highly enough! From the moment we started the process, the administrative staff was incredibly professional, efficient, and effective in communicating every step of the way. They clearly outlined what needed to be completed prior to the assessment, and there was immediate follow-up on the same day the assessment was completed, which made the entire process seamless.

The psychologist Dr Anne Bailey and pediatrician Dr. Yasmin Kazemi we met with were outstanding. They were not only highly professional but also personable, creating a warm and comfortable environment for us to share information. My 17-year-old son felt incredibly at ease, and it was clear that both the psychologist and pediatrician took the time to understand his struggles deeply.

What truly amazed us was their ability to pinpoint the areas where our son was facing challenges and to offer incredible, actionable strategies for moving forward.  Springboard truly exceeded all of our expectations, and we are so grateful for their expertise and care.

I would not recommend Springboard. I took both my children there, paid a lot of money, with poor to no results. I found both the psychiatrists that my kids worked with to be  odd, with poor communication skills and little insight. The medical doctor was very knowledgeable on ADHD drugs and we followed her advice. But the drugs prescribed had many adverse side effects and did more harm than good. We had our son signed up for “coaching” services. He dreaded these appointments and they had no effect. My advice to parents and children with ADHD is to do your research and if you use a service such as Springboard, critically analyze everything they do. Their motive is to have a successful business! And yours is a better quality of life for your loved one with ADHD. You can do better than Springboard provides with learning about ADHD and the variety of treatment and strategies. And save yourself a ton of money in the process.

I had an outstanding experience at Springboard Clinic! The staff was incredibly welcoming and attentive. Their response time was amazing, and they demonstrated so much patience with me throughout the process. I felt truly cared for and supported. Highly recommend Springboard Clinic for anyone seeking exceptional care and service! 🌟

Their one-day ADHD assessment is an incredible service! They do the tests and interviews in the morning and I had the results by the afternoon. I had been waiting for an ADHD assessment with another clinic which was going to be 4 or 5 appointments over several weeks (not to mention the 4 or 5 month waitlist for the appointments in the first place). I was very happy to hear about Springboard's one-day assessment.

Their care team is also great, with the other clinic I could never get a hold of anyone - phone calls were not returned and e-mails took days to be replied to. Springboard's team was readily available and texting with me frequently, answering any question I had.

name: St James Town - Toronto - 200 Wellesley St E - The Corner @ 200
description:
Community corner offering services to St. Jamestown community. Staff here are very friendly and helped me try and get vaccines early on in the Covid pandemic.

promised a letter of attendance then failed to provide it along with the sign in sheet.

Management refuses to take security threats/risks seriously despite knowing about them, and having a daycare in the building. Security issues have been ongoing for at least a year, and steps taken by management to solve/mitigate these issues have been subpar. Although this community center provides valuable resources, the lack of motivation shown by management in identifying impactful solutions to keep people safe is concerning. Although they've hired on site-security guards, they are severely understaffed for the type and amount of safety issues they face (for example: people showing up with weapons and people distributing narcotics in the building).

A while ago , I was in need of emotional support . Life is very tough sometimes . I was lucky enough , very lucky to be introduced to a social worker who helped me immensely . While I'm in a better place now , this would not have been possible without the help of a TCHC staff member and a great RSW at the community corner .
The services made available by the good people of the " Corner " is a real resource for us all.

My appointment with a lady ran short of time & I was asked to visit again after a week, which lengthened my process of getting a Work Permit in Canada. But every single one of them in the office was welcoming & friendly.

name: Strides Toronto - Community Counselling
description:
Strides Toronto is located inside of 100 Consilium Place (in the very big and tall building). Strides Toronto doesn't have a sign outside of their unit, which is unit 102. You have to walk past the elevators, past the first glass wall/door area, past the second glass wall/door area, and then turn right. The opaque double doors you see at the end of the hall is where Strides Toronto unit 102 is located.

On the doors of the organization, you'll see song posters about how to keep yourself safe from COVID-19 and the flu, as well as posters advertising their "What's Up Walk In" brief mental health counseling sessions that they have. On some of these posters, you'll see the Strides Toronto logo on it, meaning, you've come to the right place.

Inside, you'll find a table with various informational posters about all sorts of services and programs that they have to offer. You'll also see a open space format and kind of wide/big ish waiting area (the seats are colorful and there's some crayons/colored pencils and paper on the low table). There are more wide bench like seats down the hall. The lights above are in the shape of a capital "T".

On the receptionist counter, there are plenty of informational posters as well. Including the current event calendar (April). There also seems to be what looks like a shuttle bus schedule that goes from STC (Scarborough Town Centre) to 100 Consilium Place. There's a "The Zone Guidelines" poster (I assume rules in place for an area within the unit).

We came here today to ask some questions regarding the services that they have. We had previously talked to someone over the phone who worked at Strides Toronto who claimed that there was a walk-in CBT session on certain days of the week for people aged 18 to 29 (and something being specifically geared towards Autistic people).

The receptionists told us that they didn't have that kind of service here at this location... Or at any of their other locations. What they did have was a CBT service for 18 years old and under.

They looked up all sorts of information on their computers and told us that most of their services are aimed towards people under 18 or to 21 years old. None of them are specifically geared towards Autistic people at all. It's in general for everyone.

They said we could look up the Strides Toronto website, and check out the Autism page and see what services/programs are available for adults. It seems like there are services at the Galloway location. However the services may not be the right ones were looking for.

Strides Toronto has no services/programs related to job hunting/searching. What they do have is a lot of mental health programs, youth programs (don't know the age range), young women programs, etc.

Overall, they have services for people under 18. If you're over 18... There are some services. If you're Autistic and looking for Autism type services, you'll likely not really find the ones you're looking for (or you could, depends on your situation). For us though, it's another dead end.

I’ve used their speech and language services for my child for over a year. The assessments and online sessions were fine, but the in-person therapy was disappointing. The therapist struggled to divide her attention between multiple children, and the room was crowded and distracting. Too many people were allowed in, leaving my child overwhelmed despite doing well in one-on-one sessions. This may be typical for free services, but it offered little support for our family. There was also inconsistency, we were told off for being late, while another family wasn’t addressed for missing sessions. It didn’t feel like the neutral environment we expected. On the positive side, only the receptionist and facility were organized, friendly, and helpful.

Had an excellent session with Leigh after a terrible one somewhere else many years ago, and I am looking forward to my next one here. This is an excellent free service that unfortunately is not advertised well but would help so many people in Scarborough. Make sure to show up as early as you can because I arrived at 5:50 and got the last appointment of the day at 6:45.

I went there to have a walk-in sessions to release my negative bottled up thoughts. It was a massive mental relief to let it all go and feel better about my self. The person, Nicole, helped me with my issue was friendly and understanding. She helped me to overcome my personal issue with some short activities then a list on how to keep positively and keeping my confidence up.

I am very happy to have a free walk-in counselling service available to young people (up to 30) in Scarborough. This service is an asset in our community. We all deserve to feel heard, valued, and respected.

name: Strides Toronto - What's Up Walk-in Clinic - In-person and virtual sessions
phone: Call 416-438-3697, press 1 and then enter ext 12230 for reception.

name: Sunnybrook Health Sciences Centre - Sunpact (SUNNYBROOK PROGRAM OF ASSERTIVE COMMUNITY TREATMENT)
description:
My partner was referred by her doctor to see a pyschologist and after spending months on the waitlist she was sent home in under 45 minutes being told they could not help her. Her appt was to be from 9a-11a. We left the hospital at 935a as the pyschologist said they only handle certain anxieties and her anxiety wasn't one that could be helped. This office should do a better job of reviewing referrals and know from before hand if they can help the referred individuals. This was a complete waste of time and money. Dr. Renato was not helpful at all.

name: Unison Health and Community Services - Unison Health & Community Services - Youth Clinic
description:
They're amazing, since I started to come there I don't have nothing bad to say. Really it's the opposite, when I need a doctor appointment usually in that week I get it, if not on the same day. Everyone is super nice and friendly

If I could give zero stars I would. Don’t even bother to call.
Called to ask about booking appointments and they said they don’t take new patients anymore.
I said okay, that’s understandable but are there any other resources or clinics that you can direct me to.
She said you can call back later in the month to book appointments for February.
Okay, so you’re telling me that this clinic that is supposed to help people with no OHIP or private insurance is just not taking any patients? Because they are short on staff?
This person who picked up the phone has absolutely no empathy and doesn’t seem like is trained to do jobs like this.

I said but if I can only see a doctor at February I don’t know if I’ll even be alive.
The lady just says, okay thank you for calling. Goodbye.

🤣🤣🤣 this is how a community service health center talks to people with mental illness? What a way to make sure they do kill themselves. Why even operate this health center at all if it isn’t serving and helping the community?

They said they offer trans heathcare but the intake process had me say/hear my deadname 7 times and my real name only 2. After all that I asked for a single appointment to fill my requirements for GCS. They said this is not possible and they can only replace my family doctor. If you're looking for GCS authorization here (a central part of trans healthcare), this is a dead end. Go elsewhere.

I have recently been very surprised with the service here. I called in having a terrible cough, the result of a cold or viral infection I am still not sure. I usually have Dr. Gao as a doctor, and she has been very precise which is great, but she is now on maternity leave.

I work everyday, and have no more sick days, so I'd been working although my lungs were very wheezy and I was struggling to breathe normally. Throw in the fact that I was coughing up large amounts of other stuff. I asked for a chest x-ray (req) so I can rule out anything that was going to hospitalize me, and also still be able to get to work. They told me I needed to be seen in person and would not issue me the req, regardless of the fact that I could not get across the city to them.

I was very confused by this, as it wasn't helpful as I could not get there, and it was extremely obvious that I was quite sick and most definitely sounded that way. Either way they insisted, I needed to be seen in person, and offered me a list of places closer to me that I could attend.

I went to the walk-in nearest to me (two days later, which was the soonest I could get there with work.) At that point they refused to see me in person, after they saw and heard my symptoms.

They immediately sent me home and asked me to wait for a phone call (to which they immediately ordered a chest x-ray).

This is absolutely baffling to me? I know that doctors have different styles, but my own family doctor should have my best interest in mind, and insisted I needed to be seen in person for a requisition.

When I spoke to the walk in doctor on the phone she was equally as baffled. Based on my symptoms I was told I should not be seeing any doctor in person, and something as simple as such shouldn't be an issue?

This definitely made me feel unsupported by this clinic, I will be attempting to find a new one if Dr. Gao does not return soon 😓

The dr, who i see at the sister clinic, refused to write me a medical note from this clinic. Even though she was there, while she is only at "my" clinic 2 days a week.
I'm going to lose 25% of my final grade of a class because of Gillian Graham. Terrible terrible healthcare service.

name: Unity Health Toronto - St Michael's Hospital - Mental Health and Addictions Services - Community Outreach Services - CATCH Homeless
address: 30 Bond St, Cardinal Carter Wing, 17th Fl
description:
I delivered my baby via c‑section at St. Michael’s and had the most incredible, positive experience from start to finish. Every single person I encountered, from the triage nurse to the L&D team, including Ashley and Teresa, as well as the medical staff — showed such genuine compassion, patience, and kindness. They created an environment where I felt supported, respected, and truly cared for.
I was in labour for nearly 44 hours, originally planning for a vaginal delivery before ultimately needing an emergency c‑section. Throughout that long and emotional journey, the team made sure I was as comfortable as possible and never left me feeling uncertain or alone. They kept me informed at every stage, honoured my birth plan wherever they could, and approached every decision with empathy and professionalism.
The anesthesiologist and OBGYN were exceptional — calm, reassuring, and thorough in explaining each step, which made a world of difference during such a vulnerable moment.
I’m deeply grateful to the entire team for their dedication and the outstanding care they provided. You are all so appreciated, more than words can express.

Their Diabetes Clinic is excellent. Otherwise, my experience in Ultrasound, Nephrology, and Urology is that they do not respect patients. We are human, not objects. During 2 different procedures on the same day, one while mostly unclothed, other people walked in and out of the room. One came in and left the door open as I was fully exposed trying to get off the gurney. In addition, some staff are very judgmental about our personal problems. Others are rude and unhelpful, not even bothered to write complete sentences in emails or provide contact info with referrals. They don't even say hello or make eye contact when taking blood pressure!

The emergency department is hit or miss. I had a cyst removed and they were very nice, but did not do the follow-up to arrange home care. I called back as I had a hole in my back stuffed with gauze that hadn't been changed in 5 days, so was told to come back. Then, even after repeating this info, the triage nurse gave me serious attitude for coming there and not a walk-in clinic. I was told to come there!

Their Patient Relations did an "investigation." They couldn't figure out who was walking in and out, meaning the doctor and nurse did not know either. Who were these strange people entering during medical procedures? But the managers will remind them, because sometimes the staff forget about privacy. That should be instinctive. I get comprehensive and deliberate privacy from RMTs, so hospital staff can do the same.

Finally, on the way out, they have the nerve to have young people accost you for donations! They should have senior staff stand there so they can have real discussions, because the lack of respect is systemic despite their many signs telling patients to be respectful.

Whatever happens to me in the future does not matter. What truly matters is that, five days after my open-heart surgery, I can say with full confidence that I was cared for by some of the coolest, most skilled surgeons and the kindest, most compassionate nurses on earth.

This hospital is truly iconic. The level of professionalism, humanity, and dedication I experienced here goes far beyond expectations. You should be immensely proud—this hospital stands as a true Canadian symbol of care, compassion, and help.

I will always be grateful for the treatment and respect I received here.

I really wish I could put zero stars and not give this hospital a single star. However, I am writing this as the daughter of a disabled patient, Daniel Cubias, who was recently subjected to appalling treatment at St. Michael's Hospital.

My father was physically assaulted by security personnel while in a vulnerable state. Despite being a patient under the hospital's care, he was forcibly pulled out of a bathroom after only five minutes based on a baseless and discriminatory assumption of drug use. To make matters worse, security used physical force against him simply for being loud on a phone call—a noise issue that should have been handled with de-escalation, not assault.

Treating a disabled person with such aggression and denying them basic privacy is a violation of the Ontario Human Rights Code and the Patient Bill of Rights. St. Michael's is supposed to be a place of healing, but for my father, it was a place of trauma and physical harm.

We will be filing a formal complaints with Patient Relations and the Patient Ombudsman, and we will be pursuing this further. If you have a loved one with a disability, be incredibly careful. The security culture here is militaristic and dangerous.

☆ My Birthplace (September 1987)

✅️ Enormous facility

✅️ Hours Of Operation: 24 hours, 7 days

✅️ Heart of Downtown Toronto

✅️ Close to T.T.C bus stops

✅️ Food court for patients, visitors and staff

✅️ Seems really clean

YSM Evergreen Centre for Street-Involved Youth is a beacon of hope and support in Toronto, dedicated to transforming the lives of young people facing homelessness and adversity. From the moment you step through the doors, you are greeted by a warm and welcoming environment that embodies compassion and understanding.

The staff at YSM Evergreen are truly outstanding. They are not only highly trained professionals but also passionate advocates for the youth they serve. Their commitment to building trusting relationships is evident in every interaction, ensuring that each young person feels valued and heard. The programs offered are comprehensive and tailored to meet the diverse needs of the youth, including access to mental health services, education, job training, and life skills development.

One of the standout features of YSM Evergreen is their holistic approach to youth support. They recognize that each individual comes with unique challenges and strengths, and they strive to provide personalized care that empowers young people to take control of their lives. The Centre also fosters a sense of community, encouraging peer connections and support networks that are crucial for healing and growth.

Moreover, the impact of YSM Evergreen extends beyond immediate support. Their commitment to advocacy and raising awareness about youth homelessness is commendable, as they work to address systemic issues and promote long-term solutions.

In summary, YSM Evergreen Centre for Street-Involved Youth is an exemplary organization that plays a vital role in the Toronto community. Their unwavering dedication to improving the lives of young people in need makes them a model for other organizations. If you are looking for a place that truly cares about youth and their futures, YSM Evergreen is the place to support and champion.

description:
had top surgery with Dr. Armstrong yesterday and she is incredible!!
her and the whole team were so incredibly kind and explained everything as it was happening so i knew exactly what was going on.
other doctors were involved, like Dr. Xu and Dr. Gupta who were also incredible.
