I am so glad i got to have Dr. Armstrong as my surgeon and would recommend her as a great option for anyone wanting to get on a list for top surgery.

I am a 22-year-old patient who waited nearly two years to see Dr. Lemos. Unfortunately, my experience at this women’s hospital was deeply distressing and traumatic.

At my first appointment after 1.5 years, I was scheduled for a urodynamic study. I arrived early and on time and clearly explained to the nurse that I have pelvic floor dysfunction and cannot hold my bladder for long periods. I asked multiple times how long the wait would be and was told approximately 30 minutes. When I explained I could not hold my bladder that long, the nurse told me to empty my bladder.

Shortly after using the bathroom (as instructed), I was called in, only to be told that my bladder now needed to refill — resulting in an additional 1.5-hour delay. I was later blamed for using the bathroom despite having asked for clarification multiple times and following staff instructions.

During the procedure, the nurse assisting me was kind, compassionate, and did her best to support me through what was the most painful medical experience I have ever had. Due to the severity of the pain, dizziness, and shortness of breath, I was unable to complete the test.

When Dr. Lemos entered the room, he did not introduce himself and responded to my distress dismissively, stating that many women complete the test and implying that I should be able to as well. When I asked what to expect next, I was told that nothing could be done since the test was incomplete and that any further testing would be inconclusive.

For two months following this appointment, I experienced internal bleeding, severe pain with urination, and was unable to engage in sexual activity due to the trauma and physical pain.

After hearing nothing from the hospital, Dr. Lemos’ assistant later contacted me regarding an ultrasound referral that I had never received. I explained my experience honestly and respectfully. Shortly afterward, I was informed that Dr. Lemos would no longer provide me care because he was “uncomfortable” with how I described what happened.

Since then, both my family doctor and I have been requesting my medical records for nearly a month without success.

I am sharing this not to cause conflict, but to warn other young women — especially those dealing with suspected endometriosis or chronic pelvic pain — to seek providers who show compassion, accountability, and trauma-informed care. No patient should be made to feel like a lab subject or dismissed during moments of extreme vulnerability.

Had surgery with them today and honestly the entire team on the 8th floor was amazing. Receptionists, nurses, anesthesiologist. Everyone was kind and efficient…and big shout out to Dr.İm for being a real one! Am rarely a happy camper but this experience was 10/10.

Everyone was very friendly has smiles on their faces and took their time with you. I was there twice. Both times same wonderful experience. I had the same nurse both times. Her name is Elsie. name: Reactivation Care Centre
description:
When I a child this was called the hospital you were sent to to die. At 53 years old, operating as a hybrid clinic/whatever it is, it is still total garbage. Everyone in the wound clinic I saw attending recently as a patient was horrible except one nurse. The rest were just plain mean. My wound is not healed yet but healing. Still a week ago the nurse just arbitrarily in the moment said ah it looks good enough you aren’t t anymore, so I am going to discharge you. And even though I said I need, as a type 2 diabetic, care to make sure that this wound is really healed as it is still raw and red, she said use some Vaseline, walked away and discharged me. The chief of staff needs to look at this as I am looking into medical malpractice and negligence charges. I want my wound cared for properly and healed. Or is that too much to ask from a wound clinic ?!! Am I suppose to play diabetic Russian roulette with my limbs? We keep saying we need more nurses. No we need less nurses that are so jaded a 5 minute bandage change in a sterile environment, with the proper lighting, and their trained eyes to make sure the wound isn’t infected isn’t a burden on them. Ridiculous building that should be torn down because it hurts more people than it ever helped.

I recently spent over two months in the RCC Reactivation Care Center, Unit 2C, suffering a serious injury after falling from a 10-foot ladder. I had surgery on my right tibia and also sustained a fracture in my left leg. It was a very difficult time for me, but the incredible team at Unit 2C made all the difference. I want to express my deepest gratitude to every member of the staff-the doctors, nurses, physiotherapists, occupational therapists, social workers, PSWs, meal delivery staff, cleaning staff, and all other team members who work in this unit. Their dedication, professionalism, and compassion were truly outstanding.

Thanks to their excellent care, support, and encouragement, I am now ready to move on to rehab for further training and strengthening so l can walk normally again. I am extremely grateful for everything they have done for me. Their commitment and expertise were truly outstanding. Thank you Unit 2C, for helping me through one of the toughest periods of my life. You truly deserve 5 stars. God bless all of you and your families. God bless Canada and people of Canada 🙏

With Sincere thanks
From Aamir Jajo Room 209 b unit 2c

There are no words for this so-called “hoteling hospital,” because that’s all it is — a place where patients are housed, not cared for. Call buttons go unanswered while nurses are on their cellphones, and patients are routinely ignored. The floors are dirty, basic cleanliness is lacking, and the food is something I wouldn’t serve to anyone, let alone sick or vulnerable people.

This is not healthcare — it’s warehousing. Families come here expecting care, dignity, and attention, and instead they witness neglect and indifference. What’s most disheartening is the sense that this has become normal and that nothing ever changes. Patients deserve far better than this.

I arrived on August 20/25
I had a compress spine accident with a small fracture. I was at this institution for 2 weeks. The food was good.The nurses were fine but! I began to have bouts of diarrhea. Was 13 days?No one told me why I was pooping so much.The Doctor did cut back on the. BM medicine. I received 1 hour 40 mins of walking therapy, hardly adequate. Around august 31 i was averaging 6/7 mushy BMs , still no answer why I was wasting away. On september the 1st. The nurses were ordered not to give me. Antibowel medicine, on September 4th, doctor arrived in my room.I tell me it was because of stuck poop.\nAnd then I didn't have any disease or other illnesses. By then i was only having diarrhea every two days. On the evening I i was scheduled to leave the hospital september 5th it made more medicine. Makes me poop even more. I was so worried going home that night. So terribly worried.
They only helped with walking skills.They refused to help with pooping other than giving me occasional b m medicine. They don't care, but I'm going home with diarrhea. Bad diarrhea. My advice don't come to RCC. It's a hell hole, of lies

I would not wish this facility on my worst enemy. My father was admitted to RCC after a hospital stay at sunnybrook. He is in the late stages of Alzheimer’s.

Under RCC watch, he suffered 4 falls, the final of which put him in hospital with a broken pelvis. What’s the point of bed alarms if no one is there to investigate when they start to go off? This occurred the day before he was supposed to be transferred to private LTC.

The day before his last fall, I spoke to the nurse on the floor, and explained it’s only a matter of time until he falls again, and requested a reclining wheelchair so he wouldn’t be able to get out. I was told they wouldn’t give him one because they didn’t want him sleeping during the day as it makes the nights difficult for nursing staff.

In one instance, they put him in a wheelchair to monitor him outside the nursing station, somehow, he fell out of his wheelchair despite staff sitting directly in front of him.

The staff are poorly trained, and the facility ill equipped and understaffed to deal with or care for individuals in this state.

name: Mental Health Inpatient Unit - Toronto General Hospital
description:
December 4, 2025 ⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐
I visited Toronto General Hospital for an ENT appointment while the hospital is undergoing renovations. What I appreciated most was how clearly everything was labeled, without that, it would be very easy to get lost. The appointment itself went well. I was given helpful information to review and further testing was arranged. I’m hopeful this will lead to some answers and solutions.

I would like to express my sincere appreciation for the outstanding care I received during my three-day stay. The staff ensured I was consistently informed, supported, and treated with the utmost professionalism and compassion throughout my time there.

A special thank you to the 5th floor Cardiology team for their exceptional dedication and attentiveness, it was clear that patient care is truly a priority. Having experienced care at other hospitals in the past, I can confidently say that the level of service and attention I received at Toronto General Hospital was truly exceptional.

Our deepest gratitude to the incredible cardiology ICU team at Toronto General Hospital. My husband, Bill, was admitted in critical condition with heart failure, and there are truly no words that feel big enough to express our thanks.🙏🏻🙏🏻

From the moment he arrived, the doctors, nurses, and ICU staff showed extraordinary skill, dedication, and compassion. During the most frightening days of our lives, they not only worked tirelessly to save Bill’s life, but also supported our family with kindness, patience, and reassurance.

We would especially like to acknowledge Dr. Adriana Luk, along with the many other physicians involved in Bill’s care, for their expertise, dedication, and compassion. Their dedication made an enormous difference during a very critical time 🙏🏻

Every nurse on the unit went above and beyond, treating Bill with dignity, respect, and genuine care.
The ICU staff cared for Bill as an individual, with kindness and respect, not as just another name on a chart. The teamwork, professionalism, and humanity shown by this cardiology ICU were truly remarkable ❤️🙏🏻👍🏻

Because of this extraordinary team, my husband is alive today. We will forever be grateful for the care we received and for the hope we were given.

With heartfelt thanks,
Bill & Rachel

Great care. Prompt. Friendly, efficient , knowledgeable personnel. Definitely trustworthy. You get quick attendance, proper therapy. Nurses, doctors ,volunteers  all superb. Big Thank you to all of them.

I cannot begin to put into words how grateful I am for the care my father received at TGH through the cardio clinic. From the admin staff, to the volunteers, nurses, staff, surgeons, doctors- everyone was incredibly supportive, patient, empathetic and they truly made this life changing surgery for my father an incredible experience. We will be forever grateful for their support.

name: Addictions and Concurrent Disorder Program- Virtual
description:
Just reminiscing about my time at Harbor Light Treatment Center in Toronto on Jarvis Street. My clean date is November 1st 2001. Here's the thing I am from Northern Ontario small town. I got used to Toronto and got myself a sponsor and went to many AA meetings on my free time. I learned how to take the subway and the streetcar to go to meetings Jared my counselor encouraged me to go get my health card on my own and I didn't get lost lol. Downtown Toronto is a big place for a small town guy. I loved it there it saved my life.

Harbour Light saved my son and our family ❤️

After years of pleading for help to allow a quadriplegic to be helped from prescription medication, Harbour Light allowed him the opportunity to enter its magical doors. The only place in Canada to do this.

It changed our lives forever. Without them, well I can’t even imagine.

Nic continues to be involved with their magic after 8 years of sobriety.

We should all be so proud of the Salvation Army and how they actually change lives. ❤️

#salvationarmy

The Harbour Light Treatment Center Saved My Life.

My clean date is October.27th.2023

I entered Harbour Light Nov.2nd.2023
I was deep in my addiction , in a very dark place & Using everyday , I was in a viscous cycle and seen no way out at one point in my life. When someone had mentioned this treatment centre to me at a N/A meeting.

The best decision I made in my life was calling the intake office and admitting my self into the program that would later on save my life and make me change every aspect of my life in order to live a meaningful life with purpose. The program is not easy , mentally “I” had to challenge myself everyday in order to change my thoughts , behaviors & actions that “I”was once using that were not getting me anywhere, everything I was doing was just ruining my life and the people who cared the most about me in my life.

Thanks to this treatment center I have a stronger relationship with God , My grandmother, Parents, Brothers & Sister. Most importantly I have myself back ,  I was a lost soul but thanks to this program and all of the Councellors who challenged me each and everyday of the 3 months of being there , seen  the true potential in me and who I could be and become.

I am extremely greatful to be coming up to 5 months clean this month & the many blessings in my life today. Life is precious , invest the time in YOU!! To have a better outcome and purpose in your life. If you really want this do it , the staff at Harbour Light will support you in every step of your journey.

At the end of the day you only have this one life to live, so live it with meaning and purpose to want and to live a better life then the one you are living today.

God bless the Councellors & staff at Harbour Light Treatment Center who were part of my journey. Thank you for believing in me and giving me the gift of life back.

- If you’re reading this it’s not too late! Change can happen it’s all in how far you will go in order to CHANGE every aspect of your life.

May God bless you in your journey 🙏 - Andrew B -

In general the Salvation Army provides services not found in any job benefits or Provincial programs.
In the private sector we expect and demand confidentiality. I see no difference when it comes to the above. There are rules and guidelines that some do not wish to be subject to or they may not qualify for immediate assistance and many may find this surprising as The Salvation Army accepts assistance from both the Public and the City of Toronto Government. Programs may be accessible to those who are not connected to a facility but do attend the location on casual basis to either provide or participate in these programs.
My rating reflects my satisfaction. I think to provide specific details would not provide any useful information as every person may hold a different experience or opinion. I can only wish you the best experience should you ever be in a position of having lost everything including your family your shelter your income and ultimately yourself. Whats next. Give the Salvation Army a try. They were and are there for me. My prayers to you to find the way through today. Yesterday today and tomorrow. Yesterday has past and tomorrow has yet to come. You have the ability to change the things that happen today. So focus on today as nothing can change yesterday and tomorrow has yet to come. Today is something we can affect and we can make changes and decisions that will improve ourselves if we so choose that direction. These are the words from a group I attend on a regular basis and I express them with no expectations or intent. I found them helpful. Maybe someone else will too. Thanks.

This place saved my life... and if you thought the staff were rude and didn't know how to help, you were not ine headspace to let them.

name: Bhatti Psychotherapy and Counselling Centre
address: Bloor Islington Place, 3250 Bloor St W

name: Comprehensive Treatment Clinic - Comprehensive Treatment Clinic
description:
I struggled for my entire life with addiction. And felt that I was hopeless. As it happens, addiction and mental health affects a massive amount of our population. Here at CTC Doctor B Is by far the most compassionate listener and a firm believer in supporting addicts in recovery. If you are willing to go to any lengths to live, and thrive, then you are ready to take certain steps.
This is a safe place, and a gentle place in terms of no-judgement and heart-filled advice. Any medication to help you remain sober or remove cravings needs research. This is the place to ask and a place to receive. The doctor here monitors my recovery and listens to me babble on about the new miracles I have face today. I love it here and I strongly recommend a visit. Appointments are not needed. Also, there are drop-in groups or activities geared towards minimizing isolation during this pandemic. This is a place for either medical assisted treatment plans and medical advice from doctors, but also other medical needs as they expand their services. Dr. Beinnenstock and Mayfield are the in the business.  Doctors have stayed late to treat me when they had other commitments. Clients in front of them are their priorities. I'd give ten stars if I could.

This treatment clinic I highly recommend it has been exceptional from the start for me over the last 4 years, with compassionate understanding staff that also are the best at their jobs. This clinic has been a big factor in my recovery and recommend to anyone who  wants to make a change in their life for the better and get back to living their best life. Addiction free.

My personal testimony about CTC Danforth..  I was a very active young man, training very hard daily in hopes of becoming professional fighter. I had tore my acl 100% and put 3 vertebrae in my neck totally out of position.  After being told that I probably wouldn’t fight anymore, was 1 of the most heartbreaking, things I have ever had to hear.  I didn’t recognize myself through the cloud of obsession of never fighting again. I got surgery after surgery only to be left a broken body and a depression of losing the only thing I had going for me.  I ended up getting stuck on pain meds that without i just wanted to die. Starting the program somewhere else and also trying other drs and pain clinics only to get this standard cookie cutter drs which never listened to me and basically telling me to take or leave it, causing another problem in its self.  As soon as I met Dr B I felt that little bit of hope, that I desperately needed.  This day I cried soo much out of relief.  Even as write this I’m tearing up.  I’ve had a few ups and downs,  but Dr B standing right behind me, not to catch me but make sure I learn and achieve growth with every mistake I go through.
Dr B is that dr where you know that you are safe in his hands, but if you don’t deserve a break, you’re not gonna get a break.  He will show you what getting a break looks like, and will give you any and every opportunity you need to achieve these breaks. I can go on and on and on, Dr B truly saved my life from the slow death I was putting myself through.  Also Dr merrifield is 1 in the same.  I’m privileged and lucky to have found the best group of Drs and nurses, Amanda and Emily, the most kind, caring and supportive.  I’m a 20 year plus in the program, I never thought I would be getting off, and now for the very first, I’m making my recovery with a feeling of strength and gratitude

Can’t even give zero stars. Brown hair receptionist super rude. If your having a bad day stay home! Learn to smile and talk properly without rolling your head and giving attitude. The only receptionist who’s kind is an older , curly hair brown lady and black lady who at least removes their mask knowing it’s impossible to hear properly being hard of hearing. The doctor who deals with regular paitents not drugs addicts is only there Monday and Tuesday. A day people work, a day people are the most busy. I don’t get it at all. Went to PRO health up the street. Immediately got to see a doctor, was accommodate, everyone was polite. Left with a big smile.

I will only come here if the receptionist is changed and proper accommodation is given . This is Canada. Treat people with respect

I was a walk in, interested in a quote on removal of a keloid scar. The receptionist was extremely kind, helpful & was able to fit me in! Dr. B. was MOST wonderful! He explained everything to me; took the time to make me feel extremely comfortable & informed. He gave me the best princig for removal that I have been given.
I am MOST impressed by this community based health care centre that offers a variety of important services for people.

name: Ehn Bellwood Toronto - Substance Use Treatment Program For Ontario Residents - Semi Private/Private
description:
I owe so much to all staff at Bellwood from Dr Weiss to my Psychotherapist Edward to all nurses, SCs, all educators , kitchen crew and cleaning crew all made me feel welcomed , cared for and that I truly mattered .
Without Bellwood can honestly say I would not be here today . With a CPTSD  , major post concussion syndrome , major depressive anxiety disorder , TRD diagnosis  before all this was determined I  was ready to throw in the towel ,as I felt my situation  had let my children down and I would no longer be able to return to my career as a FFer . With kindness ,professionalism of Bellwood staff  as well as the genuine kindness and support from my fellow Bellwood cohorts (friends)  I began to see there was a light and I slowly began to believe in myself again . I could write a book on my experience at Bellwood both the highs and the lows but like anything if you keep on fighting you can and will succeed . I'm happily  living a positive life surrounded by my children good friends and work colleagues and was promoted to Lieutenant in SJFD.
At first when arriving at Bellwood I was lost and in a daze but each day ( was one of the longest to stay 5.5mths) it would get more clear . But into the program do all the programs from Wendy's fitness classes to Christine's art classes and you will begin to see and feel more clear .

I am beyond grateful to Bellwood Treatment Centre for the incredible care they gave my friend, Peter Macreanu. He was in a dark place and we were all so worried about him. The transformation after his time at Bellwood has been nothing short of amazing. He came back healthier, clearer, and more hopeful than we have seen him in years. The staff cleanly care deeply about what they do. Bellwood did not just help Peter, they help all of us who love him.

My close friend Peter Maclean’s went to treatment to Bellwood and have a life changing experience. The program was structured, affective and delivered by professionals who clearly know what they are doing. Since finishing the program, Peter has doing better than ever. Bellwood gave him the tools to rebuild his life and I can’t thank them enough.

Peter M’s transformation at Bellwood Treatment Centre has been truly remarkable. Their holistic and compassionate approach played a vital role in his recovery. I would highly recommend this centre to anyone seeking meaningful, lasting change.

I was a client at Bellwoods in 2013, for a period of 80 days. It was a tremendously positive experience. I have maintained complete sobriety since then. This has led to vast changes and improvements in my life. I also continued on to volunteer thier in a few capacities. I have also participated in alumni activities over the years. I have also spoken a few times for the organization, to the clients, about my experience. I have found Bellwoods to be a positive environment, and I would recommend it to anyone.

name: Eva's Initiatives For Homeless Youth - Eva's Satellite
description:
A horrible place to be at. Some staff will treat you like family, others will treat you like dirty dogs. I do not recommend this shelter to anyone who  Is in need of a safe place to stay. You would be at the emotional whims of the staff who treat you with no respect and act as if they are above you. Find another place and stay safe. Peace and love.

Of years of being in and out of the shelter system, Eva's Satellite is by far the worst shelter of staff members I've ever come across. And that's saying something! The staff members are rude, unhelpful and abuse their power.

If you value finding an apartment and getting any sort of help from Shelter support don't come here. They won't help you in any capacity.

If you value being spoken to with respect, do not come here. They will treat you like garbage and get away with it.

The staff are awesome. The residents suck. The facility is much cleaner than other shelters. If you stay here, keep in mind that, you'll most likely have a roommate that starts violent drama, does drugs or will try to steal your stuff. The staff do everything they can to get youth back on track with their lives though and most importantly, they're lenient and understanding. The selection of child youth workers here, are byfar, better than any other shelter staff in Toronto. This is coming from a year-long resident.

During my week at Eva’s satellite I can say that overall, this is place does fairly well for what a shelter needs to do. Most staff are nice, supportive but assertive when they need to be. They provide you with food and a place to sleep. If you want to talk to them, they will be there to support you and let you speak. Every staff is a youth worker, which to my understanding means that they are social workers (people you can talk to about your problems and they’ll listen). Every staff also has a different approach in which they’ll help you when speaking to them individually. For instance one staff was more blunt than others as she wanted her patients to know what they might not be aware of, while another was more listening and kind, providing the nurturing and supportive environment for you to grow.

Some of these staff include Kiran, Latifa, Claudia, Shawna and Marcel have been great in my experience (I may be spelling the names incorrectly and emphasis that this is just my experience, these staff members may have mistreated other residents here and there that I do not know of and vice versa).

There is however one staff that I had a bad experience with in particular. Her name is Kathy.

There was one incident when she started yelling at me for asking for a supervisor, and I asked her nicely if she can stop raising her voice. (She had already made a few demands previously that bugged me and so I wanted to speak to a supervisor). She then became defensive and said “I don’t have time for this” and couldn’t not save face. After a few back and forth exchanges, I could not take it anymore and said that if she perisisted, I would report her to her boss.

About an hour later, when shifts switched another staff spoke to me. I was told to stop “making threats to other staff” and “breaking the rules”.

There was another incident when I was transferred to another shelter. I came back to pick up my belongings and then asked to fill up my water bottle (shelters give food and water to the homeless for free). I was given one cup, and then requested another. The staff that I was speaking to said that “I’m sorry, I cannot. I am part time. Full time staff know what to do.” Since I knew what I knew, I was then inquisitive and suspected Kathy was bringing in her personal emotions into professional decisions. So I then asked if Kathy was the one behind this, then out of nowhere, Kathy opened the door and yelled “come back” towards the staff I was speaking to and then with full force and anger grabbed her back in, closing the door.

I’m certain I am not the only one who’s been treated like this by her and am concerned for the safety and future of other residents. I am a youth that is fortunate enough to speak about this. Other shelter residents may need serious forms of mental health support, that may have gone through traumatic experiences, abuse and may take such an experience more damagingly to their own self worth and psychological well being.

I have been coming to this location at least once a week for the past 3 years to get harm reduction supplies, and I am so thankful this place exsists. Not only can you quickly and discreetly drop by for supplies 24/7, the staff has always been extremely respectful. It's been a pleasure having a quick chat with the staff when I pop by as they are supportive and positive and always ready to help out when they can. Thank you for everything you guys do & just know you guys are appreciated!!

name: Good Shepherd Non Profit Homes, Toronto - Toronto
description:
The Creative Arts Studio is ableist. For almost six months I had a walker and could not enter to use the studio due to stairs. Because it is not wheelchair accessible, this disrupted my creative work and made all of the projects I was working on unable to complete. I wish I never got involved in this agency.

Great service, amazing people;!

Terrible food. Would not recommend to eat there. Unhealthy about to go bad food made with spices to hide the flavor. No nutrients. Sometimes not even cooked fully. Repetitive. Chance of food poisoning.

Very nice and welcoming
I see the way they welcoming treating and comforting new people is very sweet and peaceful. Keep the great work for God and the community.

They are the best of the best. Please support this organization. Even during lockdown, they were heroic in serving those most in need

name: Centre for Mindfulness Studies
address: 180 Sudbury St, Unit C2, Toronto, ON M6J 0A8
description:
I did the Mindful Self-Compassion program with Christine and Elaine at the Centre and it was such a life changing experience. The facilitators created a really compassionate environment. It felt like going to therapy for 3 hours! I was skeptical about the course before but it really helped me change my perspective about myself. I find that (and many who took the course with me) adding self compassion to mindfulness can create such a positive shift. Overall, highly recommend.
Note: The Centre for Mindfulness Studies offer this course for MUCH cheaper than other Centres in Toronto offering the exact same course!

I showed up for a free mindfulness session today at 12 noon, as advertised on the company website. On my arrival I discovered that the place was completely empty of people. There was no staff, just an empty room with some chairs and tables. I called but no-one picked up so I left a voicemail. Very disappointing.

The Mindfulness-Based Cognitive Therapy (MBCT) course that I recently completed was incredibly helpful and informative. The instructors were both compassionate and knowledgeable, creating a supportive and enriching learning environment.

This place kind of ruined mindfulness for me. About half way through the course, I got a new job, and could no longer make it during the time I was supposed to attend. There were other courses at different times, and I asked if I could complete the remaining sessions there. No I could not. Could I get a partial refund? No I could not. Apparently it was impossible for me to switch to a new group because people had a certain comfort level within their own groups and I would disrupt that. I find that laughable since the courses are so crammed full of people you hardly recognize anyone from one week to the next, let alone have a special comfort level with them.The teachers did try to help me (they are the only reason I'm giving this 2 stars instead of 1) but it was the administration that kept me from taking the rest of the course. ALSO, about a month or so after I could no longer attend, thereby wasting hundreds of dollars, they contacted me because my OHIP had expired. Of course, it had expired AFTER I stopped being able to attend, but that didn't seem to bother them. I also find it interesting that we pay a very big fee to attend these courses and then they also bill OHIP. Somebody is making good money off all us anxious suckers. In any case, the way this place is run does not feel very mindful. They are just trying to make money off as many people as possible.

The Center for Mindfulness Studies combines modern medical knowledge with centuries old teachings to create a state of the art program like no other. Instructors come from hospital, clinical and family service backgrounds with a caring persona.
The course I took gave me all the basics  to eliminate depression from my life. I now have a job, secure living arrangements and a positive future outlook. I recommend any course that is offered at the Center. Follow the course path with an open mind, work hard and results will come. I can speak honestly from experience.
Mindfulness is now an integral part of my life. One can never stop learning and  receiving benefits  from living mindfully.Give it a chance...you will not be sorry.

The support offered by Alternatives is life changing. Litty George ( Case Manager) has managed to work on  broadening my ability & skills to prioritize tasks I want and need done. To not get caught up in all the details (OCD), and get stalled with procrastination. There is much you can learn even as a senior to develop more effective strategies to improve your personal self worth, and minimize stresses while living with ADD. Thanks Litty!

For personal one one one counseling for people with mental health issue.

name: George Brown College - Toronto - Transitions To Post-Secondary Education
address: St. James Campus, 534E-200 King St E, Toronto, ON M5A 3W8
description:
Best college ever
George Brown College is a good place to study. The teachers are supportive and explain concepts clearly. I really liked the practical learning style, which helped me understand how things work in real life. The college also offers helpful student services and a positive learning environment. Overall, it’s a good experience and a great choice for career-focused education.

Completed my 1 year Arts program at this college. Most professors were very helpful and supportive. The campus is pretty big and beautiful. There's good diversity.  The canteen food menu is kinda limited and have room for improvement. Lots of sitting spaces outside of classrooms though which is good. Admin work is fast. Lots of amenities like test center for disability, good library selection. Overall I feel really liked this place.

I wish they wouldn't promote themselves as a diverse school accepting large numbers of international students when they can't provide adequate care for them. I don't consider it a school that offers value for money, including the classroom atmosphere and teaching methods. Students considering enrolment in business-related programmes in particular should think carefully.

The administrative procedures were extremely slow. I completed my course in mid-August, yet by late-September, the school still had not released the results. This significant delay has negatively impacted my ability to proceed with the licensure exam.

One of the Best place to study in Toronto. They provide excellent infrastructure and so many things to do for students. They also help the students in different ways like free grovery etc.

name: Gerstein Crisis Centre - FRESH (FINDING RECOVERY THROUGH EXERCISE SKILLS AND HOPE)
address: 100 Charles St E, Toronto, ON M4Y 1V3
description:
The lady who answered my call was very dismissive and rude. She  said “what do you want me to do about that I have other callers on the queue” in a very dismissive and rude tone when I called to vent about a situation. The workers should be more empathetic. Nothing about the call helped my situation.

FRESH program (more so rotten due to my experience) was absolutely not as good as I'd hoped (putting it lightly). putting it harshly (and truthfully) it was awful, futile, boring, frustrating in execution and the facilitator Steve and the cast is seriously such a bunch of duds who I didn't enjoy at all whatsoever. what a bust. i do not love who joins as clients because most were a very older demographic which i could hardly ever relate to and even worse if foreign and you can barely understand them. majority isn't younger in age like myself so if i am not feeling apart of something because of that, you have to expect i won't take it well. Not fun at all and for over a month I wasted my time ever being involved. I'm glad I said what I said to Steve even the way I handled it I wouldn't take back and don't get me started on his boss whom I hate with a passion and have no respect for taking his employee's word for something he never witnessed which was a false incident report. Well guess what Michael Aucoin, your employee deserved how I spoke to him and wasn't nearly as bad as I could've had it be. I'm glad you were so frustrated you hung up mid call because your "warning" doesn't mean anything to me and I declared I was out before that. I don't care what you think and you don't run me like you run others. So you aren't going to tell me what I'm going to do and expect me to just go with it nonchalantly. Oh yeah also the group hike was supposed to happen already and was canceled twice due to weather so they can't even keep their word because they're so messy at scheduling so I knew then I was over it because I just refuse to take them seriously if they can't get themselves in order. I have zero patience for this. Way to lose a client and cause a bridge to be burnt

DANGER! DO NOT GO HERE

The crisis workers answering phones are incredibly unprofessional. I was accused of yelling and explaining how to "do their job" before being hung up on. I went through 3 seperate workers who all took my crisis very personally. This is not the first time I've experienced issues. I was kicked out of their facility for giving attitude to a staff... Mind you I had been kicked out of my place in the middle of the pandemic. I was just a kid in crisis. I was recommended by police to.go to them.

I regret this decision to.this.day as it has really soured my opinion of the MH system.

If they can't understand you cause you're crying, they hang up. If you're speaking too fast, they will tell you to calm down until they hang up.

Crisis aren't meant to be pretty, Crisis workers are trained to de escalate and provide safety during the lowest points in people's lives where they are struggling to help themselves. I have yet to experience a staff member at this facility who can implement these skills effectively in a crisis.

EDIT: multiple 1 star reviews of clients saying they were hung up on from crisis team all.the way to head management. This is very concerning and UNSAFE.

Please report your experiences to the City of Toronto. I believe this is a city run, charity type centre. Please advocate for safety.

THIS PLACE IS NOT SAFE FOR THOSE IN CRISIS. PLEASE GO TO THE HOSPITAL.

it's not perfect at hospital, but at least you'll be safe and have access to more care options! Take care of you!! Not the crisis workers! They should be taking care of themselves so they are readily available to help.

I called this crisis center because I was struggling with severe depression, insomnia and work-related stress. The crisis worker who responded to my call was very dismissive and rude, and I ended up feeling worse. I cried while on the phone and I didn't think that I was going to make it through the night. It's honestly made me really scared to reach out for help elsewhere because of the bad experience I had. I didn't get the worker's name, but I hope that in the future she can show more empathy because it might seriously hurt someone out there.

Great people to talk to but honestly they judge more then you judge your self so no o will not be calling again thanks

name: Canadian Mental Health Association, National Office
Mood Disorders Association of Ontario and Toronto
description:
I have been going to MDAO for a long time. I've done the WRAP program. Attend dropin groups. Have used the warm lines, and social worker.
I can't say enough positive things about this organization.  This organization is run by volunteers and peer support services, along with paid staff. They are empathetic, kind, supportive. They have walked in our shoes.
In WRAP, I  learned I have agency, can ask for help, but can do something to help myself.
In dropin group I realized I'm not alone. I listen. Offer validation and support.
I don't know where I'd be today. I can't thank them enough.

*Edit from 5 stars. I NEVER gave this place 5 stars. My review had been edited somehow because I tried this for the "WRAP" program and never got a call back even a few years later

The person I spoke with was so unhelpful and withholding. I still wish to keep them anonymous, but the service was awful, like they weren’t trained to do their job and completely uninterested. I was highly disappointed.

Empathetic and caring staff, helpful peer to peer and drop-in programs, and a supportive environment; all of which have helped me in healing the mind one day at a time. Thank YOU Mood Disorders Association of Ontario. God Bless!

I was looking forward to participating in the painting program and peer support they offer only to learn that over a year after Ontario ended the segregation between people who choose to get a vaccine and people who chose to excercize their right not to, the Hope and Me organization are still discriminating, even tho Ontario ended requirment of proof of vaccination, in all settings on March 1st, 2022. Its very unfortunate that an organization and program geared toward mental health is refusing people for a non mandated vaccine, especially since some people did not have a choice in being able to get the vaccine due to a severe phobia that is life altering and debilitating.

name: Ontario Obsessive Compulsive Disorder Network
description:
I was diagnosed with ocd after struggling with it on and off ever since a young age.
I’m writing this review as I understand how hard it can be in choosing the right therapist to work with. First and foremost a few years ago I was seeing a psychotherapist with no diagnosis to realize it did help but I had not been diagnosed I figured it was just anxiety as did the therapist so with ocd there was a lot of reassurance seeking each session which continued to feed the loop.
After being diagnosed this year and reading review after review trying to find the best therapist out there to tackle the disorder with me I found Robert with the ocd and anxiety clinic of Ontario.   Life changing. Is all I can say. I cannot thank you enough Robert for guiding me and teaching me the ins and outs of ocd. Soo unbelievable what guidance from An experienced professional can do and has allowed me to reach new levels in life I never thought I could. I want to thank you Robert for giving me the tools and knowledge that will forever change my life. And I was able to do it at my own pace and grow and put the tools to work. I went from debilitating at home for months, to. Return back to work better and stronger than ever receiving employee of the month and moving up to new heights.
I sincerely hope this review helps many as if it weren’t for reviews I wouldn’t have landed with Robert I highly highly highly suggest reaching out to ocd and anxiety clinic of Ontario. Be patient and put in the work provided and trust the process it will pay off.
Sincerely

Robert has dramatically changed my life. I am a health care provider who works in the hospital setting. I struggle with contamination OCD to the point where I was unable to complete activities of daily living. A therapist I work with recommended Robert and when I reached out he was kind enough to see me. Through meditation, CBT for OCD, and ERP Robert was able to help me learn to live with anxiety and uncertainty and eventually helped me stop all of my compulsive behaviours. Robert is a very smart, compassionate, and knowledgeable practitioner. He gets the results that so many of us eagerly look for. I give him a 10/10. I thank him from the bottom of my heart.

Rob is very kind, patient and encouraging, Speaking to him has given me much clarity & has grounded me back into reality. If you’re struggling with OCD, here is the place to go ✨

Robert's professionalism and dedication exceeds the boundaries of a mere five-star rating. I am so grateful to have crossed paths with him. His unwavering commitment to supporting others is truly unparalleled. Not only does he excel as a psychologist, delivering the highest quality of care to his clients, but his genuine kindness, compassion, and understanding extend far beyond the confines of therapy sessions, which truly shows his exemplary character. Robert's authenticity, down-to-earth nature, and exceptional expertise make him stand out as one of the most remarkable clinicians I’ve encountered.

Of all the OCD specialists I had intake appointments with, Rob was the only one I believed in/believed could actually help me. I must have spoken to 10-15 different therapists/psychologists.
In the end my intuition was correct. Rob was confident, compassionate, very knowledgeable and was also good at redirecting my focus as needed. So all in all he was great at his job.

IMHO it is very important to believe in whoever is helping you when it comes to therapy/etc. Rob was easy to believe in and was very helpful to learning about my OCD and thus aided in the treatment of it.

name: Youth Net (Networking Youth Nationally) c/o Children's Hospital of Eastern Ontario
description:
When I first arrived in Canada as a newcomer, I spent nearly two months with my daughter at CHEO. It was a challenging time for us, but the experience at CHEO made a huge difference.
Dr. Gardin closely monitored my daughter’s condition, providing clear explanations and answering all our questions with patience. The surgery was performed successfully by Dr. Maharajh, whose professionalism and care gave us great confidence throughout the process.
The nurses were incredibly kind and gentle with my daughter, helping to ease her stress and mine as well. Throughout our stay, I felt supported, respected, and cared for—not only medically, but emotionally as a parent in a new country.
I am very grateful to Dr. Gardin, Dr. Maharajh, and the entire CHEO team for their compassion and expertise.

We were sent to CHEO ER with a referral from our doctor today for an infant ultrasound (our baby is 5.5 months, we suspect a milk allergy, our GP wanted to rule out intussusception). The security guard that spoke with us upon arrival seemed a little too happy to inform us of the potential 20-hour waiting time ahead of us. Due to it being flu season, only one parent was allowed to stay in the waiting area with our baby. I left my wife with our son and headed for the place I was allowed to wait at the opposite end of the hospital. Beyond the initial waiting area, every hallway I walked through was lined with more parents and their children, waiting. It was a real shock to see. I returned shortly thereafter to my wife and son and we decided to head to the comforts of home rather than risk gathering a different illness from the cramped ER. The whole experience left me feeling helpless as a new parent. What a sad state for a children’s urgent care facility.

Edit: I have to revise my review following having to return to CHEO four days later and finally getting beyond the emergency room after an arduous 8-hour wait post-initial-triage. The nurses and doctors are all wonderful, hard-working individuals that have been so kind and loving. No one working the floor is at fault for the lack of support hospitals are receiving from both provincial and federal governments. If your child is sick, these people will take care of them, you just have to get through that first challenging hump. If you’re reading this, I wish you and your family well.

Extremely disappointed with the emergency wait times. Waiting over 6–8 hours with a sick 9 month infant is unacceptable and incredibly stressful for parents. While I understand hospitals are busy, better staffing and communication are urgently needed. No family should feel this helpless while seeking medical care.

I am very disappointed with my experience at CHEO ER, especially with the registration staff.

I brought my young son to the ER, where he was assessed by a doctor and suspected to have strep throat. The doctor clearly told me a throat sample would be sent to the lab, results would take 1–2 days, and I would receive a phone call with a prescription if the result was positive.

The result came back positive on MyChart, yet no one ever called me.

During this time, my son became extremely unwell. He did not eat or drink for two full days and did not urinate the entire day before I returned to the ER. Concerned and desperate, I went back to the ER only to get the prescription so my child could start treatment.

At registration, instead of receiving help or concern, the staff member was very rude, dismissive, and even laughed. When I explained that my son hadn’t been eating, drinking, or peeing, she showed no care or urgency at all. She repeatedly said, “They will call you when they call you. Maybe tomorrow, maybe after tomorrow.”

I was told that my only options were:
•	Wait at home for a call that never came, or
•	Sit in the ER for another minimum of 8 hours just to see a doctor again only for a prescription.

This was incredibly frustrating and unsafe.

To make matters worse, I am pregnant and due next month, yet I was not allowed to have my husband accompany me inside to help manage my sick child. Handling a very ill child alone, while pregnant, with no compassion or flexibility shown by staff, was overwhelming.

The lack of communication, empathy, and basic professionalism at registration was shocking. No parent should be treated this way—especially when their child is clearly unwell and at risk of dehydration.

CHEO is supposed to be a children’s hospital that supports families. Unfortunately, this experience showed the complete opposite.

One of  nurse who was working in triage, told us that she “bet” our daughter was not sick.

Parents come to the emergency department because they know their children better than anyone else. We monitor them for hours before deciding to seek medical care. When we come to the emergency room, we are looking for professionals not people who are willing to “bet” on our child’s health.

They were hiding their badge names, likely because they are ashamed of their behavior and actions.

name: Garry Hurvitz Centre for Community Mental Health at Sick Kids
address: 155 Gordon Baker Road, Ste. 400, Toronto, ON, M2H 3N5
description:
i was a part of their youth program a while back. i stayed there for 5 days a week, coming home on weekends.

the walls are hard brick. you’re only allowed 5 minute showers, even for females, and if you go any longer than that, a staff member will come in.

there’s barely any real therapy. just one group thing a day. when i was there, they made us eat dried crickets for ‘sensory mindfulness’ and they FORCED everyone to do it. i wish i could make this up, this happened on my first week there.

this was years and years ago but they traumatized me for life.

i think this program is just a place to give parents a break from their teens, rather than a place that actually helps.

It saved my life.

name: Aptus Treatment Centres
description:
Similar organizations in the Toronto area have pension plans or RRSP matching opportunities. APTUS has none and does not seem interested in implementing any!

I keep getting spam phone calls from this number and them swearing at me. I called them back to let them know that it might be someone using their phone line. But their response was, “that sucks” and hangs up.

name: Oakdale Child and Family Service
address: 10 Buchan Court, North York, ON, M2J 5A3
description: My experience was terrible the electrcal was illegal wiring  and staff making  me work for  free and they made me eat  salad out of the green ben  criticized me and you cozd me to end up in the hospital with mental health issues and mental trauma your abuse I've ways  because of you yelling at me you gave me schizophrenia  all because all the violence and trauma and because of you guys i ended up in the hospital 🏥 with psychosis because of the ways you've treated me

name: Scarborough Hospital Regional Crisis Mobile Response Program
address: 3050 Lawrence Ave E, Scarborough, ON, M1P 2V5
description:
Still open?
Typical band-aid approaches per se.
No real helps/fundings if need housing etc.

A well informed and compassionate team who provide much needed support to those looking for assistance.

name: SickKids Hospital - Department of Psychiatry: Tics and Tourette’s Clinic

name: Vita Community Living Services
description:
Unison is the best place to receive all kinds of support, especially for newcomers.
I just want to say thank you — truly wonderful people.

Went for an appointment with the UHCS' dietitian, really appreciated her knowledge and expertise. She gave me practical, expert advice on my eating habits. I went into the meeting very glum, thinking I'd be told to restrict some of my favourite foods, but instead left looking forward to trying out the many excellent suggestions I was provided. Very much appreciated!

Do you want to know why tent encampments grew by 20% in just one month this year? Could have something to do with housing staff here refusing any/all assistance on the basis that “I have no legal case, just a personal one” after being served an eviction notice 9 months into my tenancy that claimed I had not paid for 10/12 months and alongside a fake ledger easily contradicted by bank statements. I also have multiple recordings of landlord harassment. Unison staff became rude and confrontational when I have had to correct them about how many months are in a year and are now suddenly out of office every other week, and when they’re not, “they’ve sent them an email and will get back to me.”

It is a bitter pill to swallow that people who behave this way are the gatekeepers of housing in a crisis. Ego rules over compassion, or even basic logic here. Shame on you.

They also insist I travel to them despite significant disability which impacts my mobility. Isn’t “housing worker” an oxymoron if they’re not willing to work within their communities?

I write this out of a sincere desire to see change. I would be thrilled to have reason to edit this to five stars, regardless of my outcomes. Housing clinics should not be enabling slumlords full stop.

Edit: It’s actually worse since writing this. Not one person in the chain of command here gives one single sh

I cannot express my gratitude enough for the weekly fruit & veggie market held every Wednesday this summer. Being able to get such freshly harvested food for a small donation made a big difference for my family. Everyone is struggling right now with the high cost of LIFE and this has been such a tremendous experience. Seeing the community come together and talking with people from all different backgrounds is even a better experience. Making pamphlets with recipes to use for the food that is purchased is such a fantastic idea! I had no idea what a yellow carrot was, turns out it was a parsnip and I have never had  eaten that before. Your recipe told me what to do and it was delicious! I really truly hope that you can make this happen again next year. The only thing I see that needs improvement is the "priority vs. non-priority line. I completely understand the priority line is for those with disabilities/age/etc, however, there needs to be fair way to do so. I stand in line for sometimes 2 hours and wait my turn, all for someone to stroll up to the priority line and get there stuff in 10 mins. It's disappointing, but, again, I am so thankful that whatever I get, I'm blessed, thanks again!

P.S. The lovely woman who takes the money and give the tickets is just all around pleasant! I look forward to seeing her every time I go! She's firm, yet fair, kudos to you!!

Tracey

Organized with many free supports like doctors, social workers, housing workers dental, food bank and meal program. I visit their green farmer's market on Wednesdays for $5  you can get great vegetables and fruit. The music is thoughtful in keeping the energy while in line. The green market is for a limited time  see you there!

name: Youthlink
description:
1) I was admitted at age 11 when the minimum age was supposed to be 13. There was no clinical or forensic justification for this. I just had rich, abusive parents who could really sell a sob story, particularly about “their abusive daughter.”

2) I remember distinctly a 6 foot plus boy lurking in my bedroom doorway at night. This is the quality of staff you’re trusting your children with. I wish I could stop remembering this.

3) My parents gave me a picture of my cat in a flimsy plastic, nearly rubber textured dollar store frame. This could not possibly have been used as an instrument of harm. Staff confiscated it at admission and lied to my mom about doing so.

4) The strip searches and lice treatment upon admission are a nice touch, for an 11-year-old child who’s never had a lice exposure.

5) If that wasn’t vulnerable and humiliating enough, a staff member confronted me after going through my belongings because they found period stained underwear and chastised me “because this was human waste.” I’m pretty sure that female staff member was actually. Had she ever stopped to consider that a child menstruating that young and evidently quite heavily needed medical attention, or the attentive mother she never got? Not to be screamed at for experiencing normal bodily functions, and not provided basic comforts like a laundry hamper, or a single picture from home.

No daylight, no fresh air, no safety, no comfort, nothing but hospital slop to eat. This place is “The Yellow Wallpaper” for CHILDREN. Raze it already.

I stayed at the Huron house from the ages 16-17 from 2021-2022. The staff truly saved me and gave me a steady ground to work on. They helped to shape the person I am today and I miss them dearly. Today they sort of make up part of my internal counsel when I feel out of sorts. You have to be open and willing to truly work with them. If you’re reading this, thank you endlessly!

Hey guys it’s Rachel!! Update I’ve got a job , been going to the gym , found a new church with a great community !! Been passing all my school . Been doing greater then ever missing you guys thank you for everything

I was a troubled kid and was placed under Youthdale's “care”.  It was the worst experience of my life!  I was dumped off by my mother (adopted), and progressed to lose a chance of an education and wasted an extremely intelligent young mind.
This progressed to running away, living on the streets and all the horrors that come with it!  Don’t even consider bringing your child here, unless you’re planning on destroying them and forever.  I’m 54 now, I was there and in their Aurora house in the early 80’s.  I can attest to the damage they caused and my life has never been the same since.
I still remember Yuri, I still have nightmares about him.
DON’T trust anyone with your child there are ways to reach them if you try and LISTEN to them!

Youthdale is a wonderful place people are great here, no trauma!! Never even went in a padded room lol Which low-key was a disappointment jk lol but the reviews on here are not giving this place  A good reputation so I’m gonna change that! I was on the fourth floor for the six week program

people here are very nice And we have a great giraffe as team lead yes you!! if you’re reading this.
the rest of the team is pretty great too

I made great experiences here learned a lot Made some great friends!  had some great food Only complaint is the hospital food !!lol But we do groceries so not a big deal!
And every Friday we did something fun and went on new Adventures ! I really did love it here and Dr. Henry and the team are amazing people
Bye giraffe bye Holly And bye, cupcake!!! And
I will miss you guys so much I will not miss to wake up calls!  🫶🏻

This place is the place I met my best friend i will always be so happy and grateful I got to go here 🩷

name: Centennial College - Centre for Accessible Learning and Counselling Services (CALCS)
address: P.O. Box 631, Station A, Toronto, ON, M1K 5E9
description:
I paid for a locker rental on January 12th and have been using it consistently for three weeks now. Yesterday, I discovered my lock had been cut and replaced. When I spoke with Enrollment Services, I was informed that a system glitch on the college’s end failed to register my selection, allowing the locker to be reassigned to another student. While I was told to simply "pick another locker," there are no suitable options near my labs available anymore. As a 4th-semester student, I followed the rental process correctly. I should not be disadvantaged and forced to use a distant locker and pay for a replacement lock due to a technical error on the college's part so I requested  the following:

Restoration of my locker as I paid for and selected the locker first, and replacement of my lock.
Today I get an email saying they will not be giving my locker back (so what happened to first come first serve?)  and will only replace my lock. ( Now I'm curious to see when they will replace it and how long they will let me have my stuff unprotected in the new locker).

I am very dissatisfied with how these people handle things and this is the second time in the 3 weeks of this semester that this school messes up my things.

Such a disgrace!

Centennial College in Scarborough, particularly the Progress Campus, offers an exceptional and comprehensive educational experience. The college provides a wide array of courses that cater to diverse academic interests and career goals, making it an ideal choice for students pursuing diplomas, certificates, or degrees.

The Progress Campus is expansive, offering ample space for both academic and extracurricular activities. Classrooms are equipped with state-of-the-art facilities, ensuring an optimal learning environment enhanced by modern technology and resources.

In addition to its strong academic offerings, the campus features a range of excellent amenities designed to support student life. The recreation center provides a fantastic space for students to stay active and unwind. The welcome center creates a warm and inviting atmosphere for new students. For those entering the job market, the interview room is a valuable resource, and the study rooms offer quiet spaces for focused learning. The library is well-stocked with a vast collection of books and resources, making it an indispensable tool for research and study.

Parking is abundant, with a large parking area available for students and visitors, though it is a paid facility. Despite this, the convenience and security it offers make it worthwhile. Additionally, the campus benefits from excellent TTC bus service, ensuring easy and reliable public transportation options for commuting students.

In summary, Centennial College's Progress Campus in Scarborough stands out for its quality education and comprehensive student support. The variety of courses, excellent facilities, expansive campus, and strong transport links make it an outstanding choice for anyone looking to further their education. I highly recommend Centennial College for those seeking a top-tier academic experience in a supportive and well-equipped environment.

Can you please fix the issues with the Wi-Fi? There's students constantly having to hot spot because of the lack of reliability. I'm a 2nd year student and every single semester I've had to deal with this. I've even had professors tell me to bring my phone to class during in-class tests in case the Wi-Fi isn't working. It's literally the first week of the winter 2026 semester.

While I appreciate efforts to enhance the campus visually (new electronic signs, digital bulletin boards) it would be far more beneficial to students to have stable Wi-Fi across the Progress campus

I am extremely dissatisfied with the level of service provided by Centennial College’s Financial Aid/OSAP department. For over a week, I have attempted to contact the office regarding an important matter, spending several hours on hold without receiving assistance. The lack of timely communication and support has caused unnecessary stress.

Financial aid services are critical for students, and this experience does not meet reasonable expectations. I hope the college addresses these issues to improve student support in the future.

⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️
Progress Campus offers a modern and professional learning environment 🎓✨.
The classrooms and labs are well-equipped, making it easy to focus on studies 💻📚.
Faculty and staff are knowledgeable, approachable, and supportive 👩‍🏫👨‍🏫.
There are many opportunities for hands-on learning and practical experience 🌟🔧.
The campus is clean, organized, and comfortable for students to spend long hours 🌿🏫.
It is easily accessible by public transport 🚍, which makes commuting convenient.
Student services and resources, like counseling and workshops, are excellent 👍🤝.
Overall, Progress Campus provides a motivating and welcoming environment for academic success 💯✨.
I highly recommend it to anyone looking for a practical and career-focused education 🌟🎉.

name: Catholic Family Services of Simcoe County
description:
I spoke with Debbie. I was her 1st client. She helped me get through the depth of my situation and had me pull my own answers for what was strong with me to carry forward. Also, she gave me a simple mental exercise that distracted me from dark and confusing matters. Very pleasant and easy going experience.

I have suffered from anxiety for years.  I've tried to work around it.  I was physically sick almost every day leaving the house.  After a few months of education and implementing exercises.  I can leave the house without being sick.  I definitely recommend these services.  Put in the work and the methods really work.

Make better use of your money if you can and seek professional counselling elsewhere.  My wife and I tried couples counselling here and it didn't help any.  A great counsellor we had before gave us exercises and information to read and try in order to give us tools to resolve our issues.  The Catholic Family Services did nothing like that.  In fact, the guy we went to admitted to my wife that he already had his mind made up that we would separate and didn't suggest one single thing that we could try to resolve our differences.

Here using the facilities for a warming centre. It's a beautiful space and we're grateful for the generosity of CFSSC

My counsellor was so awesome. It has been life changing.  If you need counselling see someone here.

name: The Trauma Centre
description:
Staff very friendly, compassionate, resourceful and a tremendous support system during my visits. More than satisfied.

Great team over there. Friendly.  Mel is wonderful.

Every person I have interacted with here has been great.

Has the at home atmosphere making it comfortable.

Very well cared for

name: Barrie After Baby Group (EarlyON Child and Family Centre)
description:
Friendly and respectful satff.
Clean environment.
Nice circle time.
Place doesn't feel crowded or chaotic.
Plenty of things for babies/toddlers to do. My son LOVED IT. 💖

Disappointed by the lack of empathy and respect

I’ve brought my child here because I value her social development, but every visit leaves me drained. The environment feels more like a rule-enforcement zone than a supportive community for parents and children. Staff can be condescending instead of encouraging, and there’s little sense of real empathy for the parents who show up and try.

Children’s programs should be run with warmth and respect, not coldness or superiority. If the goal is to support families, then speaking down to parents in front of others is not the way to do it. My child deserves spaces that teach empathy and community through example — unfortunately, I haven’t experienced that here.

The centre itself is always clean and there are so many different areas for the kiddos to play. The staff are incredible and really try to connect with the families there, providing resources when needed and even just for a little chat. They have toys, sensory stations, books, and activities like painting and drawing. I really appreciate how careful the staff are with regards to the mask mandate. I know it's not the most comfortable for us adults but it makes me feel so much more comfortable knowing we are collectively doing what we can to keep our little ones safe.
If you've never been here before, it's important to note that they run a specific programs on Wednesday mornings for babies 12 and under. Otherwise, you can drop in with the little ones!

This place isn’t community, it’s control. Staff talk down to parents instead of showing empathy, and the whole program feels like it’s run by a broke system with broken people. If you can’t respect parents, you have no business teaching kids about community.

This place is awesome. My 1 year old loves it and even when he was too young to really "play" we took him and he loved just seeing what was going on. Both my parents have taken him and had fun too and my wife was a regular with him on her maternity leave. The staff are awesome, free parking and they do a great job interacting with the kids. He loved the musical circle time too

name: Barrie and Community Family Health Team Program
description:
Really disappointed by this merging/consolidation. I love Dr. Van Walraven and Lori at reception, but ever since this merger, I've had more issues than ever. Where it may have taken max 2-3 weeks to see a specialist before, it's now taking over 3 months at minimum.

I don't know why I'm complaining, maybe just hoping that whoever was in charge of this consolidation can feel some shame and guilt knowing that they've made "getting better" a LOT harder. If possible, please go back to the unconsolidated teams, and allow doctors to refer outside of this "community" team, because clearly, it's not working for the community.

Absolutely fed up with this doctors office. 2nd time now I’ve showed up for my 8am appointment and they canceld my appointment the morning of with no call no text, nothing. When us as patients cancel before 48hours we are subject to a charge. Who’s gonna pay me for missing work twice now…  this is absolutely unacceptable. Receptionist tried telling me they called me the night before then the story changed to this morning I showed my call log they never even tried reaching out. She then called whoever to find out I was correct and they didn’t call. Where’s my compensation for my loss work time. Are you going to pay my bills and gas for driving here for no reason?

My husband visited  for the second time yesterday and his experience was very good.
Dr was professional, to the point, helpful to assist with info about where to connect to get a CPAC machine,
He was on time and thorough.
Our first visit was not as cordial but we understand   .doctor are very stressed and we appreciate his expertise.

The Prenatal Well Baby Clinic ladies have been great. All my visits with my nurse practitioner, Nicole, have been better and more thorough then with my OB. She continues to provide support, knowledge and answer all my questions when I feel they are not being answered. Keep up the great work!!! I will be continuing to bring my baby there after birth as well.

Good evening,
I took my son this morning to the Prince William Way Clinic, was standing in front of the door trying to figure out what the procedure was when the lady that was receiving people opened the door and asked me if I had an appointment to which I said "yes," and she rudely told me to read the signs that were posted and closed the door.  Another patient's mom who heard the interaction said to me "she was feisty, but it's always that way." I told her that I thought it was me but she said "no, no it's always that way." To be honest I've been to that clinic a handful of times either for myself or my in-laws and I have always been treated nicely but not today.  My son had an appointment and we had been asked to arrive 5 mins earlier which we did.  When the lady who was doing the intake reopened the door and let my son and I in I decided to mention to her that she didn't need to be so rude.  To which she barked "I wasn't being rude, you were standing in front of the door for 10 minutes reading the sign, is that how long it takes you to read?" I said your policies are always changing and I wasn't sure what I was supposed to do. So she yelled at me to get out, she told me that the office was privately run and that she didn't need to put up with me and that I had to leave (all while yelling) I told her no, that I had an appointment for my son and he needed to be seen and I couldn't leave without him being seen, she kept yelling at me to leave and I refused because I wasn't the one that had behaved rudely to begin with to which she told me to "shut my mouth!"  The lady from reception then asked what was going on and I said "this lady is being rude and telling me that I have to leave but my son has an appointment and I'm not leaving so she told me to "keep your mouth shut and wait outside," I turned to the lady that was doing the intake and said look I'm sorry but you're sign sits on top of another sign and then just kept repeating that I was sorry and I was crying hysterically and was so desperate for my son to be seen (I found a bump on the back of my son's ear and was attempting to get an answer by having the doctor check him out).  She finally agreed and took our temperatures and  let my 8 year old son pass to his appointment.  I have never been belittled like that in my 42 years of life. I am a well educated individual and I teach my children to respect others and respect authority.  Today this lady completely abused the authority that was entrusted to her in a way that no one should have to experience.  Like I stated above, I've been to this clinic before and have never endured such verbal abuse or the like by anyone there, in fact everyone has always been so pleasant but to be honest I highly doubt that I will be returning and I will be mentioning my experience to my doctor's office.
