name: Dr. Luzcak
description:
This team really goes above and beyond to care for their patients, with great communication, proactivity and thoughtfulness. I’m glad I came here for my wisdom teeth extractions.

On initial X-ray, my teeth appeared close to a nerve so I was sent for additional scans to gather more information before going into surgery. Later we discussed the process, what to expect, and pain management options. Had a happily unmemorable surgery. Received check-ins post procedure, and am feeling great, healing quickly and comfortably. I’m booked for a 2 week post-op visit as a bonus, just to check in on the recovery.

Very grateful to Dr. Alex, April, Makaela & the whole team!  I intend to be a patient for life.

I now understand why this dental team is so highly rated.

My wife and I moved from the city and were looking for a new dentist. DBTB was very highly rated and from our initial consult to my recent extraction every aspect of the team has been top notch.

Mel and Mekala at the front desk, so nice and professional, April the assistant very knowledgeable and kind, and “Dr Alex” does exceptional work. My extraction was virtually pain free, no swelling, bruising or pain.  The operatories are very clean with modern equipment to boot.

So glad we choose DBTB, they feel like family and their work is top notch. Highly recommend!

After visiting four different dentists in the Sudbury area who all referred my daughter to an oral surgeon, we decided to see Dr. Alex and his incredible team in Barrie at Dentistry By The Bay, while visiting my parents in Gravenhurst — and I’m so thankful we did.

Dr. Alex went above and beyond for my daughter. He explained everything thoroughly, made her feel completely at ease, and performed her wisdom teeth removal with exceptional care and skill. What truly stood out was his compassion — he even checked in on her Saturday morning when the office was closed, just to make sure she was okay.

I honestly wish we lived closer, because Dr. Alex would absolutely be our dentist for life. His dedication, kindness, and professionalism are unmatched. We’re beyond grateful for the care we received!

The team at Dentistry by the bay were all so helpful and lovely. Dr Alex was so good to my daughter who was only coming home for xmas and came up with a game plan to help in a tough timely situation because she was in pain.   Took out her wisdom teeth without any issues
Even a follow up text on the weekend to see how she was. Fantastic experience there!

Amazing team! I'm always greeted with a smile - everyone is so friendly and helpful. My hygienist Amie is the best, super friendly and my teeth always look and feel great thanks to her and their team. Our whole family - even our daughter, actually enjoys going to the dentist!

name: Dr. Gwen Jenkins
address: 164 Innisfil Street, Unit 103, Barrie ON L4N 3E7
description:
Dr. Jenkins is a FANTASTIC psychologist. With the upmost confidence, I can say that she is a one-of-a-kind psychologist.

She is articulate, highly educated and competent, curious in how to best support someone, friendly and inviting, and a professional who will ALWAYS be an advocate. Dr. Jenkins uses numerous therapeutic methods such as positive psychology, humanistic psychology, and so much more!

The expertise in diagnostic assessment and therapeutic techniques goes a long way. Lastly, her practical ideas and suggestions for improving challenging / difficult / new situations are highly appreciated. She is high quality care.

Dr. Jenkins, thank you for being you. Thank you from start to finish. Thank you for LISTENING and HEARING.

Because of Dr. Jenkins, I have trust in health care.

10/10 would recommend Dr. Jenkins.

Kind, warm and personable. Felt comfortable and acknowledged.

Awful. Quick to give an opinion during the initial meeting and made my son feel terrible. Doesn't have a true understanding of the Autism spectrum. Her knowledge is based on the old book and she didn't seem to care that the office misgendered him.

The kindest! Very meticulous!

name: Dr. Tim Smith
address: 11 Ferris Lane, Studio 2, Barrie ON L4M 5N6
description:
Dr. Smith and his team are fantastic!  We’ve done two psycho educational assessments with them for our children.  They are accommodating with online appts where possible, they did a payment plan when my MVA caused an interruption in income, there prices are reasonable and they explained the results in detail.

They even did a weekend feedback appt when I had appts filling up my weekdays from my MVA but needed results to plan for back to school.

We actually tried a different provider when we moved away online and were greatly let down in service and exponentially more costs, which lead us to return back to Smith Psychological for help.

We plan to do more assessments with them in the future as well.

We accessed Dr. Smith’s services for an educational assessment for my son. Dr. Smith and his associates were respectful, compassionate and professional from start to finish. They were transparent with costing and worked within our benefits. Dr. Smith is a highly skilled practitioner who created a therapeutic environment where my family felt comfortable and learned valuable information to best support our son.

Dr. Smith was clear and concise. He made our family feel important, and the appropriate amount of time was given for our questions and concerns.
Tiffany was also a pleasure to deal with.
Thank you for everything!

My daughter came here for an assessment, and Doctor Tim along with his team were exceptionally welcoming, kind, and supportive. I extend my gratitude to everyone for their time and effort in assisting my daughter. With the new insights gained, I am confident that the sky is the limit for her future.

I recently came to see Dr. Smith for a learning diagnosis. I had a wonderful experience from start to finish. The team at the clinic were exceptionally friendly, accommodating, very pleasant and comfortable to be around. Dr. Bannerman was very lovely to work with, she was comforting, calm, and I really enjoyed our sessions. It made my whole experience much better. I highly recommend Dr. Smith to anyone. He is very friendly and I found him extremely knowledgeable and made receiving my diagnosis much easier than anticipated. I was quite nervous to attend the initial meetings, but Dr. Smith helped make me feel understood, validated and showed a light at the end of my tunnel for getting the answers I needed. Thank you to everyone at this clinic, and a special thanks to Dr. Smith!

name: Lakeview Psychology (Dr. Andrea Guthrie)
description:
Dr Guthrie, is a great therapist!  I have made more progress in a short period of time than I have with other therapists. She promotes a very open and welcoming environment. She is very knowledgeable and helpful.

Great place! All staff are very friendly , warm heart , helpful.

Dr. Guthrie is a great psychologist! I confidently refer clients to her as she is competent, warm, and very professional. She also is an excellent supervisor to her associates and a competent consultant to her peers. I highly recommend Dr. Guthrie!

Dr. Andrea Guthrie is warm, inviting and easy to talk to.  That helps with those hard initial steps it takes to book to see someone and after that she helps foster amazing growth and learning in a positive, safe space.

I don't have a review on an actual experience with Lakeview Psychology however I tried to get in touch with them to book an appointment, which was already a huge step for myself and they never followed up/through. I received one email back in an exceptional amount of time that honestly had my hopes up because mental health help is something I've waited a long time to decide to get help for. Once I responded to this email and answered the questions that were asked, I never received a response and was very disappointed & discouraged. I understand they are very busy however, I told the person I was emailing that I was willing to wait even months to get in with someone as long as I had a set appointment. It made me once again very hesitant to reach out to any other psychologists because in my head, I felt forgotten about and that I wasn't worth their time. I have since had to guts to reach out to another psychologist who booked me in a matter of a few days. I'm sure Lakeview Psychology has helped loads of people so I'm not discrediting them on their practice but it would be seriously appreciated if they actually got back to someone even if they can't take any clients, at least tell the person that so they aren't waiting and hoping because deciding to get help for your mental health isn't an easy task for some people.

name: Dilico Anishnabet Family Services - Main Office
address: 200 Anemki Place Fort William First Nation
description:
After my kids went into care so I could heal from brain damage and ptsd, they took me to court behind my back and made my children crown wards. I asked several times if they were going to take custody because I had a bad feeling they might and they kept saying no. After lying for months on end saying they were short staffed and thats why I could only have visits once a month instead of once a week… My daughter told me the truth. They had planned it that way… I haven’t seen my kids in about 5 months now. Dilico said I had threatened the workers and thats why I cant see my kids. Dilico won’t tell me what I said or go to the police about these threats because they are lying. I finally got a phone call with my kids a couple weeks ago  which Dilico demanded be supervised by a staff member where I am living. No reasons other than they keep saying Im crazy are being given for their demands of me or what they are doing. I have called the police several times about this and even the staff members where I am staying have spoken to police to investigate dilicos actions but the cops refuse every time. I am very worried for my children. Has anyone else experienced this? :’(

We have been going to Dilico for years and I have to say this is the best care I have gotten in a very long time.  Excellent Nurse Practitioner and staff.  Well organized so grateful to have Dilico.

AWFUL! they are kidnappers. They took me and my sis from my mom. Didn’t bother to let my dad know we were in care for a week. My dad had to find out where we were taken. If I could I’d reward them 0 stars if I could.

From what i can gather in the adult in patient treatment, sounds like nothing more than a sexual free for all.  With little to no supervision from staff!? Teach patients it's okay to blame others than to look at yourself.

Dilico is a piece of trash organization. It rips more native family's apart then the residential schools ever did.

name: Native Child & Family Services of Toronto
description:
My experience with this agency has been terrifying, heartbreaking, and deeply traumatic for both me and my daughter. I have been raising extremely serious concerns about my child’s safety for a long time, including things she disclosed to me that no parent should ever have to hear. When she reached out to me about situations in her foster home that made her uncomfortable and frightened, I immediately reported everything to the authorities because I take her safety seriously.

Instead of being supported, I have felt dismissed, silenced, and pushed aside. I’ve been told things about my daughter’s wishes that do not match what she personally shared with me. She told me she wanted to leave care and live a normal, safe life — but I keep receiving the opposite message from the people who are supposed to protect her. It feels like my relationship with my daughter is being interfered with, and that is one of the most painful things a parent can experience.

I have repeatedly voiced fears about her wellbeing and my own safety, yet my complaints go nowhere. Nothing gets properly investigated, and I’m left feeling helpless and terrified for my child. I have also raised concerns about possible breaches of privacy, stalking, and retaliation, which have only added to the fear and confusion surrounding this situation. Instead of receiving support, I feel targeted, monitored, and unsafe.

Right now I am a mother who is scared for her child’s life and for her own. I feel completely unheard, unprotected, and pushed out of my daughter’s life at a time when she needs me the most. I desperately hope that someone in leadership finally takes accountability, looks into these situations with urgency, and ensures that children and families are truly protected. No parent should have to live in fear like this, and no child’s voice should be ignored or dismissed.

Gorgeous interior! For all parents looking for information re: early on. It is strictly for native children only!

It’s a shame because I would love to learn more about native nursery songs and culture, but of course I respect the decision. I wish that maybe the program can open to outsiders maybe once a month!

This is a stunning facility. So much consideration has gone into it. There is a longhouse, sweat lodge and roof top healing garden. It is Leed certified.

Really bad experience, lady on the front desk was really rude

I called the centre to know about the hours of operation for the early on centre and they informed me about the hours and dental services and mentioned all is welcome. Went there with my toddler but I was not allowed to get in because its only restriced to the native community. Pls mention it over the phone and on the website and add it to your IG page. This was a bad experience and customer service.

name: Walpole Island Health Centre
description:
Very helpful with any and all medical issues. They have nurses on site, addiction workers, foot care people. If they dont have the solution, they will direct you in the right direction.

I received access same day

name: ANISHNAWBE-MUSHKIKI THUNDER BAY ABORIGINAL HEALTH CENTRE
description:
My Doctor is great, but the reception staff are terrible, rude, and kind of mean. I've had to request refills over and over before they actually fax anything to my pharmacy. When my pervious nurse practitioner left they didn't tell me I was assigned a new Doctor. Receptionist screams over you on the phone, and lies saying your phone reception is breaking up. When attempting to remind the receptionist you struggle with mental health issues, she just yells louder. It's either a matter of their phones not working or just not wanting to listen. Reception blames pharmacy but when you follow the paper trail the pharmacy has done everything correctly. The way they behave regarding refills and paper work is unacceptable, what ever happened to telephone etiquette.

i’m happy to say i’ve never had an issue at this office. my doctor, Dr. Saliba (occasionally i see another doctor whose name i can’t recall), is incredibly empathetic, informative & proactive. she checks on my mental health every appointment & always asks if there’s anything else i need while i’m there. i feel genuinely cared for, heard & seen.

the nurses are also so kind and truly concerned about their patients wellbeing. over the last 5 years, i’ve faced some scary, severe & sudden health problems, they have always been gentle, prompt, & attentive.

that being said, i do understand the concerns about the reception staff’s shortness. i haven’t had a nice experience with them yet.

i do however think it’s important for everyone to recognize how their actions & words, even when they feel justified, can affect others, including our healthcare providers. we need to show more kindness, especially when we’re frustrated or not feeling our best. let’s try to be kinder, theyre here to help.

I was in an accident and had a concussion.  It became a WSIB claim.  Nancy refused to fill out my forms to extend my care.  She said : why can't use your husband's health insurance, and said,  what is it , drugs you want?
She discouraged me from going to a concussion specialist,  but sent in the referral.  Yes. I have a concussion still, which did not get treated.  I am paying out of pocket now for a therapist to help Me learn to move my eyes again.  I suffer from severe headaches.  But I am not a Drug addict. Never have been a drug addict. I am simply Metis ... and they know it.

They seem to lose prescription refills almost every single time. You can watch your pharmacy fax over a request two weeks in advance and still not have it filled in time, because they claim they “never received it.” My pharmacy is irritated at them, because time after time it just gets annoying and shows their lack of organization regarding prescriptions. Also very inconvenient if you need the medication on a daily basis, like anti-depressants - then your mental health and even sometimes your life is really in their hands.

I've been a patient at Mushkiki for almost 3 years and really like it. The providers have always been kind and compassionate with me, which is not the norm in healthcare. Sarah Gleeson (NP), in particular, is the best HCP I have ever had. She is empathetic, a great listener, and never makes me feel rushed. She works collaboratively with patients to meet their needs and provides them with options, which feels really empowering. Sometimes things seem to be a bit disorganized from an admin perspective, but they always make things right. Thanks to the team at Mushkiki for all that they do.

name: B'SAANIBAAMAADSIWIN ABORIGINAL MENTAL HEALTH PROGRAM (Muskoka-Parry Sound Community Mental Health Service-Parry Sound Site)
description:

Remember folks stay skibidi fortnite toilet poggers l get tagged lmao I love cheese

name: DE DWA DA DEHS NYE>S ABORIGINAL HEALTH CENTRE
description:
Great health centre. Very friendly and professional staff. Always looking out for their patients. Highly recommended

During covid I was denied access to medical treatment by All hospitals & 12 walk-in clinics in Hamilton And my Essential shopping by almost all grocery stores, even with a medical exemption letter because I can’t wear a mask!!  My NP helped me through the whole ordeal  when I needed stitches on my left forefinger to to see me in person but I was 3 days too late for stitches!!  - with Video conferences he could see what I needed & with advice I healed myself & have been since!! He also arranged video mental health counselling through St Joe’s programs!  For 15 yrs, they All have been very good to me & helping me get through any hurdles in my health & wellness!!

I ( and my family ) have been under the care of the doctors at the Aboriginal Health center for over 14 years. I have brain cancer and other health issues; I honestly wouldn't trust anyone else with my health issues. My doctor has always been friendly and professional.  The staff are some of the most caring people I have ever had the pleasure to meet. I highly recommend them! They go above and beyond.  My many thanks to my doctors and traditional elders there ❤

Hamilton Centre: Awesome Healthcare Staff. The Receptionists are helpful as well. To get the best care, know that the Dr's (except for one) are only temporary, and you will be transferred to different people. However, they have your Info and data. Make a list of your healthcare concern questions for during your visit. They also offer Community Programs, so they take care of both the physical and mental healh aspects of our being. Five stars all the way.

I absolutely adore the health care staff. They genuinely care about my well being. I have been a patient here since I was a baby. 10/10 amazing people over all!

name: GANAAN DE WE O DIS ^YETHI YENAHWAHSE (SOAHAC)
description:
Had a appointment for a depo that is very much needed only to find out no one is there and they been making appointments on their holidays breaks without informing their patients this is the second time this has happened to me been my family has with Soahac for 22+years now never had this problem before

The receptionist here are an absolute joke!! I've had them mess up my appointments numerous times then try sending me home like it was my mistake. I call over and over multiple days but no one ever answers the phone. I left 4 messages in 3 days but still have not heard anything back!
The doctors/nurses are amazing here but need new staffing!!

My son and I moved recently to this area and got connected with Soahac. It made all the difference in my son's life. Roberta, a very friendly, involved and caring child and youth worker started visiting with my son and arranged outings where he could meet new friends. Counselling services are amazing. His counsellor is professional, knowledgeable, very thoughtful and available at times of need. I am thoroughly impressed and very thankful for all the services and help Soahac provides.

Waitlist is unacceptable and needs to be addressed. Submitted an application for Primary Care back in August 2021. Was told the waitlist was a year, but they were possibly getting a new Doctor in Oct 2022. Just called back now in October 2023, over a year later - and the staff cannot give me any information regarding where I am in the list, what they’re doing to address it, or how much longer the wait could be. All they can tell you is the waitlist is now two years. Two years from now? Two years from when I applied? Two years from the next time I call? I’ve spent over a year putting off seeing a doctor because I wanted to be with my people and be with a primary care physician who actually cared. Sad reality check. I’ve updated my address but I’ve all but given up my hopes for this location. I will now have to seek help else where. I recommend you don’t get your hopes up either.

Called and asked if I could get a cab ride as my ride split last second.
Told me they will call right back. Waited 40 minutes for a call and nothing.  Called them and right away they said no.
Didnt bother calling.me back so.i could try to find a way to my much needed appointment.
Lola/lois what ever her name is dont know how to.do her job and is very rude.

name: N'MNINOEYAA: MENTAL HEALTH PROGRAM
WAASEGIIZHIG NANAANDAWE’IYEWIGAMIG (Satellite Clinic)
description:
I extend my deepest gratitude to Waasegiizhig Nanaandawe'iyewigamig for the invaluable support and guidance provided during my journey towards emotional well-being. Specifically, I wish to express my sincere appreciation to Rochelle Shibish for her exceptional counseling services.

Rochelle's expertise, compassion, and unwavering support were instrumental in helping me navigate through a challenging period marked by depression. Her personalized approach and insightful interventions empowered me with the tools and resilience needed to overcome obstacles and embrace a brighter future.

The compassionate care and professionalism exhibited by Rochelle has left a profound impact. I am deeply thankful for her dedication to fostering healing and growth within the community.

Since then, I will be wholeheartedly recommending Waasegiizhig Nanaandawe'iyewigamig to anyone seeking support on their journey towards emotional well-being. Your commitment to excellence and genuine concern for the welfare of your clients are truly commendable, and I am immensely grateful for the positive difference it has made in my life.
Gitchi-Miigwech

name: OOSHKE BEMAHDESEWIN (ALPHA COURT COMMUNITY MENTAL HEALTH ADDICTIONS)
description:
My worker may have very well saved my life! What an amazing organization with amazing support workers! I had a worker years ago(20+)who was also very kind! Thank you for your services!!! Thank you Aliyah!!!!!

The day center is the best. Offers great social/recreation opportunities. The staff are amazing and it’s always a friendly and safe place to go and hang out. They have free coffee and lunches for members. There are opportunities to do boxing, bowling, swimming, walking, tai chi, yoga, painting, puzzles, cooking/baking, short psychosocial programs, etc. There is something for everyone. Bingo is always well attended, and the best part is that we get to choose our prize (anything from dollarama). There’s no wait list to go to the day center. I did have to wait for other services from them but it was worth the wait. I don’t know what I would have done without them. I know I’d be in a much worse off place with my mental health. I’m not sure why ppl are commenting negatively, but it’s important to remember that this isn’t a therapy service, also, these workers have multiple clients and cannot drop everything for someone. I’m positive that if someone was having an issue with their worker, their managers would be more than helpful in solving any issues. All in all, I’m so thankful I have these programs and services in my life, they are a huge part of what is keeping me going.

My boyfriend did a better job then the social workers here

I t is wonderful. Everyone who works there is the best. I miss it.

name: APO-WAY-A-IN WIGAMIN – MISSISSAUGA WOMEN’S SHELTER
description:
I reached out to the organization for assistance in September and been on the wait list since then. I have spoken to Aisha on multiple occasions asking the same questions and each time she would confirm with answers I didn’t even answer. She gaslight me in thinking I was safe and able to move forward with the organization but to get a call back months later to be told they are unable to assist because I didn’t feel comfortable answering personal questions.

I truly hope they don’t push away other sister from the help they require and prolong the process. I asked Aisha if I was able to speak to a supervisor to get better understanding of their questioning process and I was once again gaslight as she did tell me someone will reach out the next day. A full week has been passed and I finally received a call from a supervisor just to find out she is putting incorrect information each time we spoke. I do not suggest coming here if you require assistance straight away. In sha Allah they are able to change there ways and there policy to reflect more on the deen of Islam.

This place discriminates based on disability. I mentioned my disability during my intake. The caseworker ended the intake immediately and denied me a spot at Nisa Homes.

I tried to follow up to understand the reasons for ending the intake, but was lied to and gaslit by different staff members.

Unethical behaviour like this from a place where people go to during the hardest times of their lifes is unbelievable.

I'm seriously considering legal action, but I currently don't have the financial or psychological capacity for this. However, this is totally illegal and unacceptable in every way.

Ayesha in the Mississauga location is unprofessional and this whole insititutuon is just useless space. They degrade women into thinking there’s a safe space for them jus so they have something to gossip about.  They should take down their website for false advertisement. Occupying space is illegal without proper use in Canada. DISGUSTING service.

I’ve been a donor because I genuinely empathize with my fellow sisters facing hardship. I believed in Nisa Foundation's mission wholeheartedly and wanted to support it in any way I could.

But when I found myself in need, I was deeply disappointed. I reached out through several emails, calling wasn’t an option for me that time, byand received only one reply. Someone (whose name I’ll respectfully leave out) informed me that my case had been assigned to them. I followed up multiple times afterward, but never heard back.

My situation involved emotional abuse. I wasn’t seeking financial assistance, just moral support, guidance, and information on legal options to navigate what I was going through. I’m left wondering whether my case was deemed unworthy of attention, or if there was another reason for the silence. Either way, I feel profoundly let down.

I turned to an organization I trusted, expecting compassion and support. Instead, I was met with silence.
I’m extremely disappointed.

My Experience with Nisa Homes
Unfortunately, my experience with Nisa Homes was not what I expected. While the mission and purpose of the organization sound beautiful, the way I was treated made me feel unsupported and even judged during one of the most vulnerable times in my life.
There was a lack of genuine care, understanding, and professionalism from some staff members. I came for help, hoping to feel safe and uplifted, but instead I felt like just another case, not a human being with real pain and needs.
I pray no other woman or mother has to feel this way when seeking refuge. I hope Nisa Homes reflects deeply on how they treat the people they claim to serve.
This experience was deeply disappointing, but I know God sees everything. I’m a convert to Islam and you’re not showing me the good picture of Islam at all shame on you

– Dayane Williams

name: MIGISI ALCOHOL AND DRUG TREATMENT CENTRE
description:
Amazing place

Great place

I'm cured!!!

Nice

name: The Salvation Army London Centre of Hope
description:
I have a few clients whom I mentor who use the addictions services at Salvation Army and it sounds like such wonderful programs. This is exactly the type of programming and support people who are ready to get clean need to propel their life forward. There is the Heart Hub program that supports up to a year and another longer program up to 4 years which is amazing in ensuring the client has a safe, reliable and ongoing treatment team to provide the best long term outcome! More centers and organizations need to take note!

The resources that fill our community cracks are amazing, from the Christmas salvation in Hope guide giving you stories recipes and more just to align your headspace with what others could be feeling and how they can bring you joy just by sharing those little things. The staff has to be another great aspect that I need to mention being very stressed out in hard situations with decision makers that I didn’t think we’re rooting the best for me had me finding solace in the group of ladies who were able to present a great transaction but then also the follow up and the explanation and not as if I couldn’t understand but if I was capable and that I will succeed, I think that’s called Effervescent-Hope! Thank you again.

Lana White - Thank You Staff

Well was there long time ago hunting for food as a cable guy lool i joke. They guys understands. Love

Been trying to get help from the salvation army for a month. No one answers the phone in the housing and stability. No one returns your messages. Operator instructed me to show up in person and someone will help me. I was turned away and giving no help. Treated like I'm not even human. They don't care to help you.

name: Center for Addiction and Mental Health (CAMH) - Sudbury Site
Minto Counselling Centre - Cochrane Head Office
St. Joseph's Health Care London - Concurrent Disorders Services (Parkwood Institute)
description:
We are deeply grateful to the entire team at Parkwood Geriatric Mental Health for the exceptional care and compassion they showed our father over the years. From the moment he was admitted with Frontotemporal Dementia (FTD), every doctor, nurse, and staff member treated him with kindness, dignity, and patience, always prioritizing his safety and well-being. Special thanks to Dr. Achatar, Dr. Maria, Talya, Lucy, and all the nurses involved in his care. Your empathy and professionalism made an enormous difference in our father's life, and our family will always be thankful. Even from heaven, our father would thank you for the love and care you gave him.

I can't complain about anything, after all the friendly and wonderful services they have provided for me. As a bonus, the surroundings in the area are beautiful. They have a nice, outside walking area that goes through some of this scenery on their property; if you are interested in more, there is a massive forest area behind with trails to walk and hike on. I only took a couple of pictures of the main hospital entrance (taken late October).

I was admitted to park wood on September 4th  and endured a deeply troubling experience. Upon arrival, I was confined to a small room with no means of communication or escape. The only items provided were a makeshift toilet, a single blanket, and a bed that was uncomfortably hard.

For six hours, I was left alone without any updates or explanations. There was no staff interaction, and I felt completely isolated. This lack of basic care and communication is unacceptable in any healthcare setting.

When I was finally seen, the medical staff seemed unaware of the conditions I had been subjected to. This lack of coordination and oversight raises serious concerns about the hospital’s operations.

I urge [Parkwood] to review its patient care protocols to ensure that no one else has to endure such an experience. Patients deserve respect, dignity, and timely care.

Parking Lot 1’s machine almost never works. I have a monthly pass and 4 out of 5 times it tells me the lot is full, even when it’s completely empty, so I’m constantly having to press the help button just to get in.

On top of that, for people without passes, the machine eats credit cards and now doesn’t even accept coins, leaving them with no way to pay or park there. The whole setup is extremely frustrating.

Recently I had a wonderful experience with nurses, OTs, PTs, and other staff on the 5th floor!

I am very grateful for their care and professionalism. In particular, Dr. Martyniak is the most excellent doctor who is kind, flexible, and good at communication. Nurse Teresa, Suzy, and Jubin are excellent as well.

Highly recommend this facility.

name: Kent County Addictions Program
description:
Offered nothing.

These people told me to “stop taking drugs” what are they my therapist?

name: Lambton County Addictions Services (Bluewater Health)
Two-Spirited People of the First Nations
description:
What a great organisation. So many great programs. Some staff are better than others. Americo is fantastic.

A drag show at a powwow? Loved it!!! This year's 2S pow wow was a blast. I didn't get a chance to see the art installation on the hill up close but I did run into ALL the neechees and instagram famous natives. Didn't have a closing give away but did have a vendor giving out gift bags. Had a great time.

Great resource & super helpful, caring people work here. So glad to have connected w them thru TCCS.

I got to experience heading out with their outreach team and enjoyed engaging with the public and all the teachings from staff and coworkers. They organization works hard to provide individuals with harm reduction, clothing, food, and making sure that the streets are safe for eveone

2spirits is an awesome organization. The staff are wonderfully supportive.   During the pandemic they have gone above and beyond the call of duty.   They have been there for me in every way.   There are not enough words to thank them.  Miigwetch Keith, Shiva, Americo, Jeremy,  Sara, Brooke and Pam, Miigwetch. 💛❤🖤🤍

name: Canadian Mental Health Association – Middlesex / Crouch – Women’s Mental Health Resources
description:
If you're in a crisis and desperately need don't come here. Ive had many many very odd and weird experiences here. The employees and volunteers seem to have their own issues. It's almost as if they need their own crisis worker. They are uneducated, inexperienced and most importantly lack compassion. I get it they are probably over worked and underpaid and some don't even get paid. But If you are in crisis situation and you come here or you call them and the person who attempts to help you actually makes you worse, then what's the point. The in person sessions and the ones on the phone are very short, too short in my opinion. You say that they really helped you but there is good chance you haven traveled outside of London or southwestern Ontario. As you share your anguish and major issues in your life many of the workers will just sit there and laugh at you. This has happened to me so many times. The psychiatrist that works here is even worse considering his level of education and years of experience. There just isn't a passion to help people here. Everyone just drag their feet. Can't wait to go home. Can't wait to get paid. Everyone is just treated like a number. The resources they give us can easily be be found online with no need for a crisis worker. They're not professional or organized.

Shame on this place — and honestly, on London, Ontario as a whole. It feels like everywhere that’s supposed to help with mental health just hires anyone, even people who clearly don’t care. If you only care about the paycheck and not the people, you shouldn’t be working with those who are struggling. All you’re doing is pushing people away from getting the help they need,  what a shame.

I am so incredibly grateful for the nursing/support staff here. I use services regularly without issue and I think this is a wonderful place to seek help for many issues, however the one star review is due to their psychiatry service.  It is very disheartening to feel that proper treatment, credence, or respect is  given only to the experiences of those falling under the cookie cutter straight, cis, white male category of patients.  It is unfortunate to see such an archaic approach to psychiatry, especially from a place that strives to eradicate such barriers in our community.  My main suggestion? Listen to your patients. Even if you end up disagreeing, people deserve to have a voice in their care and to have their experiences heard and listened to. Not hearing what your patients have to say is a disservice to everyone involved.

I was treated with respect. They helped me navigate the problems i was dealing with and gave me great advice on how to deal with it in the future.

"A" for effort. If I was on my way to jump off a ledge and went for an intervention through CMHA, I would have gone straight back to the ledge after my appointment.

Some points to consider:

1) Give the patient an opportunity to respond without interruptions.

My counselor talked over me repeatedly. I started to think he didn't actually want me to answer his questions.

2) Don't suggest that the patient doesn't seem like they belong at a crisis centre.

3) Take time to investigate what the patient actually wanted to talk to you about. Chances are they are there because they want to work through it even if it has to be with a random stranger.

4) Consider that this was not their first choice. Honour their courage beyond a simple verbal acknowledgement. Let your practice prove your words.

5) The service is advertised as unlimited with regard to timeframe, but yet I felt pushed out of the room. The counselor seemed uncomfortable and inexperienced. I could tell because he kept leaning on relating my unique experience to his own life. Professional malpractice 101.

Just like many professions, the mental health sector appears to be striving to ensure they have enough bodies available without the proper training.

The crisis centre is in crisis per this experience if you ask me. Fortunately, I am not in as life threatening of a position as I imagine others are.

I am grateful for this service, but I'd be hesistant to try them again in the future to be completely honest.

name: Can-Voice
description:
Come gather in a comfortable, welcoming place with a homey atmosphere, and kind people, for those with lived experience of mental illness. Various activities weekday afternoons and a decades-long potluck lunch Thursdays. Or bring nothing and eat anyway. Safe, friendly, relaxed, a great place to make like-minded friends.
A social hub, critical for when you need someone to talk to, get your thoughts off things, get out of the house, and realize you're really not alone after all! A lifesaver for many over the decades.
If you dislike the medical model of mental illness treatment, you'll find an encouraging ear happy to listen. If you still take meds you'll also find no issues with that here, either. Super laid back, casual and unstructured.
All are welcomed with a sincere smile! 🙂

Activity centre for people with mental health challenges. Lots of activities visit to the sugar bush, mini golf, bowling, trip to the movies, Malibu lunch. Indoor activities quilting group, crafts, puzzles, cards, Wii, watch movies.  Meet new friends. Have fun.

There is a lot of Support and staff.

name: Sexual Assault Centre of London
description:
Highly disappointed in the experience I witnessed here. If you have ever known anyone with PTSD, you should know that post traumatic stress and addiction are buddies, and the fact that your counselors say they can't help with that is very distressing. I am not someone who needed counseling, but rather a close friend. I went with her to her sessions and waited an hour for her every Monday, and the counselor in question never said more than three words to me. I know she wasn't there for me, but how unfriendly is that?

On another note, please please please stop advertising 'sexual assault' all over your walls and decorations. The young women that are waiting for their sessions don't need to be constantly reminded that they were assaulted, they need to feel comfortable and at home.

If you have serious problems, please seek out a real therapist or psychiatrist.

There needs to be an online way of contacting you guys, for people with anxiety surrounding phones and phone calls.

More service like this are needed.

name: Nokee Kwe Career Directions
description:
I am pleased to share my own experience with Nokee Kwe. I started this journey back in October 2020 with Blanca in Nokee Kwe. Since the first e-mail sent to Nokee Kwe, I received a professional quick respond with information and steps to start my journey. Blanca from Nokee Kwe became my support and guide to build a solid file. We had weekly meetings focused on the same direction, working as a team to reach my goals. Second Career process was a challenger journey for me but with professional and effective assistance, then it was possible. Thanks Blanca and Nokee Kwe.

I went in to Nokee Kwe (South London Employment Help Centre) to get information on The Second Career program.

I met with Blanca Alagon who was extremely helpful and always on time.
She helped me from start to finish and went out of her way to make sure my application was perfect before sending it in for review.

I was accepted into the SC program and i owe alot of it to the organizational and professional skills and help that Blanca brought forward.

Thank you Blanca @ Nokee Kwe - The South London Employment Help Centre

Sincerely
Joey G

I had a wonderful experience with Nokee kwe. They guided me with all steps to help me succeed. Blanca was amazing, she always answered my texts and emails in a timely fashion. Highly recommend them!

After being put into the Nanda coding course it seems no one really cares whether you ink or swim. The Nanda course was developed for people who already have some kind of coding experience. It is difficult, hard to understand for newbie beginners.  I think the government just wanted to show they were trying to help and thru the money out without actually getting involved and seeing what was being done. I feel that this too is just going to be another funded "go nowhere" job bank kinda deal. Sure they can show numbers going in BUT how many will successfully come out with that certificate? My suggestion is that if you dont even know BASIC dont get into the course. One star for a very poorly thought out idea.

A thank you very much to Blanca A. for always returning my calls immediately and having the patience to answer all my questions.  I highly recommend her as she was professional and very helpful in finding me not just job opportunities but other helpful skills tailored to my work preferences    Thank you again Blanca, stay awesome!

name: Wiingashk Alternative Secondary School
description:
A very wonderful and good environment,  suitable and comfortable for learning. The teachers I have come across are so wonderful. I finished my healthcare science, I am equally done with Personal Support Worker (PSW) course here. I love the amazing teachers. God bless them all.

Such a great facility. It is my second term here and I can say that I am very pleased with my experience so far. A great place if you’re wanting to retrieve any high school credits you couldn’t complete for whatever reason. Most of the staff is friendly, and the cafeteria has a wide variety of food and drinks you can buy. Overall, a great school. I am looking forward to my next few terms here!

Hello, the school is not bad but security guys is very bad man I do like to change the security because nobody like him. He always have a bad face with everyone.

I loved my time at Wheable. Specifically the teachers were amazing. Lots of courses to choose from. I was able to get more than what I needed to go to university. If you were too cool for grades in highschool and you want a transcript you can use; Wheable is the cost-effective and time-flexible solution.

All the staff here is so amazing. If it weren't for their good hearts and care for their students myself and others would struggle alot to graduate. The guidance they give and how much they want you to succeed is so amazing. Anyone to attend wheable is lucky to be surrounded by the amazing teachers. Thanks so much wheable for helping me and so many others.

name: Canadian Mental Health Association – Ottawa Branch
description:
I've been a client of CMHA since 2015 in both Ottawa and the Prescott Russell township. I absolutely love this organization I've received so much help and learned so many skills and tools to help manage my anxiety and depression... Impulsive thoughts and racing memories from my PTSD ect.

It's actually kinda comical CMHA has helped me get through the criminal court system 3 times now because of overzealous police officers that laid silly charges on me when the incidents were purely a civil matter, but I digress.

These people do amazing work and they definitely need more staff and more funding as time goes on and mental health takes more of a priority and given more resources.. I can't say enough about my experiences with CMHA. please if your struggling with addiction or mental health issues or both (concurrent disorders) please reach out to CMHA get your intake started now because there's a wait list to get a worker. You'll never regret the decision to reach out and get the help or resources needed to make life manageable again!

Much love to the citizens of Ottawa!

CMHA has had a huge impact on my life. They contacted me when I was at my lowest point and helped get me to where I am today. The staff is amazing and they really put their clients at the forefront of their recovery. I wouldn’t be where I am today without this organization and am very grateful!

One 5-star review was obviously not from someone who has been a client; they just list the supposed things CMHA Ottawa offers. The worker I had did very, very little for me. Two emails to their supervisor garnered no response.
Asked the worker to attend a doctor's appointment with me where the doctor was rude, insulting and obnoxious. The worker said nothing.
CMHA Ottawa continues to go downhill. They offer very few groups while Middlesex CMHA had 50 separate groups.
They don't participate in the national fundraiser or have a major fundraiser of their own. They aren't doing anything to improve the situation for those with mental health issues in Ottawa. The director of CMHA Ottawa has been there too long. It's time to retire and let someone who cares take over (Hopefully from outside CMHA Ottawa or nothing is likely to change).
The staff in charge of communications at CMHA Ottawa don't keep the website up-to-date, even when it's mentioned the website has misinformation.
There are a few great people working there but I had little interaction with them. Failure to accommodate was another issue I had with them.
Sadly, CMHA National doesn't seem to care about all of this.

They don't know a thing about support. Government need to put there money else were.

They were good at one point . Their quality has decreased drastically over the years. Professional skills.... not sure how professional it is to intentionally not respond to a person.
They teach not to judge. The say reach out when in need. What they teach and what they do  don't match.
Really best recommendation is if you go with them.... pray you get a person who's not in it for the money and is in it to make a true difference . They do have a few .

This organization has helped me grow and flourish, and create a life worth living. It has been a crucial part of my wellness recovery and I only hope others can experience this too with this amazing, caring and invaluable organization.

name: NORTHEAST MENTAL HEALTH CENTRE / NORTH BAY REGIONAL HEALTH CENTRE
description: The address for the Claude Ranger Mental Health Clinic is years out of date

name: ALGOMA TREATMENT AND REMAND CENTRE
THUNDER BAY SEXUAL ASSAULT & SEXUAL ABUSE COUNSELLING & CRISIS CENTRE
description: The centre has many kind, compassionate folks working here.

name: CHILDREN'S CENTRE THUNDER BAY
description:
I started seeing Angela after my mother passed when I was 14 years old, I'm now 20 with two kids and I still phone her when I need advice or help, I was feeling incredibly let down in regards to my oldest son until I called her for advice, now he's on a waiting list and I finally feel heard about his situation. I also went to the children's centre as a young child too, im so incredibly thankful for the staff here, especially Angela, thank you guys for being such wonderful people ❤️

Very caring and helpful staff! Thanks for supporting my kiddo when she needed support and encouragement. Her self esteem and coping skills are very much improved thanks to Sandra and her terrific councelling and advice. You're help is very much appreciated and we wish you all the best. And to the front desk staff... very much enjoyed the many conversations with you all while I waited for my daughter. Hope I didn't put you behind in your work too much. Y'all are a great bunch. Thank You!

Good child screening programs and services to assist kids who may have issues with social development. A bit pushy with programs and services and not very user friendly hours for full time working parents .

Incredible covid protocol, kind and knowledgeable staff. My youngest son has benefitted greatly from the services here.

Susan has helped our son and they are all friendly and go above and beyond service. Even gave our son Christmas gift

name: DISTRESS CENTRE NORTH HALTON
description:
Answered ph right away. Good listener and she and I made a plan.  A bit hard to understand at times as she had a soft voice and Asian accent. She supported me and said my feelings were normal & justified.   I felt heard & reassured.

Never been able to talk to someone when I call. No one ever picks up.

name: DISTRESS CENTRES ONTARIO
description:
I had 2 visits here in this month. One for changing driving license address and another one for OHIP.

On both occasions I was helped by 2 different individuals who were very kind and helpful. The whole process went very smooth and my work was done with in10 minutes.

I just found out yesterday that my health card was going to expire next week, as you can imagine for anyone, this news can cause a lot of anxiety. I made an appointment at this location and I was not looking forward to it. Most ServiceOntario locations offer the worst customer service and the wait times are astronomical. I walked in, signed in, and sat down for two minutes before my number was called!! I couldn’t believe it ! Dana helped me out and provided friendly service and made the whole process super quick and easy. I was told to always make an appointment which I will always do from now on. I was so relieved to be in and out in ten minutes wow !

I had such a smooth and reassuring experience at the Duke Street Service Ontario office. After being turned away at another location despite having all the correct documents, I arrived here feeling anxious and unsure. But the staff at Duke Street were incredible—professional, kind, and efficient. They reviewed my documents without any fuss and issued my health card paper right away.

It was such a relief to be treated with respect and clarity. The team here clearly knows what they’re doing and handles things with care. Thank you for restoring my confidence in the process. You made a stressful situation feel easy.

Everything went well. Kinda ignorant and unprofessional make someone wait till January get there card renew when card about to expired. Kinda waste of time to come im glad I wasn't working be patient. Back when I used work this 2 cards been so stressful and think last time had pay out of pocket for no reason that my girlfriend said to me that I shouldn't have too if my record is on file. Just annoyed but I'll get over it least a person with mental health has better self control and awareness then to react for nothing.

Went in to this location for a services around 4.40 pm after taking time off from my office.
Thare were two ladies serving the customs -one in counter one and in the left corner kind of little office there was the other lady having a long chat with another customer.
Two people were in front of me and one went out in around five minutes and just one lady was getting the service in the counter one....
The lady in the left corner has completed the lengthy chat around 5 PM and started closing the office - FURTHER, SHE ASKED ME TO LEAVE THE OFFICE AS SHE NEED TO CLOSE.

This is my first bad experience ... please correct, if I'm wrong - I do not see any office - asking customs to leave - if they come in for a service well ahead ....

name: DURHAM MENTAL HEALTH SERVICES - CRISIS SERVICES
description:
Just spoke to an intake coordinator. Very prejudiced against immigrants; does not consider Mental Health to be a reason for case management support. I have read the experiences of others and I don't know if it's because of my ethnic background, but those with more "Canadian" names seem to have no problem getting support. She decided my result before even speaking with me, was unable to listen to my story, suggested my experience isn't relevant to support, explained she was a trained mental health worker but seemed not to care at all about mental health suffering.

I did intake in August 2022. Had to wait for a worker to come back. We made an appt she never came to for Sept 9. I was left alone struggling. I emailed and asked the intake worker for another counsellor. Never got one. Now July 2023 I've struggled the last year with abuse from ex, VAW shelter, homelessness, family Dr hasn't seen me in years, severe anxiety, depression which I've been diagnosed with over 40 years ago but no counsellor, meds cut in 2017 by a new family Dr. Years of treatment down the toilet & this agency won't help as promised. Get a referral to a psychiatrist despite the long wait.

Was trying to set up getting my monthly B12 shot via the Nurse with Senior supports in our building. She finally called me back and was more concerned with how to get out of assisting me than with actually assisting me! All I heard was her complaining about having to cover all of Durham region and if my doctor prescribed the shot why am I not getting it done through him? She told me her name but I couldn't even understand her. I have tremors due to my meds and can't do it myself or I would. So much for helping seniors. Not impressed with her at all! 😢

I had a casworker for a long time with this agency. I moved and was not informed that I could no longer access their services. There was no discharge email/letter etc. I was dropped without warning and only knew because I ASKED if I was still a client after not hearing from my caseworker for almost a month. THAT is how I found out. How is this okay? This made me change my mind about looking for another case worker.

I had a terrible experience at Durham Mental Health they don't provide any services or refer to programs or take your need seriously would never recommend them.
